instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Update safety stock for all finished goods SKUs based on 2x average daily demand over 30 days
output:
UPDATE inventory
SET safety_stock = ROUND(2 * daily_avg.avg_daily_demand, 0),
    updated_at = GETDATE()
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku = p.sku
JOIN (
    SELECT soi.sku,
           SUM(soi.ordered_quantity) / 30.0 AS avg_daily_demand
    FROM sales_order_items soi
    JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id = so.so_id
    WHERE so.order_date >= DATEADD(DAY, -30, GETDATE())
    GROUP BY soi.sku
) daily_avg ON i.sku = daily_avg.sku
WHERE p.product_type = 'FINISHED_GOODS';

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Insert a new demand forecast record for SKU RM-2200 for April 2026

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Soft-delete all inactive suppliers who have had no purchase orders in 3 years
output:
UPDATE suppliers
SET status = 'INACTIVE',
    updated_at = GETDATE()
WHERE status = 'ACTIVE'
  AND supplier_id NOT IN (
      SELECT DISTINCT supplier_id
      FROM purchase_orders
      WHERE order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -3, GETDATE())
        AND status NOT IN ('CANCELLED')
  );

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Register a new purchase requisition with HIGH priority for 200 units of RM-4401
output:
INSERT INTO purchase_requisitions (
    requisition_number, created_by, created_date,
    total_estimated_value, status, priority, justification
) VALUES (
    'PR-2026-' + FORMAT(NEXT VALUE FOR pr_sequence, '0000'),
    (SELECT employee_id FROM employees WHERE employee_name = 'Priya Nair' AND department = 'PROCUREMENT'),
    GETDATE(),
    (SELECT 200 * unit_cost FROM products WHERE sku = 'RM-4401'),
    'PENDING_APPROVAL', 'HIGH',
    'Production line requirement for Q2 manufacturing schedule'
);

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show me which product categories have the highest return-on-inventory metric
output:
SELECT p.category,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand * p.unit_cost) AS inventory_investment,
       SUM(soi.ordered_quantity * soi.unit_price) AS revenue_last_year,
       ROUND(SUM(soi.ordered_quantity * soi.unit_price) / NULLIF(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand * p.unit_cost), 0), 2) AS roi_ratio
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON p.sku = i.sku
JOIN sales_order_items soi ON p.sku = soi.sku
JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id = so.so_id
WHERE so.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
GROUP BY p.category
ORDER BY roi_ratio DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the number of shipments per logistics partner per month for the past year
output:
SELECT lp.partner_name,
       FORMAT(sh.actual_delivery_date, 'yyyy-MM') AS month,
       COUNT(sh.shipment_id) AS shipments,
       SUM(sh.freight_cost) AS total_freight_cost
FROM shipments sh
JOIN logistics_partners lp ON sh.logistics_partner_id = lp.partner_id
WHERE sh.actual_delivery_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
  AND sh.status = 'DELIVERED'
GROUP BY lp.partner_name, FORMAT(sh.actual_delivery_date, 'yyyy-MM')
ORDER BY lp.partner_name, month;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Which warehouses have the most inventory adjustments indicating shrinkage?
output:
SELECT w.warehouse_name,
       COUNT(*) AS adjustment_count,
       SUM(CASE WHEN it.quantity < 0 THEN ABS(it.quantity) ELSE 0 END) AS negative_adj_units,
       SUM(CASE WHEN it.quantity < 0 THEN ABS(it.quantity) * p.unit_cost ELSE 0 END) AS shrinkage_value
FROM inventory_transactions it
JOIN warehouses w ON it.warehouse_id = w.warehouse_id
JOIN products p ON it.sku = p.sku
WHERE it.transaction_type = 'ADJUSTMENT'
  AND it.transaction_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
GROUP BY w.warehouse_name
ORDER BY shrinkage_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: What is the order confirmation rate — how many received orders were confirmed within 24 hours?
output:
SELECT
    COUNT(*) AS total_orders,
    SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(HOUR, so.order_date, so.confirmed_date) <= 24 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS confirmed_within_24h,
    ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(HOUR, so.order_date, so.confirmed_date) <= 24 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 2) AS confirmation_rate_pct
FROM sales_orders so
WHERE so.confirmed_date IS NOT NULL
  AND so.order_date >= DATEADD(MONTH, -3, GETDATE());

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show potential savings from consolidating multiple small POs to the same supplier into a single bulk order
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       FORMAT(po.order_date, 'yyyy-MM') AS month,
       COUNT(po.po_id) AS po_count,
       SUM(po.total_amount) AS total_spend,
       AVG(po.total_amount) AS avg_po_value,
       COUNT(po.po_id) - 1 AS consolidation_opportunity
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id = s.supplier_id
WHERE po.status NOT IN ('CANCELLED')
  AND po.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, FORMAT(po.order_date, 'yyyy-MM')
HAVING COUNT(po.po_id) > 3 AND AVG(po.total_amount) < 50000
ORDER BY po_count DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Identify orders where partial shipments have been made but the remainder is overdue
output:
SELECT so.so_number, c.customer_name,
       so.promised_delivery_date,
       SUM(soi.ordered_quantity) AS total_ordered,
       SUM(soi.fulfilled_quantity) AS total_fulfilled,
       SUM(soi.ordered_quantity - COALESCE(soi.fulfilled_quantity, 0)) AS remaining_qty
FROM sales_orders so
JOIN customers c ON so.customer_id = c.customer_id
JOIN sales_order_items soi ON so.so_id = soi.so_id
WHERE so.status = 'PARTIALLY_SHIPPED'
  AND so.promised_delivery_date < GETDATE()
GROUP BY so.so_number, c.customer_name, so.promised_delivery_date
ORDER BY so.promised_delivery_date ASC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show suppliers blacklisted in the past year with their last transaction date
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.category, s.status,
       MAX(po.order_date) AS last_po_date,
       s.updated_at AS blacklisted_on
FROM suppliers s
LEFT JOIN purchase_orders po ON s.supplier_id = po.supplier_id
WHERE s.status = 'BLACKLISTED'
  AND s.updated_at >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, s.category, s.status, s.updated_at
ORDER BY s.updated_at DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: What is the transit damage rate per mode of transport?
output:
SELECT lp.service_type AS transport_mode,
       COUNT(*) AS total_shipments,
       SUM(CASE WHEN sh.damage_reported = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS damaged,
       ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN sh.damage_reported = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 2) AS damage_rate_pct
FROM shipments sh
JOIN logistics_partners lp ON sh.logistics_partner_id = lp.partner_id
WHERE sh.status = 'DELIVERED'
GROUP BY lp.service_type
ORDER BY damage_rate_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the cumulative spend per supplier against their contract value
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, c.contract_number,
       c.start_date, c.end_date, c.contract_value,
       SUM(SUM(poi.line_total)) OVER (PARTITION BY c.contract_id ORDER BY po.order_date ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING) AS cumulative_spend
FROM contracts c
JOIN suppliers s ON c.supplier_id = s.supplier_id
JOIN purchase_orders po ON po.supplier_id = c.supplier_id
  AND po.order_date BETWEEN c.start_date AND c.end_date
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id = poi.po_id
WHERE c.status = 'ACTIVE'
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, c.contract_id, c.contract_number,
         c.start_date, c.end_date, c.contract_value, po.order_date
ORDER BY c.contract_id, po.order_date;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the supply resilience index — for each SKU, how many weeks of cover does current stock provide?
output:
WITH weekly_demand AS (
    SELECT soi.sku,
           SUM(soi.ordered_quantity) / 52.0 AS avg_weekly_demand
    FROM sales_order_items soi
    JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id = so.so_id
    WHERE so.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
    GROUP BY soi.sku
)
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS total_stock,
       wd.avg_weekly_demand,
       ROUND(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) / NULLIF(wd.avg_weekly_demand, 0), 1) AS weeks_of_cover,
       COUNT(DISTINCT sp.supplier_id) AS approved_supplier_count
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON p.sku = i.sku
JOIN weekly_demand wd ON p.sku = wd.sku
LEFT JOIN sourcing_products sp ON p.sku = sp.sku AND sp.status = 'APPROVED'
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, wd.avg_weekly_demand
ORDER BY weeks_of_cover ASC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: How many goods receipts were processed per warehouse this month?
output:
SELECT w.warehouse_name, COUNT(gr.grn_id) AS grn_count,
       SUM(gri.received_quantity) AS total_units_received
FROM goods_receipts gr
JOIN warehouses w ON gr.warehouse_id = w.warehouse_id
JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON gr.grn_id = gri.grn_id
WHERE gr.receipt_date >= DATEADD(DAY, 1 - DAY(GETDATE()), CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE))
GROUP BY w.warehouse_name
ORDER BY grn_count DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show all suppliers whose status changed to BLACKLISTED and the reason
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.category, s.status, s.updated_at,
       sl.change_reason, sl.changed_by
FROM suppliers s
JOIN supplier_status_log sl ON s.supplier_id = sl.supplier_id
WHERE sl.new_status = 'BLACKLISTED'
ORDER BY sl.changed_at DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: What is the average order value per customer segment?
output:
SELECT c.segment,
       COUNT(so.so_id) AS total_orders,
       AVG(so.total_value) AS avg_order_value,
       SUM(so.total_value) AS total_revenue
FROM customers c
JOIN sales_orders so ON c.customer_id = so.customer_id
WHERE so.status NOT IN ('CANCELLED')
  AND so.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
GROUP BY c.segment
ORDER BY avg_order_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: List all raw material SKUs that have no pending purchase orders but are below reorder point
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, i.quantity_on_hand, i.reorder_point,
       i.reorder_point - i.quantity_on_hand AS shortfall
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON p.sku = i.sku
WHERE p.product_type = 'RAW_MATERIAL'
  AND i.quantity_on_hand < i.reorder_point
  AND p.sku NOT IN (
      SELECT DISTINCT poi.sku
      FROM purchase_order_items poi
      JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id = po.po_id
      WHERE po.status IN ('PENDING_APPROVAL', 'APPROVED')
  )
ORDER BY shortfall DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show monthly freight spend trend over the past 12 months
output:
SELECT FORMAT(sh.actual_delivery_date, 'yyyy-MM') AS month,
       COUNT(sh.shipment_id) AS shipment_count,
       SUM(sh.freight_cost) AS total_freight_spend,
       AVG(sh.freight_cost) AS avg_freight_per_shipment
FROM shipments sh
WHERE sh.status = 'DELIVERED'
  AND sh.actual_delivery_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
GROUP BY FORMAT(sh.actual_delivery_date, 'yyyy-MM')
ORDER BY month;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Find purchase orders where the total received quantity is less than ordered quantity
output:
SELECT po.po_number, s.supplier_name,
       SUM(poi.ordered_quantity) AS total_ordered,
       SUM(COALESCE(gri.received_quantity, 0)) AS total_received,
       SUM(poi.ordered_quantity) - SUM(COALESCE(gri.received_quantity, 0)) AS short_qty
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id = s.supplier_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id = poi.po_id
LEFT JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON poi.po_item_id = gri.po_item_id
WHERE po.status IN ('RECEIVED', 'CLOSED')
GROUP BY po.po_number, s.supplier_name
HAVING SUM(poi.ordered_quantity) > SUM(COALESCE(gri.received_quantity, 0))
ORDER BY short_qty DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show all sales orders placed by top-tier customers in the last 90 days
output:
SELECT c.customer_name, so.so_number, so.order_date,
       so.total_value, so.status, so.promised_delivery_date
FROM sales_orders so
JOIN customers c ON so.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE c.segment = 'PREMIUM'
  AND so.order_date >= DATEADD(DAY, -90, GETDATE())
ORDER BY so.order_date DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: What is the invoice aging breakdown — current, 30, 60, 90+ days overdue?
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY, inv.due_date, GETDATE()) <= 0 THEN inv.total_amount - COALESCE(inv.paid_amount,0) ELSE 0 END) AS current_amt,
       SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY, inv.due_date, GETDATE()) BETWEEN 1 AND 30 THEN inv.total_amount - COALESCE(inv.paid_amount,0) ELSE 0 END) AS overdue_1_30,
       SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY, inv.due_date, GETDATE()) BETWEEN 31 AND 60 THEN inv.total_amount - COALESCE(inv.paid_amount,0) ELSE 0 END) AS overdue_31_60,
       SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY, inv.due_date, GETDATE()) > 60 THEN inv.total_amount - COALESCE(inv.paid_amount,0) ELSE 0 END) AS overdue_60_plus
FROM invoices inv
JOIN suppliers s ON inv.supplier_id = s.supplier_id
WHERE inv.status IN ('UNPAID', 'PARTIALLY_PAID')
GROUP BY s.supplier_name
ORDER BY overdue_60_plus DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show total inventory value change week over week
output:
WITH weekly_value AS (
    SELECT DATEPART(WEEK, it.transaction_date) AS week_num,
           YEAR(it.transaction_date) AS yr,
           SUM(CASE WHEN it.transaction_type = 'INBOUND' THEN it.quantity * p.unit_cost
                    WHEN it.transaction_type = 'OUTBOUND' THEN -it.quantity * p.unit_cost
                    ELSE 0 END) AS net_value_change
    FROM inventory_transactions it
    JOIN products p ON it.sku = p.sku
    WHERE it.transaction_date >= DATEADD(WEEK, -12, GETDATE())
    GROUP BY DATEPART(WEEK, it.transaction_date), YEAR(it.transaction_date)
)
SELECT yr, week_num, net_value_change,
       SUM(net_value_change) OVER (ORDER BY yr, week_num ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING) AS cumulative_value
FROM weekly_value
ORDER BY yr, week_num;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Which purchase orders have more than 10 line items?
output:
SELECT po.po_number, s.supplier_name, po.order_date,
       COUNT(poi.po_item_id) AS line_item_count,
       SUM(poi.line_total) AS total_value
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id = s.supplier_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id = poi.po_id
GROUP BY po.po_number, s.supplier_name, po.order_date
HAVING COUNT(poi.po_item_id) > 10
ORDER BY line_item_count DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the number of stockout events per month over the last year
output:
SELECT FORMAT(se.start_date, 'yyyy-MM') AS month,
       COUNT(*) AS stockout_events,
       COUNT(DISTINCT se.sku) AS affected_skus,
       AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY, se.start_date, COALESCE(se.end_date, GETDATE()))) AS avg_duration_days
FROM stockout_events se
WHERE se.start_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
GROUP BY FORMAT(se.start_date, 'yyyy-MM')
ORDER BY month;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Find all SKUs where inbound purchase lead time exceeds the product's defined lead time
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, p.lead_time_days AS standard_lead_time,
       AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY, po.order_date, sh.actual_delivery_date)) AS actual_avg_lead_time,
       AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY, po.order_date, sh.actual_delivery_date)) - p.lead_time_days AS excess_days
FROM products p
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON p.sku = poi.sku
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id = po.po_id
JOIN shipments sh ON po.po_id = sh.po_id
WHERE sh.status = 'DELIVERED'
  AND po.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, p.lead_time_days
HAVING AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY, po.order_date, sh.actual_delivery_date)) > p.lead_time_days
ORDER BY excess_days DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the on-hand stock vs minimum safety stock gap for all SKUs in manufacturing warehouses
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, w.warehouse_name,
       i.quantity_on_hand, i.safety_stock,
       i.quantity_on_hand - i.safety_stock AS stock_vs_safety
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku = p.sku
JOIN warehouses w ON i.warehouse_id = w.warehouse_id
WHERE w.warehouse_type = 'MANUFACTURING'
ORDER BY stock_vs_safety ASC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: List suppliers who have had contract renewals more than twice in the past 5 years
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       COUNT(c.contract_id) AS total_contracts,
       MIN(c.start_date) AS first_contract_date,
       MAX(c.end_date) AS latest_contract_end
FROM suppliers s
JOIN contracts c ON s.supplier_id = c.supplier_id
WHERE c.start_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -5, GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name
HAVING COUNT(c.contract_id) > 2
ORDER BY total_contracts DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show all sales orders that were cancelled and total revenue lost this quarter
output:
SELECT FORMAT(so.order_date, 'yyyy-MM') AS month,
       COUNT(so.so_id) AS cancelled_orders,
       SUM(so.total_value) AS revenue_lost
FROM sales_orders so
WHERE so.status = 'CANCELLED'
  AND so.order_date >= DATEADD(QUARTER, DATEDIFF(QUARTER, 0, GETDATE()), 0)
GROUP BY FORMAT(so.order_date, 'yyyy-MM')
ORDER BY month;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Rank warehouses by total outbound shipment volume this year
output:
SELECT w.warehouse_name,
       SUM(it.quantity) AS total_outbound_units,
       COUNT(DISTINCT it.reference_id) AS distinct_orders,
       RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(it.quantity) DESC) AS volume_rank
FROM inventory_transactions it
JOIN warehouses w ON it.warehouse_id = w.warehouse_id
WHERE it.transaction_type = 'OUTBOUND'
  AND YEAR(it.transaction_date) = YEAR(GETDATE())
GROUP BY w.warehouse_name
ORDER BY volume_rank;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: What is the average number of days between PO approval and first shipment per supplier?
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY, po.approved_date, sh.created_at)) AS avg_days_approval_to_ship,
       COUNT(*) AS sample_count
FROM suppliers s
JOIN purchase_orders po ON s.supplier_id = po.supplier_id
JOIN shipments sh ON po.po_id = sh.po_id
WHERE po.approved_date IS NOT NULL
  AND po.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3
ORDER BY avg_days_approval_to_ship DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the split between domestic and international suppliers by spend
output:
SELECT
       CASE WHEN s.country = 'India' THEN 'DOMESTIC' ELSE 'INTERNATIONAL' END AS supplier_type,
       COUNT(DISTINCT s.supplier_id) AS supplier_count,
       SUM(poi.line_total) AS total_spend,
       ROUND(100.0 * SUM(poi.line_total) / SUM(SUM(poi.line_total)) OVER (), 2) AS spend_pct
FROM suppliers s
JOIN purchase_orders po ON s.supplier_id = po.supplier_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id = poi.po_id
WHERE po.status NOT IN ('CANCELLED')
  AND po.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
GROUP BY CASE WHEN s.country = 'India' THEN 'DOMESTIC' ELSE 'INTERNATIONAL' END;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show demand forecast error trend by month — is forecast accuracy improving?
output:
SELECT df.forecast_period,
       COUNT(*) AS sku_count,
       ROUND(AVG(ABS(df.actual_quantity - df.forecasted_quantity) / NULLIF(df.actual_quantity, 0) * 100), 2) AS avg_mape,
       ROUND(AVG(df.actual_quantity - df.forecasted_quantity), 1) AS avg_bias
FROM demand_forecasts df
WHERE df.actual_quantity IS NOT NULL
  AND df.forecast_period >= FORMAT(DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE()), 'yyyy-MM')
GROUP BY df.forecast_period
ORDER BY df.forecast_period;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Find all work orders started but not completed within planned duration
output:
SELECT wo.wo_number, p.product_name, wo.start_date,
       DATEDIFF(DAY, wo.start_date, GETDATE()) AS days_running,
       wo.planned_qty, COALESCE(wo.actual_qty, 0) AS produced_so_far
FROM work_orders wo
JOIN products p ON wo.product_sku = p.sku
WHERE wo.status = 'IN_PROGRESS'
  AND DATEDIFF(DAY, wo.start_date, GETDATE()) > (
      SELECT AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY, wo2.start_date, wo2.end_date))
      FROM work_orders wo2
      WHERE wo2.product_sku = wo.product_sku
        AND wo2.status = 'COMPLETED'
  )
ORDER BY days_running DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Which products have been reordered from more than 3 different suppliers in the last year?
output:
SELECT poi.sku, p.product_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT po.supplier_id) AS distinct_suppliers_used,
       SUM(poi.ordered_quantity) AS total_ordered
FROM purchase_order_items poi
JOIN products p ON poi.sku = p.sku
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id = po.po_id
WHERE po.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
  AND po.status NOT IN ('CANCELLED')
GROUP BY poi.sku, p.product_name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT po.supplier_id) > 3
ORDER BY distinct_suppliers_used DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the total cost of returns by reason code this year
output:
SELECT ro.reason_code,
       COUNT(ro.return_id) AS return_count,
       SUM(roi.quantity * roi.unit_price) AS total_return_value,
       ROUND(100.0 * SUM(roi.quantity * roi.unit_price) / SUM(SUM(roi.quantity * roi.unit_price)) OVER (), 2) AS pct_of_total
FROM return_orders ro
JOIN return_order_items roi ON ro.return_id = roi.return_id
WHERE ro.return_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, DATEDIFF(YEAR, 0, GETDATE()), 0)
GROUP BY ro.reason_code
ORDER BY total_return_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the inventory coverage ratio — days of stock on hand vs reorder lead time
output:
WITH avg_daily AS (
    SELECT soi.sku,
           SUM(soi.ordered_quantity) / NULLIF(DATEDIFF(DAY, MIN(so.order_date), MAX(so.order_date)), 0) AS daily_demand
    FROM sales_order_items soi
    JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id = so.so_id
    WHERE so.order_date >= DATEADD(DAY, -90, GETDATE())
    GROUP BY soi.sku
)
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, p.lead_time_days,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS total_stock,
       ad.daily_demand,
       ROUND(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) / NULLIF(ad.daily_demand, 0), 1) AS days_of_stock,
       ROUND(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) / NULLIF(ad.daily_demand, 0), 1) / NULLIF(p.lead_time_days, 0) AS coverage_ratio
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON p.sku = i.sku
JOIN avg_daily ad ON p.sku = ad.sku
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, p.lead_time_days, ad.daily_demand
ORDER BY coverage_ratio ASC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show all new suppliers onboarded in the past 3 months with their first purchase order
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.category, s.created_at AS onboarded_date,
       po.po_number AS first_po, po.order_date AS first_po_date,
       po.total_amount AS first_po_value
FROM suppliers s
JOIN (
    SELECT supplier_id, MIN(order_date) AS first_order
    FROM purchase_orders
    GROUP BY supplier_id
) first_po_date ON s.supplier_id = first_po_date.supplier_id
JOIN purchase_orders po ON s.supplier_id = po.supplier_id
  AND po.order_date = first_po_date.first_order
WHERE s.created_at >= DATEADD(MONTH, -3, GETDATE())
ORDER BY s.created_at DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: What is the total weight of shipments per carrier this month?
output:
SELECT sh.carrier,
       COUNT(sh.shipment_id) AS total_shipments,
       SUM(sh.weight_kg) AS total_weight_kg,
       SUM(sh.freight_cost) AS total_freight_cost
FROM shipments sh
WHERE sh.actual_delivery_date >= DATEADD(DAY, 1 - DAY(GETDATE()), CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE))
  AND sh.status = 'DELIVERED'
GROUP BY sh.carrier
ORDER BY total_weight_kg DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the percentage of sales orders fulfilled in full on first attempt
output:
SELECT
    COUNT(*) AS total_delivered_orders,
    SUM(CASE WHEN so.delivered_complete = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS fully_fulfilled,
    ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN so.delivered_complete = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 2) AS full_fulfillment_rate_pct
FROM sales_orders so
WHERE so.status = 'DELIVERED'
  AND so.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE());

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show cumulative GRN value received per supplier for the current year
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       FORMAT(gr.receipt_date, 'yyyy-MM') AS month,
       SUM(poi.unit_price * gri.received_quantity) AS monthly_received_value,
       SUM(SUM(poi.unit_price * gri.received_quantity)) OVER (PARTITION BY s.supplier_id ORDER BY FORMAT(gr.receipt_date,'yyyy-MM') ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING) AS cumulative_value
FROM suppliers s
JOIN purchase_orders po ON s.supplier_id = po.supplier_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id = poi.po_id
JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON poi.po_item_id = gri.po_item_id
JOIN goods_receipts gr ON gri.grn_id = gr.grn_id
WHERE YEAR(gr.receipt_date) = YEAR(GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_id, s.supplier_name, FORMAT(gr.receipt_date, 'yyyy-MM')
ORDER BY s.supplier_name, month;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show all active EOQ parameters and flag those where annual demand has shifted by more than 25%
output:
WITH actual_demand AS (
    SELECT poi.sku, SUM(poi.ordered_quantity) AS actual_annual_demand
    FROM purchase_order_items poi
    JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id = po.po_id
    WHERE po.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
      AND po.status NOT IN ('CANCELLED')
    GROUP BY poi.sku
)
SELECT eoq.sku, p.product_name,
       eoq.annual_demand AS eoq_assumed_demand,
       ad.actual_annual_demand,
       ROUND(100.0 * ABS(ad.actual_annual_demand - eoq.annual_demand) / NULLIF(eoq.annual_demand, 0), 2) AS demand_shift_pct,
       eoq.economic_order_qty
FROM eoq_parameters eoq
JOIN products p ON eoq.sku = p.sku
JOIN actual_demand ad ON eoq.sku = ad.sku
WHERE ABS(ad.actual_annual_demand - eoq.annual_demand) / NULLIF(eoq.annual_demand, 0) > 0.25
ORDER BY demand_shift_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the number and value of purchase orders per payment term bucket
output:
SELECT s.payment_terms,
       COUNT(DISTINCT po.po_id) AS po_count,
       SUM(po.total_amount) AS total_value,
       AVG(po.total_amount) AS avg_po_value
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id = s.supplier_id
WHERE po.status NOT IN ('CANCELLED')
  AND po.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.payment_terms
ORDER BY total_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Which components are used in the highest number of distinct finished goods in the BOM?
output:
SELECT b.child_sku AS component_sku, p.product_name AS component_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT b.parent_sku) AS used_in_fg_count,
       SUM(b.quantity_required) AS total_qty_across_boms
FROM bom b
JOIN products p ON b.child_sku = p.sku
GROUP BY b.child_sku, p.product_name
ORDER BY used_in_fg_count DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the GRN quantity accuracy rate per supplier — received vs ordered
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       SUM(poi.ordered_quantity) AS total_ordered,
       SUM(gri.received_quantity) AS total_received,
       ROUND(100.0 * SUM(gri.received_quantity) / NULLIF(SUM(poi.ordered_quantity), 0), 2) AS receipt_accuracy_pct
FROM suppliers s
JOIN purchase_orders po ON s.supplier_id = po.supplier_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id = poi.po_id
JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON poi.po_item_id = gri.po_item_id
WHERE po.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name
ORDER BY receipt_accuracy_pct ASC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show all contracts that were terminated early and calculate the remaining value lost
output:
SELECT c.contract_number, s.supplier_name,
       c.start_date, c.end_date, c.updated_at AS terminated_on,
       c.contract_value,
       COALESCE(SUM(poi.line_total), 0) AS spend_before_termination,
       c.contract_value - COALESCE(SUM(poi.line_total), 0) AS value_not_utilized
FROM contracts c
JOIN suppliers s ON c.supplier_id = s.supplier_id
LEFT JOIN purchase_orders po ON po.supplier_id = c.supplier_id
  AND po.order_date BETWEEN c.start_date AND c.updated_at
LEFT JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id = poi.po_id
WHERE c.status = 'TERMINATED'
GROUP BY c.contract_number, s.supplier_name, c.start_date,
         c.end_date, c.updated_at, c.contract_value
ORDER BY value_not_utilized DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Update the expected delivery date for all APPROVED shipments from supplier S-1041 by adding 5 days
output:
UPDATE shipments
SET expected_delivery_date = DATEADD(DAY, 5, expected_delivery_date),
    updated_at = GETDATE()
WHERE status = 'IN_TRANSIT'
  AND po_id IN (
      SELECT po_id FROM purchase_orders
      WHERE supplier_id = (
          SELECT supplier_id FROM suppliers WHERE supplier_name = 'S-1041'
      )
      AND status = 'APPROVED'
  );

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Insert a new contract for supplier FastParts Ltd for one year worth 2 million

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Delete all demand forecast records older than 3 years
output:
DELETE FROM demand_forecasts
WHERE forecast_period < FORMAT(DATEADD(YEAR, -3, GETDATE()), 'yyyy-MM');

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Mark all fully paid invoices where paid_amount equals total_amount as PAID
output:
UPDATE invoices
SET status = 'PAID',
    updated_at = GETDATE()
WHERE paid_amount >= total_amount
  AND status != 'PAID';

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Insert a new quality check record for a GRN item that passed inspection

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the percentage of procurement spend by product type
output:
SELECT p.product_type,
       SUM(poi.line_total) AS total_spend,
       ROUND(100.0 * SUM(poi.line_total) / SUM(SUM(poi.line_total)) OVER (), 2) AS spend_pct
FROM purchase_order_items poi
JOIN products p ON poi.sku = p.sku
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id = po.po_id
WHERE po.status NOT IN ('CANCELLED')
  AND po.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
GROUP BY p.product_type
ORDER BY total_spend DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Which shipments are currently lost or unaccounted for?
output:
SELECT sh.tracking_number, sh.carrier, sh.expected_delivery_date,
       po.po_number, s.supplier_name, sh.shipment_value
FROM shipments sh
JOIN purchase_orders po ON sh.po_id = po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id = s.supplier_id
WHERE sh.status = 'LOST'
ORDER BY sh.shipment_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the top product categories by gross margin contribution
output:
SELECT p.category,
       SUM(soi.ordered_quantity * soi.unit_price) AS total_revenue,
       SUM(soi.ordered_quantity * p.unit_cost) AS total_cogs,
       SUM(soi.ordered_quantity * (soi.unit_price - p.unit_cost)) AS gross_margin,
       ROUND(100.0 * SUM(soi.ordered_quantity * (soi.unit_price - p.unit_cost)) / NULLIF(SUM(soi.ordered_quantity * soi.unit_price), 0), 2) AS gm_pct
FROM sales_order_items soi
JOIN products p ON soi.sku = p.sku
JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id = so.so_id
WHERE so.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
  AND so.status NOT IN ('CANCELLED')
GROUP BY p.category
ORDER BY gross_margin DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show all WIP products currently in production with their expected completion dates
output:
SELECT wo.wo_number, p.sku, p.product_name,
       wo.planned_qty, COALESCE(wo.actual_qty, 0) AS produced_so_far,
       wo.start_date, wo.end_date AS planned_completion,
       DATEDIFF(DAY, GETDATE(), wo.end_date) AS days_to_completion
FROM work_orders wo
JOIN products p ON wo.product_sku = p.sku
WHERE wo.status = 'IN_PROGRESS'
ORDER BY wo.end_date ASC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show all logistics partners and their total freight claim amounts
output:
SELECT lp.partner_name, lp.service_type,
       COUNT(fc.claim_id) AS total_claims,
       SUM(fc.claim_amount) AS total_claimed,
       SUM(CASE WHEN fc.status = 'SETTLED' THEN fc.claim_amount ELSE 0 END) AS total_settled
FROM logistics_partners lp
LEFT JOIN shipments sh ON lp.partner_id = sh.logistics_partner_id
LEFT JOIN freight_claims fc ON sh.shipment_id = fc.shipment_id
GROUP BY lp.partner_name, lp.service_type
ORDER BY total_claimed DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show all products where actual demand exceeded 150% of forecasted demand last month
output:
SELECT df.sku, p.product_name,
       df.forecasted_quantity, df.actual_quantity,
       ROUND(100.0 * df.actual_quantity / NULLIF(df.forecasted_quantity, 0), 2) AS actual_vs_forecast_pct
FROM demand_forecasts df
JOIN products p ON df.sku = p.sku
WHERE df.forecast_period = FORMAT(DATEADD(MONTH, -1, GETDATE()), 'yyyy-MM')
  AND df.actual_quantity > df.forecasted_quantity * 1.5
ORDER BY actual_vs_forecast_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Find all suppliers who supply the same SKU at different prices — price inconsistency check
output:
SELECT sp.sku, p.product_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT sp.supplier_id) AS supplier_count,
       MIN(sp.unit_price) AS min_price,
       MAX(sp.unit_price) AS max_price,
       MAX(sp.unit_price) - MIN(sp.unit_price) AS price_spread,
       ROUND(100.0 * (MAX(sp.unit_price) - MIN(sp.unit_price)) / NULLIF(MIN(sp.unit_price), 0), 2) AS spread_pct
FROM sourcing_products sp
JOIN products p ON sp.sku = p.sku
WHERE sp.status = 'APPROVED'
GROUP BY sp.sku, p.product_name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT sp.supplier_id) > 1
   AND MAX(sp.unit_price) > MIN(sp.unit_price)
ORDER BY spread_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: How many days of inventory do we hold on average per product category?
output:
WITH daily_sales AS (
    SELECT p.category,
           SUM(soi.ordered_quantity) / 90.0 AS daily_units
    FROM sales_order_items soi
    JOIN products p ON soi.sku = p.sku
    JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id = so.so_id
    WHERE so.order_date >= DATEADD(DAY, -90, GETDATE())
    GROUP BY p.category
),
stock_by_cat AS (
    SELECT p.category, SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS total_stock
    FROM inventory i
    JOIN products p ON i.sku = p.sku
    GROUP BY p.category
)
SELECT sc.category,
       sc.total_stock,
       ds.daily_units,
       ROUND(sc.total_stock / NULLIF(ds.daily_units, 0), 1) AS days_of_inventory
FROM stock_by_cat sc
JOIN daily_sales ds ON sc.category = ds.category
ORDER BY days_of_inventory DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show sales order items where the unit price charged differs from the standard product price
output:
SELECT so.so_number, c.customer_name, soi.sku, p.product_name,
       soi.unit_price AS charged_price,
       p.unit_cost AS standard_cost,
       soi.unit_price - p.unit_cost AS price_diff,
       ROUND(100.0 * (soi.unit_price - p.unit_cost) / NULLIF(p.unit_cost, 0), 2) AS margin_pct
FROM sales_order_items soi
JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id = so.so_id
JOIN customers c ON so.customer_id = c.customer_id
JOIN products p ON soi.sku = p.sku
WHERE ABS(soi.unit_price - p.unit_cost) / NULLIF(p.unit_cost, 0) > 0.50
  AND so.order_date >= DATEADD(MONTH, -1, GETDATE())
ORDER BY margin_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the number of unique customers served per warehouse dispatch zone
output:
SELECT w.warehouse_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT so.customer_id) AS unique_customers,
       COUNT(DISTINCT so.so_id) AS total_orders_dispatched
FROM inventory_transactions it
JOIN warehouses w ON it.warehouse_id = w.warehouse_id
JOIN sales_orders so ON it.reference_id = so.so_number
WHERE it.transaction_type = 'OUTBOUND'
  AND it.transaction_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
GROUP BY w.warehouse_name
ORDER BY unique_customers DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the supplier payment efficiency — how many days early or late are we paying invoices?
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.payment_terms,
       AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY, inv.due_date, pay.paid_date)) AS avg_payment_timing_days,
       SUM(CASE WHEN pay.paid_date <= inv.due_date THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS paid_on_time,
       COUNT(*) AS total_paid
FROM invoices inv
JOIN suppliers s ON inv.supplier_id = s.supplier_id
JOIN (
    SELECT invoice_id, MAX(payment_date) AS paid_date
    FROM payments
    GROUP BY invoice_id
) pay ON inv.invoice_id = pay.invoice_id
WHERE inv.status = 'PAID'
  AND inv.invoice_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, s.payment_terms
ORDER BY avg_payment_timing_days DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show all products currently in both inbound and outbound shipments simultaneously
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name,
       inb.inbound_qty, outb.outbound_qty
FROM products p
JOIN (
    SELECT poi.sku, SUM(poi.ordered_quantity) AS inbound_qty
    FROM purchase_order_items poi
    JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id = po.po_id
    JOIN shipments sh ON po.po_id = sh.po_id
    WHERE sh.status = 'IN_TRANSIT'
    GROUP BY poi.sku
) inb ON p.sku = inb.sku
JOIN (
    SELECT soi.sku, SUM(soi.ordered_quantity - COALESCE(soi.fulfilled_quantity, 0)) AS outbound_qty
    FROM sales_order_items soi
    JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id = so.so_id
    WHERE so.status IN ('CONFIRMED', 'PARTIALLY_SHIPPED')
    GROUP BY soi.sku
) outb ON p.sku = outb.sku
ORDER BY p.sku;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Which products have the highest variance between max and min purchase price in the last year?
output:
SELECT poi.sku, p.product_name,
       MIN(poi.unit_price) AS min_price,
       MAX(poi.unit_price) AS max_price,
       MAX(poi.unit_price) - MIN(poi.unit_price) AS price_range,
       ROUND(100.0 * (MAX(poi.unit_price) - MIN(poi.unit_price)) / NULLIF(MIN(poi.unit_price), 0), 2) AS price_range_pct
FROM purchase_order_items poi
JOIN products p ON poi.sku = p.sku
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id = po.po_id
WHERE po.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
  AND po.status NOT IN ('CANCELLED')
GROUP BY poi.sku, p.product_name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT poi.unit_price) > 1
ORDER BY price_range_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show all inter-warehouse stock transfers planned for next week
output:
SELECT it.reference_id AS transfer_id, p.sku, p.product_name,
       w_from.warehouse_name AS from_warehouse,
       w_to.warehouse_name AS to_warehouse,
       it.quantity, it.transaction_date AS planned_date
FROM inventory_transactions it
JOIN products p ON it.sku = p.sku
JOIN warehouses w_from ON it.warehouse_id = w_from.warehouse_id
JOIN inventory_transactions it_in ON it.reference_id = it_in.reference_id
  AND it_in.transaction_type = 'INBOUND' AND it_in.sku = it.sku
JOIN warehouses w_to ON it_in.warehouse_id = w_to.warehouse_id
WHERE it.transaction_type = 'OUTBOUND'
  AND it.transaction_date BETWEEN GETDATE() AND DATEADD(DAY, 7, GETDATE())
ORDER BY it.transaction_date;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: What is the first-pass yield rate per manufacturing work center?
output:
SELECT p.category AS product_category,
       COUNT(wo.wo_id) AS work_orders,
       SUM(wo.planned_qty) AS total_planned,
       SUM(CASE WHEN wo.actual_qty >= wo.planned_qty THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS first_pass_ok,
       ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN wo.actual_qty >= wo.planned_qty THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(wo.wo_id), 0), 2) AS first_pass_yield_pct
FROM work_orders wo
JOIN products p ON wo.product_sku = p.sku
WHERE wo.status = 'COMPLETED'
  AND wo.end_date >= DATEADD(QUARTER, -1, GETDATE())
GROUP BY p.category
ORDER BY first_pass_yield_pct ASC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show me all purchase orders with emergency or rush delivery flagged
output:
SELECT po.po_number, s.supplier_name, po.order_date,
       po.expected_delivery_date, po.total_amount,
       DATEDIFF(DAY, po.order_date, po.expected_delivery_date) AS lead_time_days
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id = s.supplier_id
WHERE DATEDIFF(DAY, po.order_date, po.expected_delivery_date) <= 3
  AND po.status NOT IN ('CANCELLED')
ORDER BY po.order_date DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the average supplier response time from PO creation to shipment confirmation
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       COUNT(*) AS po_count,
       AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY, po.order_date, sh.created_at)) AS avg_response_days,
       MIN(DATEDIFF(DAY, po.order_date, sh.created_at)) AS fastest_days,
       MAX(DATEDIFF(DAY, po.order_date, sh.created_at)) AS slowest_days
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id = s.supplier_id
JOIN shipments sh ON po.po_id = sh.po_id
WHERE sh.status != 'LOST'
  AND po.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 5
ORDER BY avg_response_days ASC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Which customers have placed repeat orders for the same SKU more than 5 times?
output:
SELECT c.customer_name, soi.sku, p.product_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT so.so_id) AS repeat_order_count,
       SUM(soi.ordered_quantity) AS total_qty_ordered
FROM sales_order_items soi
JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id = so.so_id
JOIN customers c ON so.customer_id = c.customer_id
JOIN products p ON soi.sku = p.sku
WHERE so.status NOT IN ('CANCELLED')
GROUP BY c.customer_name, soi.sku, p.product_name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT so.so_id) > 5
ORDER BY repeat_order_count DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the quantity of each SKU received vs dispatched per month for the past 6 months
output:
SELECT FORMAT(it.transaction_date, 'yyyy-MM') AS month,
       it.sku, p.product_name,
       SUM(CASE WHEN it.transaction_type = 'INBOUND' THEN it.quantity ELSE 0 END) AS received,
       SUM(CASE WHEN it.transaction_type = 'OUTBOUND' THEN it.quantity ELSE 0 END) AS dispatched
FROM inventory_transactions it
JOIN products p ON it.sku = p.sku
WHERE it.transaction_date >= DATEADD(MONTH, -6, GETDATE())
  AND it.transaction_type IN ('INBOUND', 'OUTBOUND')
GROUP BY FORMAT(it.transaction_date, 'yyyy-MM'), it.sku, p.product_name
ORDER BY month, it.sku;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: What is the total customs and import duty cost for international shipments this year?
output:
SELECT sh.carrier, lp.service_type,
       COUNT(sh.shipment_id) AS international_shipments,
       SUM(sh.customs_duty_amount) AS total_customs_duty,
       ROUND(100.0 * SUM(sh.customs_duty_amount) / NULLIF(SUM(sh.shipment_value), 0), 2) AS duty_as_pct_of_value
FROM shipments sh
JOIN logistics_partners lp ON sh.logistics_partner_id = lp.partner_id
WHERE lp.service_type IN ('AIR', 'SEA')
  AND sh.actual_delivery_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, DATEDIFF(YEAR, 0, GETDATE()), 0)
  AND sh.status = 'DELIVERED'
GROUP BY sh.carrier, lp.service_type
ORDER BY total_customs_duty DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show all products with zero stock across ALL warehouses
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, p.product_type, p.category
FROM products p
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM inventory i
    WHERE i.sku = p.sku
      AND i.quantity_on_hand > 0
)
ORDER BY p.category, p.product_name;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show a heatmap dataset of shipment volume by origin and destination
output:
SELECT r.origin, r.destination,
       COUNT(sh.shipment_id) AS shipment_count,
       SUM(sh.shipment_value) AS total_value,
       SUM(sh.weight_kg) AS total_weight
FROM shipments sh
JOIN routes r ON sh.route_id = r.route_id
WHERE sh.status = 'DELIVERED'
  AND sh.actual_delivery_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
GROUP BY r.origin, r.destination
ORDER BY shipment_count DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the bottom 10 performing products by gross profit margin
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, p.category,
       SUM(soi.ordered_quantity * soi.unit_price) AS revenue,
       SUM(soi.ordered_quantity * p.unit_cost) AS cost,
       ROUND(100.0 * SUM(soi.ordered_quantity * (soi.unit_price - p.unit_cost)) / NULLIF(SUM(soi.ordered_quantity * soi.unit_price), 0), 2) AS gm_pct
FROM sales_order_items soi
JOIN products p ON soi.sku = p.sku
JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id = so.so_id
WHERE so.status NOT IN ('CANCELLED')
  AND so.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, p.category
ORDER BY gm_pct ASC
OFFSET 0 ROWS FETCH NEXT 10 ROWS ONLY;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the supplier on-time delivery performance for each quarter of the past year
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       DATEPART(QUARTER, sh.actual_delivery_date) AS quarter,
       COUNT(*) AS deliveries,
       SUM(CASE WHEN sh.actual_delivery_date <= sh.expected_delivery_date THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS on_time,
       ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN sh.actual_delivery_date <= sh.expected_delivery_date THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS otd_pct
FROM suppliers s
JOIN purchase_orders po ON s.supplier_id = po.supplier_id
JOIN shipments sh ON po.po_id = sh.po_id
WHERE sh.actual_delivery_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
  AND sh.status = 'DELIVERED'
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, DATEPART(QUARTER, sh.actual_delivery_date)
ORDER BY s.supplier_name, quarter;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show all product-supplier combinations where the approved price hasn't been updated in 2 years
output:
SELECT sp.sku, p.product_name, s.supplier_name,
       sp.unit_price, sp.updated_at AS last_price_update,
       DATEDIFF(DAY, sp.updated_at, GETDATE()) AS days_since_update
FROM sourcing_products sp
JOIN products p ON sp.sku = p.sku
JOIN suppliers s ON sp.supplier_id = s.supplier_id
WHERE sp.status = 'APPROVED'
  AND sp.updated_at < DATEADD(YEAR, -2, GETDATE())
ORDER BY days_since_update DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: What is the ratio of emergency purchases to total purchases per category?
output:
SELECT s.category,
       COUNT(po.po_id) AS total_pos,
       SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY, po.order_date, po.expected_delivery_date) <= 3 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS emergency_pos,
       ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY, po.order_date, po.expected_delivery_date) <= 3 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(po.po_id), 0), 2) AS emergency_rate_pct
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id = s.supplier_id
WHERE po.status NOT IN ('CANCELLED')
  AND po.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.category
ORDER BY emergency_rate_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the material requirement plan gap — how much raw material do we need vs what's available for next month's production plan?
output:
SELECT b.child_sku AS raw_material_sku, p_rm.product_name,
       SUM(wo.planned_qty * b.quantity_required) AS total_required_qty,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS available_qty,
       GREATEST(0, SUM(wo.planned_qty * b.quantity_required) - SUM(i.quantity_on_hand)) AS shortfall
FROM work_orders wo
JOIN bom b ON wo.product_sku = b.parent_sku
JOIN products p_rm ON b.child_sku = p_rm.sku
JOIN inventory i ON b.child_sku = i.sku
WHERE wo.status = 'PLANNED'
  AND wo.start_date BETWEEN DATEADD(MONTH, 1, DATEADD(DAY, 1 - DAY(GETDATE()), CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE)))
                       AND DATEADD(MONTH, 2, DATEADD(DAY, 1 - DAY(GETDATE()), CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE)))
GROUP BY b.child_sku, p_rm.product_name
ORDER BY shortfall DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Find all high-value purchase orders above 1 million that required emergency approval
output:
SELECT po.po_number, s.supplier_name, po.order_date,
       po.approved_date, po.total_amount,
       DATEDIFF(HOUR, po.order_date, po.approved_date) AS approval_hours
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id = s.supplier_id
WHERE po.total_amount > 1000000
  AND DATEDIFF(HOUR, po.order_date, po.approved_date) < 24
  AND po.status NOT IN ('CANCELLED')
ORDER BY po.total_amount DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the running total of inventory write-offs per category this year
output:
SELECT p.category,
       FORMAT(it.transaction_date, 'yyyy-MM') AS month,
       ABS(SUM(CASE WHEN it.quantity < 0 THEN it.quantity * p.unit_cost ELSE 0 END)) AS monthly_writeoff,
       SUM(ABS(SUM(CASE WHEN it.quantity < 0 THEN it.quantity * p.unit_cost ELSE 0 END)))
          OVER (PARTITION BY p.category ORDER BY FORMAT(it.transaction_date,'yyyy-MM') ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING) AS cumulative_writeoff
FROM inventory_transactions it
JOIN products p ON it.sku = p.sku
WHERE it.transaction_type = 'ADJUSTMENT'
  AND it.quantity < 0
  AND YEAR(it.transaction_date) = YEAR(GETDATE())
GROUP BY p.category, FORMAT(it.transaction_date, 'yyyy-MM')
ORDER BY p.category, month;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Which routes have both the highest volume and lowest delay rate — best performing routes?
output:
SELECT r.origin, r.destination, r.carrier,
       COUNT(sh.shipment_id) AS total_shipments,
       ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN sh.actual_delivery_date <= sh.expected_delivery_date THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 2) AS on_time_pct,
       DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY COUNT(sh.shipment_id) DESC) AS volume_rank,
       DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN sh.actual_delivery_date <= sh.expected_delivery_date THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 2) DESC) AS otd_rank
FROM routes r
JOIN shipments sh ON r.route_id = sh.route_id
WHERE sh.status = 'DELIVERED'
  AND sh.actual_delivery_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
GROUP BY r.origin, r.destination, r.carrier
HAVING COUNT(sh.shipment_id) >= 10
ORDER BY volume_rank + otd_rank ASC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show all purchase orders where total invoice amount exceeds PO value by any amount
output:
SELECT po.po_number, s.supplier_name,
       po.total_amount AS po_value,
       SUM(inv.total_amount) AS total_invoiced,
       SUM(inv.total_amount) - po.total_amount AS overbilled_amount
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id = s.supplier_id
JOIN invoices inv ON inv.supplier_id = po.supplier_id
  AND inv.invoice_date >= po.order_date
WHERE po.status NOT IN ('CANCELLED')
GROUP BY po.po_number, s.supplier_name, po.total_amount
HAVING SUM(inv.total_amount) > po.total_amount
ORDER BY overbilled_amount DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show all pending return orders that have been waiting for approval more than 7 days
output:
SELECT ro.return_order_number, c.customer_name,
       so.so_number, ro.return_date,
       ro.reason_code,
       DATEDIFF(DAY, ro.return_date, GETDATE()) AS days_pending,
       SUM(roi.quantity * roi.unit_price) AS return_value
FROM return_orders ro
JOIN customers c ON ro.customer_id = c.customer_id
JOIN sales_orders so ON ro.so_id = so.so_id
JOIN return_order_items roi ON ro.return_id = roi.return_id
WHERE ro.status = 'PENDING'
  AND DATEDIFF(DAY, ro.return_date, GETDATE()) > 7
GROUP BY ro.return_order_number, c.customer_name, so.so_number,
         ro.return_date, ro.reason_code
ORDER BY days_pending DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show all approved sourcing products where the supplier is currently INACTIVE
output:
SELECT sp.sku, p.product_name, s.supplier_name, s.status AS supplier_status,
       sp.unit_price, sp.updated_at
FROM sourcing_products sp
JOIN products p ON sp.sku = p.sku
JOIN suppliers s ON sp.supplier_id = s.supplier_id
WHERE sp.status = 'APPROVED'
  AND s.status != 'ACTIVE'
ORDER BY p.product_name;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the production plan coverage — how many days of finished goods stock do we have relative to open sales orders?
output:
WITH pending_demand AS (
    SELECT soi.sku,
           SUM(soi.ordered_quantity - COALESCE(soi.fulfilled_quantity, 0)) AS unfulfilled_qty
    FROM sales_order_items soi
    JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id = so.so_id
    WHERE so.status IN ('OPEN', 'CONFIRMED')
    GROUP BY soi.sku
),
wip_production AS (
    SELECT wo.product_sku AS sku,
           SUM(wo.planned_qty - COALESCE(wo.actual_qty, 0)) AS remaining_production
    FROM work_orders wo
    WHERE wo.status IN ('PLANNED', 'IN_PROGRESS')
    GROUP BY wo.product_sku
)
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS on_hand_stock,
       COALESCE(wp.remaining_production, 0) AS wip_qty,
       COALESCE(pd.unfulfilled_qty, 0) AS pending_demand,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) + COALESCE(wp.remaining_production, 0) - COALESCE(pd.unfulfilled_qty, 0) AS net_projected_stock
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON p.sku = i.sku
LEFT JOIN pending_demand pd ON p.sku = pd.sku
LEFT JOIN wip_production wp ON p.sku = wp.sku
WHERE p.product_type = 'FINISHED_GOODS'
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, pd.unfulfilled_qty, wp.remaining_production
ORDER BY net_projected_stock ASC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the warehouse capacity forecast for the next 30 days based on incoming shipments
output:
SELECT w.warehouse_name,
       w.total_capacity_units,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS current_stock,
       COALESCE(incoming.expected_inbound, 0) AS expected_inbound_30d,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) + COALESCE(incoming.expected_inbound, 0) AS projected_stock,
       ROUND(100.0 * (SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) + COALESCE(incoming.expected_inbound, 0)) / NULLIF(w.total_capacity_units, 0), 2) AS projected_utilization_pct
FROM warehouses w
JOIN inventory i ON w.warehouse_id = i.warehouse_id
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT gr.warehouse_id, SUM(gri.received_quantity) AS expected_inbound
    FROM shipments sh
    JOIN purchase_orders po ON sh.po_id = po.po_id
    JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id = poi.po_id
    JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON poi.po_item_id = gri.po_item_id
    JOIN goods_receipts gr ON gri.grn_id = gr.grn_id
    WHERE sh.expected_delivery_date BETWEEN GETDATE() AND DATEADD(DAY, 30, GETDATE())
    GROUP BY gr.warehouse_id
) incoming ON w.warehouse_id = incoming.warehouse_id
GROUP BY w.warehouse_id, w.warehouse_name, w.total_capacity_units, incoming.expected_inbound
ORDER BY projected_utilization_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Update quantity on hand for SKU RM-1100 in warehouse WH-02 after a physical count of 850 units
output:
UPDATE inventory
SET quantity_on_hand = 850,
    updated_at = GETDATE()
WHERE sku = 'RM-1100'
  AND warehouse_id = (
      SELECT warehouse_id FROM warehouses WHERE warehouse_name = 'WH-02'
  );

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Archive all closed purchase orders older than 5 years to an orders archive table
output:
INSERT INTO purchase_orders_archive
SELECT *
FROM purchase_orders
WHERE status = 'CLOSED'
  AND order_date < DATEADD(YEAR, -5, GETDATE());

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the top 3 suppliers by delivery performance per product category
output:
WITH supplier_otd AS (
    SELECT s.supplier_name, p.category,
           ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN sh.actual_delivery_date <= sh.expected_delivery_date THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 2) AS otd_pct,
           COUNT(*) AS deliveries
    FROM suppliers s
    JOIN purchase_orders po ON s.supplier_id = po.supplier_id
    JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id = poi.po_id
    JOIN products p ON poi.sku = p.sku
    JOIN shipments sh ON po.po_id = sh.po_id
    WHERE sh.status = 'DELIVERED'
      AND po.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
    GROUP BY s.supplier_name, p.category
    HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3
)
SELECT category, supplier_name, otd_pct, deliveries,
       ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY category ORDER BY otd_pct DESC) AS rank_in_category
FROM supplier_otd
QUALIFY rank_in_category <= 3
ORDER BY category, rank_in_category;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show total sales order value per customer for the past quarter vs same quarter last year
output:
SELECT c.customer_name,
       SUM(CASE WHEN so.order_date >= DATEADD(QUARTER, DATEDIFF(QUARTER,0,GETDATE()), 0)
                THEN so.total_value ELSE 0 END) AS current_quarter,
       SUM(CASE WHEN so.order_date >= DATEADD(QUARTER, DATEDIFF(QUARTER,0,GETDATE()) - 4, 0)
                 AND so.order_date < DATEADD(QUARTER, DATEDIFF(QUARTER,0,GETDATE()) - 3, 0)
                THEN so.total_value ELSE 0 END) AS same_qtr_last_year
FROM customers c
JOIN sales_orders so ON c.customer_id = so.customer_id
WHERE so.status NOT IN ('CANCELLED')
GROUP BY c.customer_name
ORDER BY current_quarter DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the demand spike alert — SKUs where this week's orders are 2x the 4-week average
output:
WITH four_week_avg AS (
    SELECT soi.sku,
           SUM(soi.ordered_quantity) / 4.0 AS avg_weekly_demand
    FROM sales_order_items soi
    JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id = so.so_id
    WHERE so.order_date BETWEEN DATEADD(WEEK, -4, GETDATE()) AND DATEADD(WEEK, -1, GETDATE())
    GROUP BY soi.sku
),
this_week AS (
    SELECT soi.sku, SUM(soi.ordered_quantity) AS current_week_demand
    FROM sales_order_items soi
    JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id = so.so_id
    WHERE so.order_date >= DATEADD(DAY, -DATEPART(WEEKDAY, GETDATE()) + 1, CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE))
    GROUP BY soi.sku
)
SELECT tw.sku, p.product_name,
       fwa.avg_weekly_demand, tw.current_week_demand,
       ROUND(tw.current_week_demand / NULLIF(fwa.avg_weekly_demand, 0), 2) AS demand_spike_ratio
FROM this_week tw
JOIN four_week_avg fwa ON tw.sku = fwa.sku
JOIN products p ON tw.sku = p.sku
WHERE tw.current_week_demand > fwa.avg_weekly_demand * 2
ORDER BY demand_spike_ratio DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show defect trend by supplier — monthly defect rate for the last 6 months
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       FORMAT(qc.inspection_date, 'yyyy-MM') AS month,
       SUM(gri.received_quantity) AS total_received,
       SUM(qc.defective_quantity) AS total_defects,
       ROUND(100.0 * SUM(qc.defective_quantity) / NULLIF(SUM(gri.received_quantity), 0), 3) AS defect_rate_pct
FROM quality_checks qc
JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON qc.grn_item_id = gri.grn_item_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON gri.po_item_id = poi.po_item_id
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id = po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id = s.supplier_id
WHERE qc.inspection_date >= DATEADD(MONTH, -6, GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, FORMAT(qc.inspection_date, 'yyyy-MM')
ORDER BY s.supplier_name, month;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the overstock value — inventory above max stock threshold
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, w.warehouse_name,
       i.quantity_on_hand,
       i.max_stock_level,
       i.quantity_on_hand - i.max_stock_level AS overstock_qty,
       (i.quantity_on_hand - i.max_stock_level) * p.unit_cost AS overstock_value
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku = p.sku
JOIN warehouses w ON i.warehouse_id = w.warehouse_id
WHERE i.quantity_on_hand > i.max_stock_level
  AND i.max_stock_level IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY overstock_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Find all purchase orders that were re-raised after being cancelled — same supplier and SKU within 30 days
output:
SELECT po_new.po_number AS new_po, po_cancel.po_number AS cancelled_po,
       s.supplier_name, poi_new.sku,
       po_cancel.order_date AS cancelled_date,
       po_new.order_date AS re_raised_date,
       DATEDIFF(DAY, po_cancel.order_date, po_new.order_date) AS days_between
FROM purchase_orders po_cancel
JOIN purchase_order_items poi_cancel ON po_cancel.po_id = poi_cancel.po_id
JOIN purchase_orders po_new ON po_cancel.supplier_id = po_new.supplier_id
  AND po_new.order_date BETWEEN po_cancel.order_date AND DATEADD(DAY, 30, po_cancel.order_date)
  AND po_new.status != 'CANCELLED'
JOIN purchase_order_items poi_new ON po_new.po_id = poi_new.po_id
  AND poi_cancel.sku = poi_new.sku
JOIN suppliers s ON po_cancel.supplier_id = s.supplier_id
WHERE po_cancel.status = 'CANCELLED'
ORDER BY days_between ASC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the receiving productivity — average GRN lines processed per day per warehouse
output:
SELECT w.warehouse_name,
       COUNT(gri.grn_item_id) AS total_grn_lines,
       COUNT(DISTINCT CAST(gr.receipt_date AS DATE)) AS working_days,
       ROUND(1.0 * COUNT(gri.grn_item_id) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT CAST(gr.receipt_date AS DATE)), 0), 1) AS avg_lines_per_day
FROM goods_receipts gr
JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON gr.grn_id = gri.grn_id
JOIN warehouses w ON gr.warehouse_id = w.warehouse_id
WHERE gr.receipt_date >= DATEADD(MONTH, -3, GETDATE())
GROUP BY w.warehouse_name
ORDER BY avg_lines_per_day DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show all contracts due for renewal review in the next 90 days with remaining value
output:
SELECT c.contract_number, s.supplier_name, c.end_date,
       DATEDIFF(DAY, GETDATE(), c.end_date) AS days_to_renewal,
       c.contract_value,
       COALESCE(SUM(poi.line_total), 0) AS spend_to_date,
       c.contract_value - COALESCE(SUM(poi.line_total), 0) AS remaining_value
FROM contracts c
JOIN suppliers s ON c.supplier_id = s.supplier_id
LEFT JOIN purchase_orders po ON po.supplier_id = c.supplier_id
  AND po.order_date BETWEEN c.start_date AND GETDATE()
LEFT JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id = poi.po_id
WHERE c.status = 'ACTIVE'
  AND c.end_date BETWEEN GETDATE() AND DATEADD(DAY, 90, GETDATE())
GROUP BY c.contract_number, s.supplier_name, c.end_date, c.contract_value
ORDER BY days_to_renewal ASC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show total claim value settled vs outstanding per logistics partner
output:
SELECT lp.partner_name,
       SUM(CASE WHEN fc.status = 'SETTLED' THEN fc.claim_amount ELSE 0 END) AS settled_value,
       SUM(CASE WHEN fc.status IN ('OPEN','IN_REVIEW') THEN fc.claim_amount ELSE 0 END) AS outstanding_value,
       COUNT(*) AS total_claims
FROM logistics_partners lp
JOIN shipments sh ON lp.partner_id = sh.logistics_partner_id
JOIN freight_claims fc ON sh.shipment_id = fc.shipment_id
GROUP BY lp.partner_name
ORDER BY outstanding_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the number of distinct SKUs purchased per supplier this year
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT poi.sku) AS distinct_skus_purchased,
       COUNT(po.po_id) AS total_pos
FROM suppliers s
JOIN purchase_orders po ON s.supplier_id = po.supplier_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id = poi.po_id
WHERE po.status NOT IN ('CANCELLED')
  AND YEAR(po.order_date) = YEAR(GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name
ORDER BY distinct_skus_purchased DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the top 5 slowest product categories by order-to-delivery time
output:
SELECT p.category,
       COUNT(DISTINCT so.so_id) AS order_count,
       AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY, so.order_date, so.ship_date)) AS avg_order_to_ship,
       ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.90) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY DATEDIFF(DAY, so.order_date, so.ship_date)) OVER (PARTITION BY p.category), 1) AS p90_days
FROM sales_orders so
JOIN sales_order_items soi ON so.so_id = soi.so_id
JOIN products p ON soi.sku = p.sku
WHERE so.ship_date IS NOT NULL
  AND so.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
GROUP BY p.category, DATEDIFF(DAY, so.order_date, so.ship_date)
ORDER BY avg_order_to_ship DESC
OFFSET 0 ROWS FETCH NEXT 5 ROWS ONLY;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: What is the write-off rate of batches that expired before being fully consumed?
output:
SELECT p.category,
       COUNT(b.batch_id) AS expired_batches,
       SUM(b.quantity_remaining) AS wasted_qty,
       SUM(b.quantity_remaining * p.unit_cost) AS wasted_value
FROM batches b
JOIN products p ON b.sku = p.sku
WHERE b.expiry_date < GETDATE()
  AND b.quantity_remaining > 0
GROUP BY p.category
ORDER BY wasted_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the percent of orders shipped via air freight vs ground to high priority customers
output:
SELECT lp.service_type,
       COUNT(sh.shipment_id) AS shipments,
       ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(sh.shipment_id) / SUM(COUNT(sh.shipment_id)) OVER (), 2) AS pct_of_shipments
FROM shipments sh
JOIN logistics_partners lp ON sh.logistics_partner_id = lp.partner_id
JOIN purchase_orders po ON sh.po_id = po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id = s.supplier_id
WHERE sh.status = 'DELIVERED'
  AND sh.actual_delivery_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
GROUP BY lp.service_type
ORDER BY shipments DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show overdue purchase requisitions grouped by department and priority
output:
SELECT e.department, pr.priority,
       COUNT(*) AS overdue_count,
       SUM(pr.total_estimated_value) AS total_value,
       AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY, pr.created_date, GETDATE())) AS avg_days_pending
FROM purchase_requisitions pr
JOIN employees e ON pr.created_by = e.employee_id
WHERE pr.status = 'PENDING_APPROVAL'
  AND DATEDIFF(DAY, pr.created_date, GETDATE()) > 7
GROUP BY e.department, pr.priority
ORDER BY e.department, CASE pr.priority WHEN 'CRITICAL' THEN 1 WHEN 'HIGH' THEN 2 WHEN 'MEDIUM' THEN 3 ELSE 4 END;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Find all SKUs that appear in the BOM but have no inventory record
output:
SELECT DISTINCT b.child_sku AS sku, p.product_name
FROM bom b
JOIN products p ON b.child_sku = p.sku
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM inventory i WHERE i.sku = b.child_sku
)
ORDER BY b.child_sku;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the impact of late deliveries on production — work orders delayed due to material shortages
output:
SELECT wo.wo_number, p.product_name, wo.start_date,
       b.child_sku AS missing_component,
       p_comp.product_name AS component_name,
       sh.expected_delivery_date AS component_due,
       DATEDIFF(DAY, wo.start_date, sh.expected_delivery_date) AS days_impact
FROM work_orders wo
JOIN bom b ON wo.product_sku = b.parent_sku
JOIN products p ON wo.product_sku = p.sku
JOIN products p_comp ON b.child_sku = p_comp.sku
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON poi.sku = b.child_sku
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id = po.po_id
JOIN shipments sh ON po.po_id = sh.po_id
WHERE wo.status = 'PLANNED'
  AND sh.status = 'IN_TRANSIT'
  AND sh.expected_delivery_date > wo.start_date
ORDER BY days_impact DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Which customers have not placed any order in the last 6 months but were active before?
output:
SELECT c.customer_name, c.segment, c.contact_email,
       MAX(so.order_date) AS last_order_date,
       DATEDIFF(DAY, MAX(so.order_date), GETDATE()) AS days_inactive
FROM customers c
JOIN sales_orders so ON c.customer_id = so.customer_id
GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.segment, c.contact_email
HAVING MAX(so.order_date) < DATEADD(MONTH, -6, GETDATE())
   AND MAX(so.order_date) >= DATEADD(YEAR, -2, GETDATE())
ORDER BY days_inactive DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show inventory holding cost per SKU assuming 20% annual holding rate
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, p.category,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS total_units,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand * p.unit_cost) AS inventory_value,
       ROUND(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand * p.unit_cost) * 0.20, 2) AS annual_holding_cost,
       ROUND(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand * p.unit_cost) * 0.20 / 365, 2) AS daily_holding_cost
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku = p.sku
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, p.category
ORDER BY annual_holding_cost DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show all GRN receipts processed without a matching purchase order
output:
SELECT gr.grn_number, gr.receipt_date, w.warehouse_name,
       gri.sku, gri.received_quantity
FROM goods_receipts gr
JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON gr.grn_id = gri.grn_id
JOIN warehouses w ON gr.warehouse_id = w.warehouse_id
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM purchase_order_items poi
    WHERE poi.po_item_id = gri.po_item_id
)
ORDER BY gr.receipt_date DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the most recent supplier performance evaluation scores with trend vs previous evaluation
output:
WITH latest AS (
    SELECT supplier_id, evaluation_date, quality_score, delivery_score, cost_score,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY supplier_id ORDER BY evaluation_date DESC) AS rn
    FROM supplier_performance
),
prev AS (
    SELECT supplier_id, evaluation_date, quality_score, delivery_score, cost_score,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY supplier_id ORDER BY evaluation_date DESC) AS rn
    FROM supplier_performance
)
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       l.quality_score, l.delivery_score, l.cost_score,
       l.quality_score - p.quality_score AS quality_trend,
       l.delivery_score - p.delivery_score AS delivery_trend
FROM suppliers s
JOIN latest l ON s.supplier_id = l.supplier_id AND l.rn = 1
JOIN prev p ON s.supplier_id = p.supplier_id AND p.rn = 2
ORDER BY s.supplier_name;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show all inbound shipments expected today with their purchase order details
output:
SELECT sh.tracking_number, sh.carrier, s.supplier_name,
       po.po_number, po.total_amount,
       w.warehouse_name AS destination_warehouse,
       sh.expected_delivery_date
FROM shipments sh
JOIN purchase_orders po ON sh.po_id = po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id = s.supplier_id
JOIN goods_receipts gr ON gr.grn_id IN (
    SELECT grn_id FROM goods_receipt_items gri
    JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON gri.po_item_id = poi.po_item_id
    WHERE poi.po_id = po.po_id
)
JOIN warehouses w ON gr.warehouse_id = w.warehouse_id
WHERE CAST(sh.expected_delivery_date AS DATE) = CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE)
  AND sh.status = 'IN_TRANSIT'
ORDER BY sh.expected_delivery_date;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: What are the top 10 SKUs by inventory value that have no active purchase orders?
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, p.category,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS total_stock,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand * p.unit_cost) AS inventory_value
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON p.sku = i.sku
WHERE i.quantity_on_hand > 0
  AND p.sku NOT IN (
      SELECT DISTINCT poi.sku
      FROM purchase_order_items poi
      JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id = po.po_id
      WHERE po.status IN ('PENDING_APPROVAL', 'APPROVED')
  )
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, p.category
ORDER BY inventory_value DESC
OFFSET 0 ROWS FETCH NEXT 10 ROWS ONLY;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Show the order lines where discount given exceeds the allowed threshold of 15%
output:
SELECT so.so_number, c.customer_name, soi.sku, p.product_name,
       soi.list_price, soi.unit_price,
       ROUND(100.0 * (soi.list_price - soi.unit_price) / NULLIF(soi.list_price, 0), 2) AS discount_pct
FROM sales_order_items soi
JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id = so.so_id
JOIN customers c ON so.customer_id = c.customer_id
JOIN products p ON soi.sku = p.sku
WHERE soi.list_price > soi.unit_price
  AND (soi.list_price - soi.unit_price) / NULLIF(soi.list_price, 0) > 0.15
  AND so.order_date >= DATEADD(MONTH, -3, GETDATE())
ORDER BY discount_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: List all approved suppliers for each SKU as a comma-separated string
output:
SELECT sp.sku, p.product_name,
       STRING_AGG(s.supplier_name, ', ') WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY sp.unit_price ASC) AS approved_suppliers,
       COUNT(DISTINCT sp.supplier_id) AS supplier_count
FROM sourcing_products sp
JOIN suppliers s ON sp.supplier_id = s.supplier_id
JOIN products p ON sp.sku = p.sku
WHERE sp.status = 'APPROVED'
GROUP BY sp.sku, p.product_name
ORDER BY supplier_count DESC;
