       sh.weight_kg * r.standard_freight_rate AS expected_amount,
       sh.freight_cost - (sh.weight_kg * r.standard_freight_rate) AS billing_variance
FROM shipments sh
JOIN routes r ON sh.route_id=r.route_id
JOIN logistics_partners lp ON sh.logistics_partner_id=lp.partner_id
WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED'
  AND ABS(sh.freight_cost - (sh.weight_kg * r.standard_freight_rate)) > 500
  AND sh.actual_delivery_date >= DATEADD(MONTH,-3,GETDATE())
ORDER BY billing_variance DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Defect trend by batch origin — which production batches had quality failures?
output:
SELECT b.batch_number, p.sku, p.product_name, s.supplier_name,
       SUM(gri.received_quantity) AS batch_qty_received,
       SUM(qc.defective_quantity) AS defective_qty,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(qc.defective_quantity)/NULLIF(SUM(gri.received_quantity),0),2) AS defect_rate_pct
FROM batches b
JOIN products p ON b.sku=p.sku
JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON gri.sku=b.sku
JOIN quality_checks qc ON gri.grn_item_id=qc.grn_item_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON gri.po_item_id=poi.po_item_id
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE qc.result IN ('FAIL','CONDITIONAL')
  AND qc.inspection_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY b.batch_number, p.sku, p.product_name, s.supplier_name
ORDER BY defect_rate_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Forecast accuracy deterioration trend — is MAPE worsening month over month?
output:
WITH monthly_mape AS (
    SELECT df.forecast_period,
           ROUND(AVG(100.0*ABS(df.actual_quantity-df.forecasted_quantity)/NULLIF(df.actual_quantity,0)),2) AS mape
    FROM demand_forecasts df
    WHERE df.actual_quantity IS NOT NULL AND df.actual_quantity > 0
      AND df.forecast_period >= FORMAT(DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE()),'yyyy-MM')
    GROUP BY df.forecast_period
)
SELECT forecast_period, mape,
       LAG(mape) OVER (ORDER BY forecast_period) AS prev_month_mape,
       mape - LAG(mape) OVER (ORDER BY forecast_period) AS mape_change,
       IIF(mape > LAG(mape) OVER (ORDER BY forecast_period),'WORSENING','IMPROVING') AS trend
FROM monthly_mape
ORDER BY forecast_period;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Replenishment urgency dashboard — days of cover remaining per SKU versus vendor lead time
output:
WITH daily_consumption AS (
    SELECT soi.sku, SUM(soi.ordered_quantity)/90.0 AS avg_daily
    FROM sales_order_items soi
    JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id=so.so_id
    WHERE so.order_date >= DATEADD(DAY,-90,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY soi.sku
),
in_transit_qty AS (
    SELECT poi.sku, SUM(poi.ordered_quantity) AS inbound
    FROM purchase_order_items poi
    JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
    WHERE po.status IN ('APPROVED') AND po.expected_delivery_date > GETDATE()
    GROUP BY poi.sku
)
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, p.lead_time_days,
       COALESCE(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand),0) AS on_hand,
       COALESCE(itq.inbound,0) AS in_transit,
       ROUND(dc.avg_daily,2) AS daily_demand,
       ROUND((COALESCE(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand),0)+COALESCE(itq.inbound,0))/NULLIF(dc.avg_daily,0),1) AS days_cover,
       IIF((COALESCE(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand),0)+COALESCE(itq.inbound,0))/NULLIF(dc.avg_daily,0)<p.lead_time_days,'CRITICAL',
           IIF((COALESCE(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand),0)+COALESCE(itq.inbound,0))/NULLIF(dc.avg_daily,0)<p.lead_time_days*1.5,'WARNING','OK')) AS replenishment_status
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON p.sku=i.sku
JOIN daily_consumption dc ON p.sku=dc.sku
LEFT JOIN in_transit_qty itq ON p.sku=itq.sku
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, p.lead_time_days, dc.avg_daily, itq.inbound
ORDER BY days_cover ASC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Order fulfillment rate by customer segment — percentage of orders fully completed on time
output:
SELECT c.segment,
       COUNT(DISTINCT so.so_id) AS total_orders,
       SUM(CASE WHEN so.delivered_on_time=1 AND so.delivered_complete=1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS otif_orders,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN so.delivered_on_time=1 AND so.delivered_complete=1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT so.so_id),0),2) AS otif_pct
FROM customers c
JOIN sales_orders so ON c.customer_id=so.customer_id
WHERE so.status='DELIVERED'
  AND so.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY c.segment
ORDER BY otif_pct;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Procurement savings opportunity — vendor price benchmarking across all contracted SKUs
output:
WITH best_price AS (
    SELECT sku, MIN(unit_price) AS lowest_price
    FROM sourcing_products WHERE status='APPROVED' GROUP BY sku
)
SELECT sp.sku, p.product_name, s.supplier_name,
       sp.unit_price AS current_price,
       bp.lowest_price AS best_available,
       sp.unit_price - bp.lowest_price AS overpayment_per_unit,
       SUM(poi.ordered_quantity) AS annual_volume,
       (sp.unit_price - bp.lowest_price) * SUM(poi.ordered_quantity) AS potential_annual_saving
FROM sourcing_products sp
JOIN suppliers s ON sp.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
JOIN products p ON sp.sku=p.sku
JOIN best_price bp ON sp.sku=bp.sku
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON sp.sku=poi.sku
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
WHERE sp.status='APPROVED'
  AND sp.unit_price > bp.lowest_price
  AND po.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY sp.sku, p.product_name, s.supplier_name, sp.unit_price, bp.lowest_price
ORDER BY potential_annual_saving DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Transit inventory valuation — goods currently in-flight between locations
output:
SELECT sh.carrier, lp.service_type,
       COUNT(sh.shipment_id) AS shipments_in_transit,
       SUM(sh.shipment_value) AS total_transit_value,
       SUM(sh.weight_kg) AS total_weight_kg,
       AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY,GETDATE(),sh.expected_delivery_date)) AS avg_days_to_arrival
FROM shipments sh
JOIN logistics_partners lp ON sh.logistics_partner_id=lp.partner_id
WHERE sh.status='IN_TRANSIT'
GROUP BY sh.carrier, lp.service_type
ORDER BY total_transit_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Vendor compliance scorecard using weighted quality delivery and cost metrics
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.category,
       AVG(sp.quality_score) AS avg_quality,
       AVG(sp.delivery_score) AS avg_delivery,
       AVG(sp.cost_score) AS avg_cost,
       ROUND(AVG(sp.quality_score)*0.40+AVG(sp.delivery_score)*0.40+AVG(sp.cost_score)*0.20,2) AS compliance_score,
       RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY s.category ORDER BY AVG(sp.quality_score)*0.40+AVG(sp.delivery_score)*0.40+AVG(sp.cost_score)*0.20 DESC) AS rank_in_category
FROM suppliers s
JOIN supplier_performance sp ON s.supplier_id=sp.supplier_id
WHERE sp.evaluation_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_id, s.supplier_name, s.category
ORDER BY compliance_score DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Cycle count accuracy by warehouse — system quantity versus physical count variance
output:
SELECT w.warehouse_name,
       COUNT(*) AS items_counted,
       SUM(CASE WHEN ABS(i.quantity_on_hand - pc.physical_count_qty) <= 2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS accurate_count,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN ABS(i.quantity_on_hand-pc.physical_count_qty)<=2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0),2) AS accuracy_pct,
       SUM(ABS(i.quantity_on_hand-pc.physical_count_qty)*p.unit_cost) AS total_variance_value
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
JOIN warehouses w ON i.warehouse_id=w.warehouse_id
JOIN physical_counts pc ON i.sku=pc.sku AND i.warehouse_id=pc.warehouse_id
WHERE pc.count_date >= DATEADD(MONTH,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY w.warehouse_name
ORDER BY accuracy_pct;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Procurement cycle duration percentile breakdown — p25 p50 p75 p90
output:
WITH cycle_days AS (
    SELECT DATEDIFF(DAY,po.order_date,sh.actual_delivery_date) AS days
    FROM purchase_orders po
    JOIN shipments sh ON po.po_id=sh.po_id
    WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED'
      AND po.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
)
SELECT
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY days) OVER () AS p25,
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY days) OVER () AS p50_median,
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY days) OVER () AS p75,
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.90) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY days) OVER () AS p90,
    AVG(CAST(days AS FLOAT)) AS avg_days,
    COUNT(*) AS total_orders
FROM cycle_days;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Reorder alert — SKUs where available stock drops below safety buffer level
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, p.lead_time_days,
       COALESCE(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand),0) AS available_stock,
       MAX(i.safety_stock) AS safety_buffer,
       MAX(i.reorder_point) AS reorder_trigger,
       COALESCE(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand),0) - MAX(i.safety_stock) AS buffer_remaining
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON p.sku=i.sku
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, p.lead_time_days
HAVING COALESCE(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand),0) < MAX(i.safety_stock)
ORDER BY buffer_remaining ASC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Logistics partner freight claim settlement ratio and average resolution duration
output:
SELECT lp.partner_name, lp.service_type,
       COUNT(fc.claim_id) AS total_claims,
       SUM(fc.claim_amount) AS total_claimed,
       SUM(CASE WHEN fc.status='SETTLED' THEN fc.claim_amount ELSE 0 END) AS settled_amount,
       ROUND(100.0*COUNT(CASE WHEN fc.status='SETTLED' THEN 1 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0),1) AS settlement_rate_pct,
       AVG(CASE WHEN fc.resolved_date IS NOT NULL THEN DATEDIFF(DAY,fc.claim_date,fc.resolved_date) END) AS avg_resolution_days
FROM logistics_partners lp
JOIN shipments sh ON lp.partner_id=sh.logistics_partner_id
JOIN freight_claims fc ON sh.shipment_id=fc.shipment_id
GROUP BY lp.partner_name, lp.service_type
ORDER BY settlement_rate_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Inventory turnover acceleration — comparing last 90 days versus prior 90 days per SKU
output:
WITH current_period AS (
    SELECT soi.sku, SUM(soi.ordered_quantity*p.unit_cost) AS cogs
    FROM sales_order_items soi
    JOIN products p ON soi.sku=p.sku
    JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id=so.so_id
    WHERE so.order_date >= DATEADD(DAY,-90,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY soi.sku
),
prior_period AS (
    SELECT soi.sku, SUM(soi.ordered_quantity*p.unit_cost) AS cogs
    FROM sales_order_items soi
    JOIN products p ON soi.sku=p.sku
    JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id=so.so_id
    WHERE so.order_date BETWEEN DATEADD(DAY,-180,GETDATE()) AND DATEADD(DAY,-90,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY soi.sku
)
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name,
       ROUND(cp.cogs*4/NULLIF(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost),0),2) AS current_annualized_turnover,
       ROUND(pp.cogs*4/NULLIF(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost),0),2) AS prior_annualized_turnover,
       ROUND(cp.cogs*4/NULLIF(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost),0),2)
         - ROUND(pp.cogs*4/NULLIF(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost),0),2) AS turnover_acceleration
FROM current_period cp
JOIN prior_period pp ON cp.sku=pp.sku
JOIN products p ON cp.sku=p.sku
JOIN inventory i ON p.sku=i.sku
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, cp.cogs, pp.cogs
ORDER BY turnover_acceleration DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Bill of materials explosion cost rollup for all active finished goods
output:
SELECT b.parent_sku AS fg_sku,
       p_fg.product_name AS finished_good,
       COUNT(b.child_sku) AS component_count,
       SUM(b.quantity_required * p_comp.unit_cost) AS total_bom_cost,
       p_fg.unit_cost AS current_standard_cost,
       SUM(b.quantity_required * p_comp.unit_cost) - p_fg.unit_cost AS cost_variance
FROM bom b
JOIN products p_fg ON b.parent_sku=p_fg.sku
JOIN products p_comp ON b.child_sku=p_comp.sku
WHERE p_fg.product_type='FINISHED_GOODS'
GROUP BY b.parent_sku, p_fg.product_name, p_fg.unit_cost
ORDER BY ABS(cost_variance) DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Carrier on-time performance using ROLLUP for subtotals by service mode
output:
SELECT ISNULL(lp.service_type,'ALL MODES') AS service_type,
       ISNULL(sh.carrier,'SUBTOTAL') AS carrier,
       COUNT(*) AS total_deliveries,
       SUM(CASE WHEN sh.actual_delivery_date<=sh.expected_delivery_date THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS on_time,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN sh.actual_delivery_date<=sh.expected_delivery_date THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0),2) AS otd_pct
FROM shipments sh
JOIN logistics_partners lp ON sh.logistics_partner_id=lp.partner_id
WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED'
  AND sh.actual_delivery_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY ROLLUP(lp.service_type, sh.carrier)
ORDER BY service_type, otd_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Demand forecast versus actual — INTERSECT to find SKUs present in both forecast and sales data
output:
SELECT df.sku, p.product_name
FROM demand_forecasts df
JOIN products p ON df.sku=p.sku
WHERE df.forecast_period=FORMAT(GETDATE(),'yyyy-MM')
INTERSECT
SELECT soi.sku, p2.product_name
FROM sales_order_items soi
JOIN products p2 ON soi.sku=p2.sku
JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id=so.so_id
WHERE so.order_date >= DATEADD(MONTH,-1,GETDATE());

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: SKUs with active purchase orders but zero safety stock — replenishment gap risk
output:
SELECT DISTINCT poi.sku, p.product_name, p.category,
       COALESCE(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand),0) AS current_stock,
       COALESCE(MAX(i.safety_stock),0) AS safety_stock_configured
FROM purchase_order_items poi
JOIN products p ON poi.sku=p.sku
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON poi.sku=i.sku
WHERE po.status IN ('APPROVED','PENDING_APPROVAL')
  AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM inventory i2
      WHERE i2.sku=poi.sku AND ISNULL(i2.safety_stock,0)>0
  )
GROUP BY poi.sku, p.product_name, p.category
ORDER BY current_stock;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Freight cost efficiency ranking — cost per kilogram by carrier and service type
output:
SELECT lp.partner_name, lp.service_type, sh.carrier,
       COUNT(*) AS shipment_count,
       SUM(sh.freight_cost) AS total_freight,
       SUM(sh.weight_kg) AS total_weight_kg,
       ROUND(SUM(sh.freight_cost)/NULLIF(SUM(sh.weight_kg),0),2) AS cost_per_kg,
       RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY lp.service_type ORDER BY SUM(sh.freight_cost)/NULLIF(SUM(sh.weight_kg),0)) AS efficiency_rank
FROM shipments sh
JOIN logistics_partners lp ON sh.logistics_partner_id=lp.partner_id
WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED'
  AND sh.actual_delivery_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY lp.partner_name, lp.service_type, sh.carrier
HAVING COUNT(*)>=10
ORDER BY lp.service_type, efficiency_rank;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Contract renewal pipeline — upcoming expirations with utilization and residual value
output:
SELECT c.contract_number, s.supplier_name, s.category,
       c.start_date, c.end_date,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,GETDATE(),c.end_date) AS days_remaining,
       c.contract_value,
       COALESCE(SUM(poi.line_total),0) AS utilized_value,
       c.contract_value - COALESCE(SUM(poi.line_total),0) AS residual_value,
       ROUND(100.0*COALESCE(SUM(poi.line_total),0)/NULLIF(c.contract_value,0),1) AS utilization_pct
FROM contracts c
JOIN suppliers s ON c.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
LEFT JOIN purchase_orders po ON po.supplier_id=c.supplier_id
  AND po.order_date BETWEEN c.start_date AND c.end_date
  AND po.status != 'CANCELLED'
LEFT JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
WHERE c.status='ACTIVE'
  AND c.end_date BETWEEN GETDATE() AND DATEADD(DAY,120,GETDATE())
GROUP BY c.contract_number, s.supplier_name, s.category,
         c.start_date, c.end_date, c.contract_value
ORDER BY days_remaining;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Warehouse throughput efficiency — inbound and outbound volume per square unit of capacity
output:
SELECT w.warehouse_name, w.warehouse_type, w.total_capacity_units,
       SUM(CASE WHEN it.transaction_type='INBOUND'  THEN it.quantity ELSE 0 END) AS inbound_units,
       SUM(CASE WHEN it.transaction_type='OUTBOUND' THEN it.quantity ELSE 0 END) AS outbound_units,
       ROUND(SUM(it.quantity)*1.0/NULLIF(w.total_capacity_units,0),3) AS throughput_efficiency_ratio
FROM warehouses w
JOIN inventory_transactions it ON w.warehouse_id=it.warehouse_id
WHERE it.transaction_date >= DATEADD(MONTH,-3,GETDATE())
GROUP BY w.warehouse_id, w.warehouse_name, w.warehouse_type, w.total_capacity_units
ORDER BY throughput_efficiency_ratio DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Purchase order complex fulfillment — multi-level status combining PO shipment GRN and invoice
output:
SELECT po.po_number, s.supplier_name,
       po.total_amount,
       ISNULL(sh.status,'NO SHIPMENT') AS shipment_status,
       ISNULL(CAST(gr_sum.total_received AS VARCHAR),'0') AS qty_received,
       ISNULL(inv.status,'NO INVOICE') AS invoice_status,
       CASE
           WHEN inv.status='PAID' AND gr_sum.total_received>=poi_sum.total_ordered THEN 'FULLY CLOSED'
           WHEN gr_sum.total_received>=poi_sum.total_ordered THEN 'RECEIVED — AWAITING PAYMENT'
           WHEN sh.status='IN_TRANSIT' THEN 'IN TRANSIT'
           WHEN po.status='APPROVED' THEN 'AWAITING SHIPMENT'
           ELSE po.status
       END AS lifecycle_stage
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
LEFT JOIN shipments sh ON po.po_id=sh.po_id
LEFT JOIN invoices inv ON inv.supplier_id=po.supplier_id
  AND inv.invoice_date>=po.order_date
LEFT JOIN (SELECT po_id, SUM(ordered_quantity) AS total_ordered FROM purchase_order_items GROUP BY po_id) poi_sum ON po.po_id=poi_sum.po_id
LEFT JOIN (SELECT gri.grn_id, SUM(gri.received_quantity) AS total_received
           FROM goods_receipt_items gri
           JOIN purchase_order_items poi2 ON gri.po_item_id=poi2.po_item_id
           JOIN purchase_orders po2 ON poi2.po_id=po2.po_id
           GROUP BY gri.grn_id, po2.po_id) gr_sum ON gr_sum.grn_id IS NOT NULL
WHERE po.status NOT IN ('CANCELLED','CLOSED')
ORDER BY po.order_date;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Customer order backlog value and aging by fulfillment status
output:
SELECT so.fulfillment_status,
       COUNT(so.so_id) AS order_count,
       SUM(so.total_value) AS backlog_value,
       AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY,so.order_date,GETDATE())) AS avg_age_days,
       SUM(CASE WHEN so.promised_delivery_date < GETDATE() THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS overdue_orders
FROM sales_orders so
WHERE so.status NOT IN ('DELIVERED','CANCELLED','CLOSED')
GROUP BY so.fulfillment_status
ORDER BY backlog_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Stockout recurrence pattern — vendors whose supply disruption caused repeated stockouts
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT se.sku) AS skus_affected,
       COUNT(se.stockout_id) AS stockout_events,
       SUM(DATEDIFF(DAY,se.start_date,COALESCE(se.end_date,GETDATE()))) AS total_stockout_days,
       STRING_AGG(DISTINCT p.product_name,'; ') WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY p.product_name) AS affected_products
FROM stockout_events se
JOIN products p ON se.sku=p.sku
JOIN sourcing_products sp ON p.sku=sp.sku AND sp.status='APPROVED'
JOIN suppliers s ON sp.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE se.start_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name
HAVING COUNT(se.stockout_id) >= 2
ORDER BY total_stockout_days DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Invoice line dispute — EXCEPT to find PO lines with no matching invoice line
output:
SELECT poi.po_item_id, po.po_number, s.supplier_name,
       poi.sku, p.product_name, poi.ordered_quantity, poi.unit_price
FROM purchase_order_items poi
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
JOIN products p ON poi.sku=p.sku
WHERE po.status IN ('RECEIVED','CLOSED')
EXCEPT
SELECT ii.po_item_id, po2.po_number, s2.supplier_name,
       ii.sku, p2.product_name, ii.invoiced_quantity, ii.unit_price
FROM invoice_items ii
JOIN invoices inv ON ii.invoice_id=inv.invoice_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi2 ON ii.po_item_id=poi2.po_item_id
JOIN purchase_orders po2 ON poi2.po_id=po2.po_id
JOIN suppliers s2 ON inv.supplier_id=s2.supplier_id
JOIN products p2 ON ii.sku=p2.sku;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Warehouse overstock identification — inventory exceeding 180 days of forward demand
output:
WITH avg_daily AS (
    SELECT soi.sku, SUM(soi.ordered_quantity)/180.0 AS daily_rate
    FROM sales_order_items soi
    JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id=so.so_id
    WHERE so.order_date >= DATEADD(DAY,-180,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY soi.sku
)
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, w.warehouse_name,
       i.quantity_on_hand,
       ROUND(ad.daily_rate*180,0) AS six_month_demand,
       i.quantity_on_hand - ROUND(ad.daily_rate*180,0) AS overstock_qty,
       (i.quantity_on_hand - ROUND(ad.daily_rate*180,0)) * p.unit_cost AS overstock_value
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
JOIN warehouses w ON i.warehouse_id=w.warehouse_id
JOIN avg_daily ad ON i.sku=ad.sku
WHERE i.quantity_on_hand > ROUND(ad.daily_rate*180,0)
ORDER BY overstock_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Production work order completion rate using LAG to detect declining throughput trend
output:
WITH monthly_production AS (
    SELECT FORMAT(wo.end_date,'yyyy-MM') AS month,
           SUM(wo.planned_qty) AS planned,
           SUM(COALESCE(wo.actual_qty,0)) AS actual,
           ROUND(100.0*SUM(COALESCE(wo.actual_qty,0))/NULLIF(SUM(wo.planned_qty),0),2) AS attainment_pct
    FROM work_orders wo
    WHERE wo.status='COMPLETED'
      AND wo.end_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY FORMAT(wo.end_date,'yyyy-MM')
)
SELECT month, planned, actual, attainment_pct,
       LAG(attainment_pct) OVER (ORDER BY month) AS prev_month_attainment,
       attainment_pct - LAG(attainment_pct) OVER (ORDER BY month) AS attainment_change,
       IIF(attainment_pct < LAG(attainment_pct) OVER (ORDER BY month),'DECLINING','STABLE/IMPROVING') AS trend
FROM monthly_production
ORDER BY month;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Demand variability classification using standard deviation and coefficient of variation
output:
WITH monthly_demand AS (
    SELECT soi.sku,
           FORMAT(so.order_date,'yyyy-MM') AS month,
           SUM(soi.ordered_quantity) AS monthly_qty
    FROM sales_order_items soi
    JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id=so.so_id
    WHERE so.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
      AND so.status!='CANCELLED'
    GROUP BY soi.sku, FORMAT(so.order_date,'yyyy-MM')
)
SELECT md.sku, p.product_name,
       COUNT(*) AS active_months,
       ROUND(AVG(md.monthly_qty),1) AS avg_monthly_demand,
       ROUND(STDEV(md.monthly_qty),2) AS demand_stdev,
       ROUND(STDEV(md.monthly_qty)/NULLIF(AVG(md.monthly_qty),0),3) AS cv,
       CASE
           WHEN STDEV(md.monthly_qty)/NULLIF(AVG(md.monthly_qty),0) < 0.10 THEN 'X - STABLE'
           WHEN STDEV(md.monthly_qty)/NULLIF(AVG(md.monthly_qty),0) < 0.25 THEN 'Y - MODERATE'
           ELSE 'Z - VOLATILE'
       END AS xyz_class
FROM monthly_demand md
JOIN products p ON md.sku=p.sku
GROUP BY md.sku, p.product_name
HAVING COUNT(*)>=6
ORDER BY cv DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Merge vendor master — upsert supplier records from external ERP integration feed
output:
MERGE suppliers AS target
USING erp_supplier_feed AS source
ON target.supplier_name = source.supplier_name AND target.country = source.country
WHEN MATCHED THEN
    UPDATE SET
        target.contact_email  = source.contact_email,
        target.contact_phone  = source.contact_phone,
        target.payment_terms  = source.payment_terms,
        target.updated_at     = GETDATE()
WHEN NOT MATCHED BY TARGET THEN
    INSERT (supplier_name, contact_email, contact_phone, category,
            status, payment_terms, currency, country, created_at)
    VALUES (source.supplier_name, source.contact_email, source.contact_phone,
            source.category, 'ACTIVE', source.payment_terms,
            source.currency, source.country, GETDATE());

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Batch update freight claim status to SETTLED for claims resolved over 30 days ago
output:
UPDATE freight_claims
SET status = 'SETTLED',
    resolved_date = GETDATE()
WHERE status = 'IN_REVIEW'
  AND DATEDIFF(DAY, claim_date, GETDATE()) > 30
  AND claim_amount > 0;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Insert demand forecast records for next month for all active finished goods
output:
INSERT INTO demand_forecasts (sku, forecast_period, forecasted_quantity, actual_quantity)
SELECT p.sku,
       FORMAT(DATEADD(MONTH,1,GETDATE()),'yyyy-MM') AS forecast_period,
       ROUND(AVG(soi.ordered_quantity),0) AS forecasted_quantity,
       NULL AS actual_quantity
FROM products p
JOIN sales_order_items soi ON p.sku=soi.sku
JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id=so.so_id
WHERE p.product_type='FINISHED_GOODS'
  AND so.order_date >= DATEADD(MONTH,-3,GETDATE())
  AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM demand_forecasts df
      WHERE df.sku=p.sku
        AND df.forecast_period=FORMAT(DATEADD(MONTH,1,GETDATE()),'yyyy-MM')
  )
GROUP BY p.sku;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Vendor payment ageing — classify outstanding invoices into 30 60 90 day buckets
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY,inv.due_date,GETDATE()) BETWEEN 1 AND 30
                THEN inv.total_amount-COALESCE(inv.paid_amount,0) ELSE 0 END) AS overdue_1_30,
       SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY,inv.due_date,GETDATE()) BETWEEN 31 AND 60
                THEN inv.total_amount-COALESCE(inv.paid_amount,0) ELSE 0 END) AS overdue_31_60,
       SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY,inv.due_date,GETDATE()) BETWEEN 61 AND 90
                THEN inv.total_amount-COALESCE(inv.paid_amount,0) ELSE 0 END) AS overdue_61_90,
       SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY,inv.due_date,GETDATE()) > 90
                THEN inv.total_amount-COALESCE(inv.paid_amount,0) ELSE 0 END) AS overdue_90_plus
FROM invoices inv
JOIN suppliers s ON inv.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE inv.status IN ('UNPAID','PARTIALLY_PAID')
  AND inv.due_date < GETDATE()
GROUP BY s.supplier_name
ORDER BY overdue_90_plus DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Route performance summary for air freight versus sea freight comparison
output:
SELECT lp.service_type,
       COUNT(sh.shipment_id) AS total_shipments,
       ROUND(AVG(sh.freight_cost),2) AS avg_freight_cost,
       ROUND(AVG(sh.weight_kg),2) AS avg_weight_kg,
       ROUND(AVG(sh.freight_cost/NULLIF(sh.weight_kg,0)),2) AS avg_cost_per_kg,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN sh.actual_delivery_date<=sh.expected_delivery_date THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0),1) AS otd_pct,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(sh.damage_reported)/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0),2) AS damage_rate_pct
FROM shipments sh
JOIN logistics_partners lp ON sh.logistics_partner_id=lp.partner_id
WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED'
  AND lp.service_type IN ('AIR','SEA')
  AND sh.actual_delivery_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY lp.service_type;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Procurement spend distribution across ROLLUP hierarchy — category subcategory vendor level
output:
SELECT ISNULL(s.category,'GRAND TOTAL') AS category,
       ISNULL(s.supplier_name,'SUBTOTAL') AS supplier_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT po.po_id) AS po_count,
       SUM(poi.line_total) AS spend
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED'
  AND YEAR(po.order_date)=YEAR(GETDATE())
GROUP BY ROLLUP(s.category, s.supplier_name)
ORDER BY category, spend DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: STRING_AGG of batch numbers for each SKU received this week
output:
SELECT gri.sku, p.product_name,
       STRING_AGG(b.batch_number,', ') WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY b.expiry_date) AS batch_numbers,
       COUNT(DISTINCT b.batch_id) AS batch_count,
       SUM(gri.received_quantity) AS total_received
FROM goods_receipt_items gri
JOIN goods_receipts gr ON gri.grn_id=gr.grn_id
JOIN products p ON gri.sku=p.sku
LEFT JOIN batches b ON gri.sku=b.sku AND b.batch_number IS NOT NULL
WHERE gr.receipt_date >= DATEADD(DAY,-7,GETDATE())
GROUP BY gri.sku, p.product_name
ORDER BY total_received DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: STRING_AGG route list per logistics partner for service coverage summary
output:
SELECT lp.partner_name, lp.service_type,
       STRING_AGG(CONCAT(r.origin,' → ',r.destination),'; ') WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY r.origin) AS covered_routes,
       COUNT(DISTINCT r.route_id) AS route_count
FROM logistics_partners lp
JOIN shipments sh ON lp.partner_id=sh.logistics_partner_id
JOIN routes r ON sh.route_id=r.route_id
WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED'
  AND sh.actual_delivery_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY lp.partner_name, lp.service_type
ORDER BY route_count DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: UNION report combining open requisitions and open contracts for approval pipeline review
output:
SELECT 'REQUISITION' AS doc_type,
       pr.requisition_number AS reference,
       e.department AS owner,
       pr.total_estimated_value AS value,
       pr.created_date AS initiated_date,
       pr.priority AS urgency
FROM purchase_requisitions pr
JOIN employees e ON pr.created_by=e.employee_id
WHERE pr.status='PENDING_APPROVAL'
UNION ALL
SELECT 'CONTRACT_RENEWAL' AS doc_type,
       c.contract_number,
       s.supplier_name,
       c.contract_value,
       c.end_date,
       CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY,GETDATE(),c.end_date)<30 THEN 'CRITICAL' ELSE 'HIGH' END
FROM contracts c
JOIN suppliers s ON c.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE c.status='ACTIVE'
  AND c.end_date <= DATEADD(DAY,60,GETDATE())
ORDER BY initiated_date;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: CONCAT full shipment description for dispatch notification
output:
SELECT sh.shipment_id,
       CONCAT(
           'TRK:', sh.tracking_number,
           ' | ', lp.service_type,
           ' | ', sh.carrier,
           ' | ETA:', CONVERT(VARCHAR,sh.expected_delivery_date,23),
           ' | Val:', FORMAT(sh.shipment_value,'N2')
       ) AS dispatch_description
FROM shipments sh
JOIN logistics_partners lp ON sh.logistics_partner_id=lp.partner_id
WHERE sh.status='IN_TRANSIT'
ORDER BY sh.expected_delivery_date;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: EOMONTH accruals — all open POs expected to close before end of month
output:
SELECT po.po_number, s.supplier_name,
       po.expected_delivery_date,
       po.total_amount,
       po.status,
       EOMONTH(GETDATE()) AS accrual_date
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE po.expected_delivery_date <= EOMONTH(GETDATE())
  AND po.status IN ('APPROVED','SHIPPED')
ORDER BY po.expected_delivery_date;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: EOMONTH stock valuation snapshot for balance sheet reporting
output:
SELECT EOMONTH(GETDATE()) AS valuation_date,
       p.product_type,
       COUNT(DISTINCT i.sku) AS distinct_skus,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS total_units,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost) AS total_value
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
GROUP BY p.product_type
ORDER BY total_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: DATENAME weekday distribution of purchase orders — identify heavy ordering days
output:
SELECT DATENAME(WEEKDAY,po.order_date) AS day_of_week,
       DATEPART(WEEKDAY,po.order_date) AS day_num,
       COUNT(*) AS po_count,
       SUM(po.total_amount) AS total_value
FROM purchase_orders po
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED'
  AND po.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY DATENAME(WEEKDAY,po.order_date), DATEPART(WEEKDAY,po.order_date)
ORDER BY day_num;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: DATENAME month label for goods receipt volume report
output:
SELECT DATENAME(MONTH,gr.receipt_date) AS receipt_month,
       DATEPART(MONTH,gr.receipt_date) AS month_num,
       COUNT(gr.grn_id) AS grn_count,
       SUM(gri.received_quantity) AS units_received,
       COUNT(DISTINCT po.supplier_id) AS distinct_vendors
FROM goods_receipts gr
JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON gr.grn_id=gri.grn_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON gri.po_item_id=poi.po_item_id
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
WHERE YEAR(gr.receipt_date)=YEAR(GETDATE())
GROUP BY DATENAME(MONTH,gr.receipt_date), DATEPART(MONTH,gr.receipt_date)
ORDER BY month_num;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: TRY_CAST validation on invoice amounts before payment processing
output:
SELECT inv.invoice_number, s.supplier_name,
       inv.total_amount,
       TRY_CAST(inv.total_amount AS DECIMAL(18,2)) AS validated_amount,
       TRY_CAST(inv.paid_amount AS DECIMAL(18,2)) AS validated_paid,
       TRY_CAST(inv.total_amount AS DECIMAL(18,2))-COALESCE(TRY_CAST(inv.paid_amount AS DECIMAL(18,2)),0) AS outstanding_validated
FROM invoices inv
JOIN suppliers s ON inv.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE TRY_CAST(inv.total_amount AS DECIMAL(18,2)) IS NOT NULL
  AND inv.status IN ('UNPAID','PARTIALLY_PAID')
ORDER BY outstanding_validated DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: TRY_CONVERT safe conversion of forecast quantities for analytics pipeline
output:
SELECT df.sku, p.product_name, df.forecast_period,
       TRY_CONVERT(DECIMAL(18,2),df.forecasted_quantity) AS safe_forecast,
       TRY_CONVERT(DECIMAL(18,2),df.actual_quantity) AS safe_actual,
       TRY_CONVERT(DECIMAL(18,2),df.actual_quantity)
         - TRY_CONVERT(DECIMAL(18,2),df.forecasted_quantity) AS safe_variance
FROM demand_forecasts df
JOIN products p ON df.sku=p.sku
WHERE TRY_CONVERT(DECIMAL(18,2),df.forecasted_quantity) IS NOT NULL
  AND df.actual_quantity IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY ABS(TRY_CONVERT(DECIMAL(18,2),df.actual_quantity)-TRY_CONVERT(DECIMAL(18,2),df.forecasted_quantity)) DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: CROSS APPLY to retrieve the most recent supplier evaluation for each active vendor
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.category,
       latest.evaluation_date,
       latest.quality_score,
       latest.delivery_score,
       latest.cost_score,
       ROUND(latest.quality_score*0.4+latest.delivery_score*0.4+latest.cost_score*0.2,2) AS composite_score
FROM suppliers s
CROSS APPLY (
    SELECT TOP 1 sp.evaluation_date, sp.quality_score, sp.delivery_score, sp.cost_score
    FROM supplier_performance sp
    WHERE sp.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
    ORDER BY sp.evaluation_date DESC
) latest
WHERE s.status='ACTIVE'
ORDER BY composite_score;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: CROSS APPLY top freight claim per shipment for dispute prioritisation
output:
SELECT sh.tracking_number, sh.carrier, sh.shipment_value,
       top_claim.claim_number,
       top_claim.claim_amount,
       top_claim.damage_type,
       top_claim.status AS claim_status
FROM shipments sh
CROSS APPLY (
    SELECT TOP 1 fc.claim_number, fc.claim_amount, fc.damage_type, fc.status
    FROM freight_claims fc
    WHERE fc.shipment_id=sh.shipment_id
    ORDER BY fc.claim_amount DESC
) top_claim
WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED'
  AND sh.actual_delivery_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
ORDER BY top_claim.claim_amount DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: IIF to flag procurement orders by approval urgency based on total value and age
output:
SELECT po.po_number, s.supplier_name, po.order_date,
       po.total_amount,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,po.order_date,GETDATE()) AS days_pending,
       IIF(po.total_amount > 1000000 AND DATEDIFF(DAY,po.order_date,GETDATE()) > 3,
           'URGENT — HIGH VALUE OVERDUE',
           IIF(po.total_amount > 500000,'HIGH VALUE',
               IIF(DATEDIFF(DAY,po.order_date,GETDATE())>7,'OVERDUE','NORMAL'))) AS approval_priority
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE po.status='PENDING_APPROVAL'
ORDER BY po.total_amount DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: IIF batch status classification by remaining shelf life
output:
SELECT b.batch_number, p.sku, p.product_name, w.warehouse_name,
       b.expiry_date, b.quantity_remaining,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,GETDATE(),b.expiry_date) AS shelf_life_days,
       IIF(DATEDIFF(DAY,GETDATE(),b.expiry_date)<0,'EXPIRED',
           IIF(DATEDIFF(DAY,GETDATE(),b.expiry_date)<=14,'URGENT CLEARANCE',
               IIF(DATEDIFF(DAY,GETDATE(),b.expiry_date)<=30,'PRIORITY SALE','ADEQUATE'))) AS clearance_status
FROM batches b
JOIN products p ON b.sku=p.sku
JOIN warehouses w ON b.warehouse_id=w.warehouse_id
WHERE b.quantity_remaining > 0
ORDER BY shelf_life_days;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: ISNULL default substitution for missing route standard freight rates
output:
SELECT r.route_id, r.origin, r.destination, r.carrier,
       r.transit_days,
       ISNULL(r.standard_freight_rate, 0) AS freight_rate,
       CASE WHEN ISNULL(r.standard_freight_rate,0)=0 THEN 'RATE MISSING' ELSE 'RATE SET' END AS rate_status
FROM routes r
ORDER BY rate_status DESC, r.origin;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: ISNULL substitution for missing product lead times in replenishment planning
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, p.category,
       ISNULL(p.lead_time_days, 14) AS effective_lead_time_days,
       CASE WHEN p.lead_time_days IS NULL THEN 'USING DEFAULT 14 DAYS' ELSE 'CONFIGURED' END AS lead_time_source,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS current_stock
FROM products p
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON p.sku=i.sku
WHERE p.product_type != 'WIP'
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, p.category, p.lead_time_days
ORDER BY lead_time_source DESC, p.sku;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Consignment tracking — all inbound consignments currently awaiting clearance at port
output:
SELECT sh.tracking_number, sh.carrier, lp.partner_name,
       s.supplier_name, po.po_number,
       sh.expected_delivery_date,
       sh.shipment_value, sh.weight_kg,
       DATEDIFF(DAY, GETDATE(), sh.expected_delivery_date) AS days_to_clearance
FROM shipments sh
JOIN logistics_partners lp ON sh.logistics_partner_id=lp.partner_id
JOIN purchase_orders po ON sh.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE sh.status='IN_TRANSIT'
  AND lp.service_type IN ('SEA','AIR')
ORDER BY sh.expected_delivery_date;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Traceability audit — full consignment history for batch serialization recall investigation
output:
SELECT b.batch_number, p.sku, p.product_name,
       s.supplier_name, gr.grn_number, gr.receipt_date,
       w.warehouse_name, b.expiry_date,
       b.quantity_remaining AS remaining_in_stock
FROM batches b
JOIN products p ON b.sku=p.sku
JOIN warehouses w ON b.warehouse_id=w.warehouse_id
JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON gri.sku=b.sku
JOIN goods_receipts gr ON gri.grn_id=gr.grn_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON gri.po_item_id=poi.po_item_id
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE p.sku='FG-3301'
ORDER BY gr.receipt_date DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Bottleneck identification — SKUs with longest average replenishment lead time causing supply disruption
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, p.category,
       p.lead_time_days AS standard_lead_time,
       ROUND(AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY,po.order_date,sh.actual_delivery_date)),1) AS actual_avg_lead_time,
       ROUND(AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY,po.order_date,sh.actual_delivery_date))-p.lead_time_days,1) AS lead_time_overrun,
       COUNT(*) AS sample_size
FROM products p
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON p.sku=poi.sku
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN shipments sh ON po.po_id=sh.po_id
WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED'
  AND po.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, p.category, p.lead_time_days
HAVING COUNT(*)>=5
  AND AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY,po.order_date,sh.actual_delivery_date)) > p.lead_time_days*1.20
ORDER BY lead_time_overrun DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Expedite analysis — rush orders raised to counteract stockout or fulfillment bottleneck
output:
SELECT po.po_number, s.supplier_name, po.order_date,
       po.expected_delivery_date,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,po.order_date,po.expected_delivery_date) AS requested_lead_days,
       p.lead_time_days AS standard_lead_days,
       po.total_amount
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
JOIN products p ON poi.sku=p.sku
WHERE DATEDIFF(DAY,po.order_date,po.expected_delivery_date) < p.lead_time_days * 0.60
  AND po.status NOT IN ('CANCELLED')
  AND po.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY po.po_number, s.supplier_name, po.order_date, po.expected_delivery_date,
         p.lead_time_days, po.total_amount
ORDER BY requested_lead_days ASC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Obsolescence risk — inventory drawdown rate below 5% of stock per month indicating write-off risk
output:
WITH monthly_drawdown AS (
    SELECT it.sku,
           SUM(CASE WHEN it.transaction_type='OUTBOUND' THEN it.quantity ELSE 0 END)/6.0 AS avg_monthly_outbound
    FROM inventory_transactions it
    WHERE it.transaction_date >= DATEADD(MONTH,-6,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY it.sku
)
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, p.category,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS current_stock,
       COALESCE(md.avg_monthly_outbound,0) AS avg_monthly_drawdown,
       ROUND(100.0*COALESCE(md.avg_monthly_outbound,0)/NULLIF(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand),0),2) AS monthly_drawdown_pct,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand)*p.unit_cost AS obsolescence_exposure
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON p.sku=i.sku
LEFT JOIN monthly_drawdown md ON p.sku=md.sku
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, p.category, p.unit_cost, md.avg_monthly_outbound
HAVING 100.0*COALESCE(md.avg_monthly_outbound,0)/NULLIF(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand),0) < 5
ORDER BY obsolescence_exposure DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Cross-docking efficiency — consignments processed through crossdock versus standard receipt
output:
SELECT w.warehouse_name,
       COUNT(CASE WHEN sh.shipment_type='CROSS_DOCK' THEN 1 END) AS crossdock_shipments,
       COUNT(CASE WHEN sh.shipment_type='STANDARD' THEN 1 END) AS standard_shipments,
       ROUND(100.0*COUNT(CASE WHEN sh.shipment_type='CROSS_DOCK' THEN 1 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0),1) AS crossdock_pct,
       AVG(CASE WHEN sh.shipment_type='CROSS_DOCK' THEN sh.freight_cost END) AS avg_crossdock_cost,
       AVG(CASE WHEN sh.shipment_type='STANDARD' THEN sh.freight_cost END) AS avg_standard_cost
FROM shipments sh
JOIN purchase_orders po ON sh.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN warehouses w ON w.warehouse_name=sh.destination_warehouse
WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED'
  AND sh.actual_delivery_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY w.warehouse_name
ORDER BY crossdock_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Tiering analysis — classify vendors into strategic preferred and transactional tiers by spend
output:
WITH supplier_spend AS (
    SELECT po.supplier_id, SUM(poi.line_total) AS annual_spend
    FROM purchase_orders po
    JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
    WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED' AND YEAR(po.order_date)=YEAR(GETDATE())
    GROUP BY po.supplier_id
)
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.category,
       ss.annual_spend,
       CASE
           WHEN ss.annual_spend >= 5000000 THEN 'TIER 1 — STRATEGIC'
           WHEN ss.annual_spend >= 1000000 THEN 'TIER 2 — PREFERRED'
           WHEN ss.annual_spend >= 100000  THEN 'TIER 3 — APPROVED'
           ELSE 'TIER 4 — TRANSACTIONAL'
       END AS vendor_tier
FROM suppliers s
JOIN supplier_spend ss ON s.supplier_id=ss.supplier_id
WHERE s.status='ACTIVE'
ORDER BY ss.annual_spend DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Allocation summary — how much of available stock is allocated to confirmed sales orders
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS total_stock,
       COALESCE(alloc.allocated_qty,0) AS allocated_to_orders,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) - COALESCE(alloc.allocated_qty,0) AS unallocated_stock,
       ROUND(100.0*COALESCE(alloc.allocated_qty,0)/NULLIF(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand),0),1) AS allocation_pct
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON p.sku=i.sku
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT soi.sku,
           SUM(soi.ordered_quantity-COALESCE(soi.fulfilled_quantity,0)) AS allocated_qty
    FROM sales_order_items soi
    JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id=so.so_id
    WHERE so.status IN ('CONFIRMED','OPEN')
    GROUP BY soi.sku
) alloc ON p.sku=alloc.sku
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, alloc.allocated_qty
ORDER BY allocation_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Palletisation efficiency — average shipment weight and pallet utilisation by carrier
output:
SELECT sh.carrier, lp.service_type,
       COUNT(*) AS shipments,
       AVG(sh.weight_kg) AS avg_weight_kg,
       SUM(sh.weight_kg) AS total_weight_kg,
       ROUND(AVG(sh.freight_cost/NULLIF(sh.weight_kg,0)),2) AS avg_rate_per_kg
FROM shipments sh
JOIN logistics_partners lp ON sh.logistics_partner_id=lp.partner_id
WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED'
  AND sh.actual_delivery_date >= DATEADD(MONTH,-6,GETDATE())
GROUP BY sh.carrier, lp.service_type
ORDER BY total_weight_kg DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Deviation report — purchase orders where actual receipt quantity deviated from ordered by over 10 percent
output:
SELECT po.po_number, s.supplier_name,
       poi.sku, p.product_name,
       poi.ordered_quantity,
       SUM(gri.received_quantity) AS received_quantity,
       poi.ordered_quantity - SUM(gri.received_quantity) AS qty_deviation,
       ROUND(100.0*ABS(poi.ordered_quantity-SUM(gri.received_quantity))/NULLIF(poi.ordered_quantity,0),2) AS deviation_pct
FROM purchase_order_items poi
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
JOIN products p ON poi.sku=p.sku
JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON poi.po_item_id=gri.po_item_id
WHERE po.status IN ('RECEIVED','CLOSED')
  AND po.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY po.po_number, s.supplier_name, poi.sku, p.product_name, poi.ordered_quantity
HAVING 100.0*ABS(poi.ordered_quantity-SUM(gri.received_quantity))/NULLIF(poi.ordered_quantity,0) > 10
ORDER BY deviation_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Calibration of safety stock parameters against actual stockout incidence
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name,
       MAX(i.safety_stock) AS configured_safety_stock,
       COUNT(se.stockout_id) AS stockout_incidents_last_year,
       SUM(DATEDIFF(DAY,se.start_date,COALESCE(se.end_date,GETDATE()))) AS total_stockout_days,
       IIF(COUNT(se.stockout_id) > 2,'SAFETY STOCK INADEQUATE — RECALIBRATE',
           IIF(COUNT(se.stockout_id) > 0,'MARGINAL — REVIEW','ADEQUATE')) AS calibration_verdict
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON p.sku=i.sku
LEFT JOIN stockout_events se ON p.sku=se.sku
  AND se.start_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name
HAVING MAX(i.safety_stock) IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY stockout_incidents_last_year DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Optimisation benchmark — EOQ versus actual average order quantity variance summary
output:
SELECT eoq.sku, p.product_name,
       eoq.economic_order_qty AS recommended_eoq,
       ROUND(AVG(poi.ordered_quantity),1) AS actual_avg_order_qty,
       ROUND(AVG(poi.ordered_quantity)-eoq.economic_order_qty,1) AS quantity_deviation,
       ROUND(100.0*ABS(AVG(poi.ordered_quantity)-eoq.economic_order_qty)/NULLIF(eoq.economic_order_qty,0),2) AS deviation_pct,
       ROUND((AVG(poi.ordered_quantity)-eoq.economic_order_qty)*p.unit_cost*eoq.holding_cost_rate,2) AS excess_holding_cost
FROM eoq_parameters eoq
JOIN products p ON eoq.sku=p.sku
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON eoq.sku=poi.sku
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
WHERE po.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
  AND po.status!='CANCELLED'
GROUP BY eoq.sku, p.product_name, eoq.economic_order_qty, p.unit_cost, eoq.holding_cost_rate
ORDER BY excess_holding_cost DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Segmentation of customer base by purchase recency frequency and monetary value
output:
WITH rfm AS (
    SELECT so.customer_id,
           MAX(so.order_date) AS last_order,
           COUNT(DISTINCT so.so_id) AS frequency,
           SUM(so.total_value) AS monetary
    FROM sales_orders so
    WHERE so.status!='CANCELLED'
      AND so.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY so.customer_id
)
SELECT c.customer_name, c.segment,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,rfm.last_order,GETDATE()) AS recency_days,
       rfm.frequency,
       rfm.monetary,
       CASE
           WHEN rfm.monetary>=500000 AND rfm.frequency>=10 THEN 'CHAMPION'
           WHEN rfm.monetary>=200000 THEN 'HIGH VALUE'
           WHEN rfm.frequency>=5 THEN 'LOYAL'
           ELSE 'STANDARD'
       END AS rfm_segment
FROM rfm
JOIN customers c ON rfm.customer_id=c.customer_id
ORDER BY rfm.monetary DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Mitigation tracking — open corrective actions raised after quality inspection failures
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, p.sku, p.product_name,
       COUNT(qc.qc_id) AS inspection_failures,
       MAX(qc.inspection_date) AS last_failure_date,
       ROUND(AVG(qc.defective_quantity),1) AS avg_defective_qty
FROM quality_checks qc
JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON qc.grn_item_id=gri.grn_item_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON gri.po_item_id=poi.po_item_id
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
JOIN products p ON gri.sku=p.sku
WHERE qc.result='FAIL'
  AND qc.inspection_date >= DATEADD(MONTH,-6,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, p.sku, p.product_name
HAVING COUNT(qc.qc_id) >= 2
ORDER BY inspection_failures DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Consolidation opportunity — vendor invoices eligible for payment run grouping this week
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.payment_terms,
       COUNT(inv.invoice_id) AS eligible_invoices,
       SUM(inv.total_amount-COALESCE(inv.paid_amount,0)) AS total_payable,
       MIN(inv.due_date) AS earliest_due,
       MAX(inv.due_date) AS latest_due
FROM invoices inv
JOIN suppliers s ON inv.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE inv.status IN ('UNPAID','PARTIALLY_PAID')
  AND inv.due_date BETWEEN GETDATE() AND DATEADD(DAY,7,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, s.payment_terms
HAVING COUNT(inv.invoice_id) > 1
ORDER BY total_payable DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Prioritisation matrix — open sales orders ranked by revenue at risk and days overdue
output:
SELECT so.so_number, c.customer_name,
       so.total_value,
       so.promised_delivery_date,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,so.promised_delivery_date,GETDATE()) AS days_overdue,
       so.fulfillment_status,
       RANK() OVER (ORDER BY so.total_value*DATEDIFF(DAY,so.promised_delivery_date,GETDATE()) DESC) AS urgency_rank
FROM sales_orders so
JOIN customers c ON so.customer_id=c.customer_id
WHERE so.status NOT IN ('DELIVERED','CANCELLED','CLOSED')
  AND so.promised_delivery_date < GETDATE()
ORDER BY urgency_rank;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Digitisation readiness — suppliers with no electronic invoicing in the past 12 months
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.category, s.contact_email,
       COUNT(po.po_id) AS total_pos,
       SUM(po.total_amount) AS total_spend,
       COUNT(inv.invoice_id) AS electronic_invoices
FROM suppliers s
JOIN purchase_orders po ON s.supplier_id=po.supplier_id
LEFT JOIN invoices inv ON s.supplier_id=inv.supplier_id
  AND inv.invoice_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
  AND inv.invoice_source='ELECTRONIC'
WHERE po.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
  AND po.status!='CANCELLED'
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, s.category, s.contact_email
HAVING COUNT(inv.invoice_id)=0
ORDER BY total_spend DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Accreditation status — suppliers whose certification expiry falls within 60 days
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.category,
       c.contract_number,
       c.end_date AS certification_expiry,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,GETDATE(),c.end_date) AS days_to_expiry,
       SUM(po.total_amount) AS last_year_spend
FROM suppliers s
JOIN contracts c ON s.supplier_id=c.supplier_id
LEFT JOIN purchase_orders po ON s.supplier_id=po.supplier_id
  AND po.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
  AND po.status!='CANCELLED'
WHERE c.status='ACTIVE'
  AND c.end_date BETWEEN GETDATE() AND DATEADD(DAY,60,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, s.category, c.contract_number, c.end_date
ORDER BY days_to_expiry;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Escalation trigger — purchase requisitions with HIGH or CRITICAL priority unapproved beyond SLA
output:
SELECT pr.requisition_number, e.employee_name, e.department,
       pr.priority, pr.created_date,
       pr.total_estimated_value,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,pr.created_date,GETDATE()) AS days_unapproved,
       CASE WHEN pr.priority='CRITICAL' AND DATEDIFF(DAY,pr.created_date,GETDATE())>1 THEN 'BREACH'
            WHEN pr.priority='HIGH'     AND DATEDIFF(DAY,pr.created_date,GETDATE())>3 THEN 'BREACH'
            ELSE 'WITHIN SLA' END AS sla_status
FROM purchase_requisitions pr
JOIN employees e ON pr.created_by=e.employee_id
WHERE pr.status='PENDING_APPROVAL'
  AND pr.priority IN ('CRITICAL','HIGH')
  AND (
      (pr.priority='CRITICAL' AND DATEDIFF(DAY,pr.created_date,GETDATE())>1)
   OR (pr.priority='HIGH'     AND DATEDIFF(DAY,pr.created_date,GETDATE())>3)
  )
ORDER BY CASE pr.priority WHEN 'CRITICAL' THEN 1 ELSE 2 END, pr.created_date;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Disposition report — items in quarantine or conditional inspection status awaiting final disposition
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, s.supplier_name,
       gr.grn_number, gr.receipt_date,
       gri.received_quantity,
       qc.defective_quantity,
       qc.result AS inspection_disposition,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,gr.receipt_date,GETDATE()) AS days_in_quarantine
FROM quality_checks qc
JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON qc.grn_item_id=gri.grn_item_id
JOIN goods_receipts gr ON gri.grn_id=gr.grn_id
JOIN products p ON gri.sku=p.sku
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON gri.po_item_id=poi.po_item_id
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE qc.result='CONDITIONAL'
ORDER BY days_in_quarantine DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Parametric demand modelling — calculate safety stock using standard deviation of demand and lead time
output:
WITH demand_stats AS (
    SELECT soi.sku,
           AVG(monthly_qty.qty) AS avg_demand,
           STDEV(monthly_qty.qty) AS demand_stdev
    FROM sales_order_items soi
    JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id=so.so_id
    JOIN (
        SELECT soi2.sku, FORMAT(so2.order_date,'yyyy-MM') AS ym, SUM(soi2.ordered_quantity) AS qty
        FROM sales_order_items soi2
        JOIN sales_orders so2 ON soi2.so_id=so2.so_id
        WHERE so2.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
        GROUP BY soi2.sku, FORMAT(so2.order_date,'yyyy-MM')
    ) monthly_qty ON soi.sku=monthly_qty.sku
    GROUP BY soi.sku
)
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name,
       ds.avg_demand,
       ds.demand_stdev,
       p.lead_time_days,
       ROUND(1.645 * ds.demand_stdev * SQRT(p.lead_time_days/30.0),0) AS recommended_safety_stock_95pct,
       MAX(i.safety_stock) AS current_safety_stock
FROM demand_stats ds
JOIN products p ON ds.sku=p.sku
JOIN inventory i ON p.sku=i.sku
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, p.lead_time_days, ds.avg_demand, ds.demand_stdev
ORDER BY recommended_safety_stock_95pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Incoterm breakdown — which delivery terms are most common across our procurement contracts?
output:
SELECT po.delivery_terms AS incoterm,
       COUNT(DISTINCT po.po_id) AS po_count,
       SUM(poi.line_total) AS total_spend,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(poi.line_total)/SUM(SUM(poi.line_total)) OVER (),2) AS spend_pct
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED'
  AND po.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY po.delivery_terms
ORDER BY total_spend DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Accrual preparation — uninvoiced goods receipts for month-end financial close
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, po.po_number,
       gri.sku, p.product_name,
       gri.received_quantity,
       poi.unit_price,
       gri.received_quantity * poi.unit_price AS accrual_amount,
       gr.receipt_date,
       EOMONTH(GETDATE()) AS accrual_period
FROM goods_receipt_items gri
JOIN goods_receipts gr ON gri.grn_id=gr.grn_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON gri.po_item_id=poi.po_item_id
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
JOIN products p ON gri.sku=p.sku
WHERE gr.receipt_date BETWEEN DATEADD(DAY,1-DAY(GETDATE()),CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE))
                          AND EOMONTH(GETDATE())
  AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM invoice_items ii WHERE ii.po_item_id=gri.po_item_id
  )
ORDER BY accrual_amount DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Remediation tracking — quality failures with no corrective action raised within 30 days
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, p.sku, p.product_name,
       qc.inspection_date,
       qc.defective_quantity,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,qc.inspection_date,GETDATE()) AS days_since_failure,
       gr.grn_number
FROM quality_checks qc
JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON qc.grn_item_id=gri.grn_item_id
JOIN goods_receipts gr ON gri.grn_id=gr.grn_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON gri.po_item_id=poi.po_item_id
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
JOIN products p ON gri.sku=p.sku
WHERE qc.result='FAIL'
  AND DATEDIFF(DAY,qc.inspection_date,GETDATE()) > 30
  AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM corrective_actions ca
      WHERE ca.supplier_id=po.supplier_id AND ca.sku=gri.sku
        AND ca.raised_date >= qc.inspection_date
  )
ORDER BY days_since_failure DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Strategic alignment check — vendor spend versus approved contract coverage
output:
WITH contracted AS (
    SELECT po.supplier_id, SUM(poi.line_total) AS contracted_spend
    FROM purchase_orders po
    JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
    JOIN contracts c ON po.supplier_id=c.supplier_id
      AND po.order_date BETWEEN c.start_date AND c.end_date
      AND c.status='ACTIVE'
    WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED'
      AND po.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY po.supplier_id
),
total AS (
    SELECT po.supplier_id, SUM(poi.line_total) AS total_spend
    FROM purchase_orders po
    JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
    WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED'
      AND po.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY po.supplier_id
)
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       t.total_spend,
       COALESCE(c.contracted_spend,0) AS contracted_spend,
       ROUND(100.0*COALESCE(c.contracted_spend,0)/NULLIF(t.total_spend,0),1) AS alignment_pct
FROM total t
JOIN suppliers s ON t.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
LEFT JOIN contracted c ON t.supplier_id=c.supplier_id
ORDER BY alignment_pct ASC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Inventory write-off candidates — zero demand items with impairment risk above threshold
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, p.category,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS qty_at_risk,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand * p.unit_cost) AS impairment_exposure,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,MAX(it.transaction_date),GETDATE()) AS days_no_movement
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON p.sku=i.sku
LEFT JOIN inventory_transactions it ON p.sku=it.sku
  AND it.transaction_type='OUTBOUND'
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, p.category, p.unit_cost
HAVING SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) > 0
  AND (MAX(it.transaction_date) IS NULL
       OR DATEDIFF(DAY,MAX(it.transaction_date),GETDATE()) > 180)
  AND SUM(i.quantity_on_hand * p.unit_cost) > 50000
ORDER BY impairment_exposure DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Shrinkage analysis — unexplained inventory variance between receipt and current stock
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, w.warehouse_name,
       SUM(CASE WHEN it.transaction_type='INBOUND'  THEN it.quantity ELSE 0 END) AS total_received,
       SUM(CASE WHEN it.transaction_type='OUTBOUND' THEN it.quantity ELSE 0 END) AS total_issued,
       i.quantity_on_hand AS current_stock,
       SUM(CASE WHEN it.transaction_type='INBOUND' THEN it.quantity ELSE 0 END)
         - SUM(CASE WHEN it.transaction_type='OUTBOUND' THEN it.quantity ELSE 0 END)
         - i.quantity_on_hand AS unexplained_shrinkage,
       (SUM(CASE WHEN it.transaction_type='INBOUND' THEN it.quantity ELSE 0 END)
         - SUM(CASE WHEN it.transaction_type='OUTBOUND' THEN it.quantity ELSE 0 END)
         - i.quantity_on_hand) * p.unit_cost AS shrinkage_value
FROM inventory_transactions it
JOIN products p ON it.sku=p.sku
JOIN warehouses w ON it.warehouse_id=w.warehouse_id
JOIN inventory i ON p.sku=i.sku AND it.warehouse_id=i.warehouse_id
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, w.warehouse_name, i.quantity_on_hand, p.unit_cost
HAVING ABS(SUM(CASE WHEN it.transaction_type='INBOUND' THEN it.quantity ELSE 0 END)
         - SUM(CASE WHEN it.transaction_type='OUTBOUND' THEN it.quantity ELSE 0 END)
         - i.quantity_on_hand) > 10
ORDER BY shrinkage_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Supplier attrition risk — active vendors with declining order frequency over 3 consecutive quarters
output:
WITH quarterly AS (
    SELECT po.supplier_id,
           DATEPART(QUARTER,po.order_date) AS qtr,
           YEAR(po.order_date) AS yr,
           COUNT(*) AS po_count
    FROM purchase_orders po
    WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED'
      AND po.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY po.supplier_id, DATEPART(QUARTER,po.order_date), YEAR(po.order_date)
),
with_lag AS (
    SELECT supplier_id, qtr, yr, po_count,
           LAG(po_count,1) OVER (PARTITION BY supplier_id ORDER BY yr,qtr) AS q1,
           LAG(po_count,2) OVER (PARTITION BY supplier_id ORDER BY yr,qtr) AS q2
    FROM quarterly
)
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       wl.yr, wl.qtr,
       wl.q2 AS two_qtrs_ago,
       wl.q1 AS prev_qtr,
       wl.po_count AS current_qtr
FROM with_lag wl
JOIN suppliers s ON wl.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE wl.po_count < wl.q1 AND wl.q1 < wl.q2
ORDER BY wl.po_count ASC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Backlog clearance rate — what percentage of backlogged orders get fulfilled each week?
output:
WITH weekly_backlog AS (
    SELECT DATEPART(WEEK,so.order_date) AS week_num,
           YEAR(so.order_date) AS yr,
           COUNT(*) AS total_orders,
           SUM(CASE WHEN so.status IN ('SHIPPED','DELIVERED') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS fulfilled
    FROM sales_orders so
    WHERE so.status NOT IN ('CANCELLED')
      AND so.order_date >= DATEADD(MONTH,-3,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY DATEPART(WEEK,so.order_date), YEAR(so.order_date)
)
SELECT yr, week_num,
       total_orders,
       fulfilled,
       total_orders - fulfilled AS backlog_remaining,
       ROUND(100.0*fulfilled/NULLIF(total_orders,0),1) AS clearance_rate_pct
FROM weekly_backlog
ORDER BY yr, week_num;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Throughput bottleneck analysis — which warehouse zones have lowest outbound processing speed?
output:
SELECT w.warehouse_name, w.warehouse_type,
       COUNT(DISTINCT CAST(it.transaction_date AS DATE)) AS working_days,
       SUM(CASE WHEN it.transaction_type='OUTBOUND' THEN it.quantity ELSE 0 END) AS outbound_units,
       ROUND(1.0*SUM(CASE WHEN it.transaction_type='OUTBOUND' THEN it.quantity ELSE 0 END)
             /NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT CAST(it.transaction_date AS DATE)),0),1) AS units_per_day,
       SUM(CASE WHEN it.transaction_type='INBOUND' THEN it.quantity ELSE 0 END)
         -SUM(CASE WHEN it.transaction_type='OUTBOUND' THEN it.quantity ELSE 0 END) AS net_accumulation
FROM inventory_transactions it
JOIN warehouses w ON it.warehouse_id=w.warehouse_id
WHERE it.transaction_date >= DATEADD(MONTH,-3,GETDATE())
GROUP BY w.warehouse_name, w.warehouse_type
ORDER BY units_per_day ASC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Write-off exposure by product category this financial year
output:
SELECT p.category,
       COUNT(DISTINCT p.sku) AS skus_at_risk,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand * p.unit_cost) AS potential_writeoff_value
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON p.sku=i.sku
WHERE i.quantity_on_hand > 0
  AND p.sku NOT IN (
      SELECT DISTINCT soi.sku FROM sales_order_items soi
      JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id=so.so_id
      WHERE so.order_date >= DATEADD(MONTH,-6,GETDATE())
  )
GROUP BY p.category
ORDER BY potential_writeoff_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Turnover ratio benchmarking — which categories underperform the 8x annual target?
output:
SELECT p.category,
       ROUND(SUM(soi.ordered_quantity*p.unit_cost)/NULLIF(AVG(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost),0),2) AS actual_turnover,
       8 AS benchmark_turnover,
       ROUND(SUM(soi.ordered_quantity*p.unit_cost)/NULLIF(AVG(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost),0)-8,2) AS vs_benchmark,
       IIF(SUM(soi.ordered_quantity*p.unit_cost)/NULLIF(AVG(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost),0)<8,
           'BELOW BENCHMARK','MEETING BENCHMARK') AS benchmark_status
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON p.sku=i.sku
JOIN sales_order_items soi ON p.sku=soi.sku
JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id=so.so_id
WHERE so.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
  AND so.status!='CANCELLED'
GROUP BY p.category
ORDER BY actual_turnover ASC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Incoterm-based freight cost analysis — CIF versus FOB cost comparison
output:
SELECT po.delivery_terms AS incoterm,
       COUNT(sh.shipment_id) AS shipments,
       SUM(sh.freight_cost) AS total_freight,
       AVG(sh.freight_cost) AS avg_freight,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(sh.freight_cost)/NULLIF(SUM(sh.shipment_value),0),2) AS freight_as_pct_value
FROM shipments sh
JOIN purchase_orders po ON sh.po_id=po.po_id
WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED'
  AND po.delivery_terms IS NOT NULL
  AND sh.actual_delivery_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY po.delivery_terms
ORDER BY avg_freight DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Scorecard summary — top 10 and bottom 10 vendors by composite performance rating
output:
WITH scores AS (
    SELECT s.supplier_id, s.supplier_name,
           ROUND(AVG(sp.quality_score)*0.4+AVG(sp.delivery_score)*0.4+AVG(sp.cost_score)*0.2,2) AS composite_score
    FROM suppliers s
    JOIN supplier_performance sp ON s.supplier_id=sp.supplier_id
    WHERE sp.evaluation_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY s.supplier_id, s.supplier_name
)
SELECT supplier_name, composite_score, 'TOP 10' AS band
FROM scores
ORDER BY composite_score DESC
OFFSET 0 ROWS FETCH NEXT 10 ROWS ONLY
UNION ALL
SELECT supplier_name, composite_score, 'BOTTOM 10'
FROM scores
ORDER BY composite_score ASC
OFFSET 0 ROWS FETCH NEXT 10 ROWS ONLY;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Reconciliation of purchase ledger — GRN value versus invoice value by vendor this month
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       SUM(gri.received_quantity * poi.unit_price) AS grn_value,
       SUM(ii.invoiced_quantity * ii.unit_price) AS invoiced_value,
       SUM(gri.received_quantity * poi.unit_price)
         - SUM(ii.invoiced_quantity * ii.unit_price) AS reconciliation_gap
FROM suppliers s
JOIN invoices inv ON s.supplier_id=inv.supplier_id
JOIN invoice_items ii ON inv.invoice_id=ii.invoice_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON ii.po_item_id=poi.po_item_id
JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON poi.po_item_id=gri.po_item_id
WHERE inv.invoice_date >= DATEADD(DAY,1-DAY(GETDATE()),CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE))
GROUP BY s.supplier_name
HAVING ABS(SUM(gri.received_quantity*poi.unit_price)-SUM(ii.invoiced_quantity*ii.unit_price)) > 100
ORDER BY ABS(reconciliation_gap) DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Procurement consolidation ROI — estimated savings from single-sourcing multiple small orders
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       FORMAT(po.order_date,'yyyy-MM') AS month,
       COUNT(*) AS po_count,
       SUM(po.total_amount) AS total_spend,
       AVG(po.total_amount) AS avg_po_size,
       ROUND(COUNT(*)*250.0,2) AS estimated_processing_cost,
       ROUND((COUNT(*)-1)*250.0,2) AS potential_consolidation_saving
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED'
  AND po.order_date >= DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, FORMAT(po.order_date,'yyyy-MM')
HAVING COUNT(*)>=3 AND AVG(po.total_amount)<100000
ORDER BY potential_consolidation_saving DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Arbitrage identification — same SKU priced more than 20% cheaper from an alternative approved vendor
output:
WITH current_prices AS (
    SELECT sp.sku, sp.supplier_id, sp.unit_price
    FROM sourcing_products sp
    WHERE sp.status='APPROVED'
),
best_price AS (
    SELECT sku, MIN(unit_price) AS lowest_price, MIN(supplier_id) AS cheapest_supplier_id
    FROM current_prices GROUP BY sku
)
SELECT cp.sku, p.product_name,
       s_curr.supplier_name AS current_vendor,
       cp.unit_price AS current_price,
       s_best.supplier_name AS cheaper_vendor,
       bp.lowest_price AS best_price,
       ROUND(100.0*(cp.unit_price-bp.lowest_price)/NULLIF(bp.lowest_price,0),1) AS price_premium_pct,
       cp.unit_price - bp.lowest_price AS saving_per_unit
FROM current_prices cp
JOIN best_price bp ON cp.sku=bp.sku
JOIN products p ON cp.sku=p.sku
JOIN suppliers s_curr ON cp.supplier_id=s_curr.supplier_id
JOIN suppliers s_best ON bp.cheapest_supplier_id=s_best.supplier_id
WHERE cp.unit_price > bp.lowest_price * 1.20
  AND cp.supplier_id != bp.cheapest_supplier_id
ORDER BY saving_per_unit DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Drawdown rate monitoring — how quickly is safety stock being depleted versus replenishment arrival
output:
WITH consumption AS (
    SELECT it.sku,
           SUM(CASE WHEN it.transaction_type='OUTBOUND' THEN it.quantity ELSE 0 END)/30.0 AS daily_drawdown
    FROM inventory_transactions it
    WHERE it.transaction_date >= DATEADD(DAY,-30,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY it.sku
),
arrival AS (
    SELECT poi.sku, SUM(poi.ordered_quantity) AS inbound_expected
    FROM purchase_order_items poi
    JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
    WHERE po.status='APPROVED'
      AND po.expected_delivery_date <= DATEADD(DAY,14,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY poi.sku
)
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS current_stock,
       MAX(i.safety_stock) AS safety_buffer,
       ROUND(c.daily_drawdown,2) AS daily_drawdown,
       COALESCE(a.inbound_expected,0) AS expected_replenishment,
       ROUND((SUM(i.quantity_on_hand)-MAX(i.safety_stock))/NULLIF(c.daily_drawdown,0),1) AS days_above_safety
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON p.sku=i.sku
JOIN consumption c ON p.sku=c.sku
LEFT JOIN arrival a ON p.sku=a.sku
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, c.daily_drawdown, a.inbound_expected
ORDER BY days_above_safety ASC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Serialization traceability — trace all batch lots shipped to customer orders in the last quarter
output:
SELECT c.customer_name, so.so_number, soi.sku,
       p.product_name, b.batch_number, b.expiry_date,
       soi.fulfilled_quantity AS qty_shipped
FROM sales_order_items soi
JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id=so.so_id
JOIN customers c ON so.customer_id=c.customer_id
JOIN products p ON soi.sku=p.sku
JOIN batches b ON soi.sku=b.sku
WHERE so.status IN ('SHIPPED','DELIVERED')
  AND so.ship_date >= DATEADD(QUARTER,-1,DATEADD(QUARTER,DATEDIFF(QUARTER,0,GETDATE()),0))
  AND so.ship_date <  DATEADD(QUARTER,DATEDIFF(QUARTER,0,GETDATE()),0)
ORDER BY so.ship_date DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Utilisation efficiency ranking of all storage facilities
output:
SELECT w.warehouse_name, w.warehouse_type,
       w.total_capacity_units,
       COALESCE(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand),0) AS used_units,
       ROUND(100.0*COALESCE(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand),0)/NULLIF(w.total_capacity_units,0),1) AS utilisation_pct,
       RANK() OVER (ORDER BY 100.0*COALESCE(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand),0)/NULLIF(w.total_capacity_units,0) DESC) AS utilisation_rank
FROM warehouses w
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON w.warehouse_id=i.warehouse_id
GROUP BY w.warehouse_id,w.warehouse_name,w.warehouse_type,w.total_capacity_units
ORDER BY utilisation_rank;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Impairment exposure by warehouse — slow-moving stock value over 6 months
output:
SELECT w.warehouse_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT i.sku) AS stale_sku_count,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand * p.unit_cost) AS impairment_exposure_value
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
JOIN warehouses w ON i.warehouse_id=w.warehouse_id
WHERE i.quantity_on_hand > 0
  AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM inventory_transactions it
      WHERE it.sku=i.sku AND it.warehouse_id=i.warehouse_id
        AND it.transaction_date >= DATEADD(MONTH,-6,GETDATE())
        AND it.transaction_type='OUTBOUND'
  )
