JOIN suppliers s ON inv.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE inv.status IN ('UNPAID','PARTIALLY_PAID')
  AND inv.due_date <= DATEADD(DAY,7,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, s.payment_terms
ORDER BY consolidated_payable DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Digitised vendor onboarding — new suppliers registered this quarter
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.category, s.country, s.created_at, s.status
FROM suppliers s
WHERE s.created_at >= DATEADD(QUARTER,DATEDIFF(QUARTER,0,GETDATE()),0)
ORDER BY s.created_at DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Streamlined goods receipt throughput — GRNs per day this month
output:
SELECT CAST(gr.receipt_date AS DATE) AS receipt_day,
       COUNT(gr.grn_id) AS grns_processed,
       SUM(gri.received_quantity) AS units_received
FROM goods_receipts gr
JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON gr.grn_id=gri.grn_id
WHERE gr.receipt_date >= DATEADD(DAY,1-DAY(GETDATE()),CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE))
GROUP BY CAST(gr.receipt_date AS DATE)
ORDER BY receipt_day;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Segmented customer revenue bands for account planning
output:
SELECT c.customer_name,
       SUM(so.total_value) AS annual_revenue,
       CASE WHEN SUM(so.total_value)>=1000000 THEN 'ENTERPRISE'
            WHEN SUM(so.total_value)>=250000  THEN 'MID-MARKET'
            ELSE 'SMB' END AS segmented_band
FROM customers c
JOIN sales_orders so ON c.customer_id=so.customer_id
WHERE so.status!='CANCELLED' AND YEAR(so.order_date)=YEAR(GETDATE())
GROUP BY c.customer_name
ORDER BY annual_revenue DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Benchmarked vendor lead time versus category average
output:
WITH cat_avg AS (
    SELECT s2.category,
           AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY,po2.order_date,sh2.actual_delivery_date)) AS avg_lead
    FROM suppliers s2
    JOIN purchase_orders po2 ON s2.supplier_id=po2.supplier_id
    JOIN shipments sh2 ON po2.po_id=sh2.po_id
    WHERE sh2.status='DELIVERED' AND po2.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY s2.category
)
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.category,
       ROUND(AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY,po.order_date,sh.actual_delivery_date)),1) AS vendor_lead,
       ca.avg_lead AS category_avg,
       ROUND(AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY,po.order_date,sh.actual_delivery_date))-ca.avg_lead,1) AS vs_benchmark
FROM suppliers s
JOIN purchase_orders po ON s.supplier_id=po.supplier_id
JOIN shipments sh ON po.po_id=sh.po_id
JOIN cat_avg ca ON s.category=ca.category
WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED' AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, s.category, ca.avg_lead
ORDER BY vs_benchmark DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Decentralised warehouse stock by regional hub
output:
SELECT w.warehouse_type, w.warehouse_name, w.location,
       COUNT(DISTINCT i.sku) AS distinct_skus,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost) AS stock_value
FROM warehouses w
JOIN inventory i ON w.warehouse_id=i.warehouse_id
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
GROUP BY w.warehouse_type, w.warehouse_name, w.location
ORDER BY stock_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Repatriated customer returns processed back to sellable inventory
output:
SELECT p.category,
       SUM(roi.quantity) AS units_repatriated,
       SUM(roi.quantity*roi.unit_price) AS repatriated_value
FROM return_order_items roi
JOIN return_orders ro ON roi.return_id=ro.return_id
JOIN products p ON roi.sku=p.sku
WHERE ro.return_date>=DATEADD(MONTH,-3,GETDATE())
  AND ro.disposition_status='RESTOCKED'
GROUP BY p.category
ORDER BY repatriated_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Overhang inventory beyond 6 months forward demand
output:
WITH daily_rate AS (
    SELECT soi.sku, SUM(soi.ordered_quantity)/180.0 AS rate
    FROM sales_order_items soi
    JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id=so.so_id
    WHERE so.order_date>=DATEADD(DAY,-180,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY soi.sku
)
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS stock,
       ROUND(dr.rate*180,0) AS six_month_demand,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand)-ROUND(dr.rate*180,0) AS overhang_qty
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
JOIN daily_rate dr ON p.sku=dr.sku
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, dr.rate
HAVING SUM(i.quantity_on_hand)>ROUND(dr.rate*180,0)
ORDER BY overhang_qty DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Undershoot months — demand fell below 50% of forecast
output:
SELECT df.sku, p.product_name, df.forecast_period,
       df.forecasted_quantity, df.actual_quantity,
       ROUND(100.0*df.actual_quantity/NULLIF(df.forecasted_quantity,0),1) AS attainment_pct
FROM demand_forecasts df
JOIN products p ON df.sku=p.sku
WHERE df.actual_quantity IS NOT NULL
  AND df.actual_quantity < df.forecasted_quantity*0.50
ORDER BY attainment_pct ASC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Production overrun — work orders exceeding planned output quantity
output:
SELECT wo.wo_number, p.product_name,
       wo.planned_qty, wo.actual_qty,
       wo.actual_qty-wo.planned_qty AS overrun_qty,
       ROUND(100.0*(wo.actual_qty-wo.planned_qty)/NULLIF(wo.planned_qty,0),1) AS overrun_pct
FROM work_orders wo
JOIN products p ON wo.product_sku=p.sku
WHERE wo.status='COMPLETED' AND wo.actual_qty>wo.planned_qty
  AND wo.end_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
ORDER BY overrun_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Decommissioned products with residual stock still on hand
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, p.category,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS residual_stock,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost) AS residual_value
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON p.sku=i.sku
WHERE p.status='DISCONTINUED' AND i.quantity_on_hand>0
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, p.category, p.unit_cost
ORDER BY residual_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Dunnage cost percentage of total freight spend by carrier
output:
SELECT sh.carrier,
       COUNT(*) AS shipments,
       AVG(sh.dunnage_cost) AS avg_dunnage,
       AVG(sh.freight_cost) AS avg_freight,
       ROUND(100.0*AVG(sh.dunnage_cost)/NULLIF(AVG(sh.freight_cost),0),2) AS dunnage_pct
FROM shipments sh
WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED'
  AND sh.dunnage_cost IS NOT NULL
  AND sh.actual_delivery_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY sh.carrier
ORDER BY dunnage_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Demand forecast accuracy last 3 months by product
output:
SELECT df.sku, p.product_name,
       COUNT(*) AS periods,
       ROUND(AVG(100.0*ABS(df.actual_quantity-df.forecasted_quantity)/NULLIF(df.actual_quantity,0)),2) AS mape_pct
FROM demand_forecasts df
JOIN products p ON df.sku=p.sku
WHERE df.actual_quantity IS NOT NULL
  AND df.forecast_period>=FORMAT(DATEADD(MONTH,-3,GETDATE()),'yyyy-MM')
GROUP BY df.sku, p.product_name
ORDER BY mape_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Active stockout events currently in progress
output:
SELECT se.sku, p.product_name, p.category,
       w.warehouse_name, se.start_date,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,se.start_date,GETDATE()) AS days_in_stockout
FROM stockout_events se
JOIN products p ON se.sku=p.sku
JOIN warehouses w ON se.warehouse_id=w.warehouse_id
WHERE se.end_date IS NULL
ORDER BY days_in_stockout DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Supplier quality inspection results pass conditional fail breakdown
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       SUM(CASE WHEN qc.result='PASS' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS passed,
       SUM(CASE WHEN qc.result='CONDITIONAL' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS conditional,
       SUM(CASE WHEN qc.result='FAIL' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS failed,
       COUNT(*) AS total
FROM quality_checks qc
JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON qc.grn_item_id=gri.grn_item_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON gri.po_item_id=poi.po_item_id
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE qc.inspection_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name
ORDER BY failed DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Goods received but not yet invoiced — accrual for month-end
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT gr.grn_id) AS uninvoiced_grns,
       SUM(gri.received_quantity*poi.unit_price) AS accrual_amount
FROM goods_receipts gr
JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON gr.grn_id=gri.grn_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON gri.po_item_id=poi.po_item_id
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM invoice_items ii WHERE ii.po_item_id=gri.po_item_id
)
GROUP BY s.supplier_name
ORDER BY accrual_amount DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Contract coverage gap — vendor spend with no active contract
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.category,
       SUM(poi.line_total) AS uncontracted_spend
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED' AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
  AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM contracts c
      WHERE c.supplier_id=po.supplier_id AND c.status='ACTIVE'
        AND po.order_date BETWEEN c.start_date AND c.end_date
  )
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, s.category
ORDER BY uncontracted_spend DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Procurement savings — actual spend versus standard cost by category
output:
SELECT s.category,
       SUM(poi.line_total) AS actual_spend,
       SUM(poi.ordered_quantity*p.unit_cost) AS budget_at_standard,
       SUM(poi.ordered_quantity*p.unit_cost)-SUM(poi.line_total) AS savings
FROM purchase_order_items poi
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
JOIN products p ON poi.sku=p.sku
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED' AND YEAR(po.order_date)=YEAR(GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.category
ORDER BY savings DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Risk heat map — high-spend vendors with poor on-time delivery
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       SUM(poi.line_total) AS annual_spend,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN sh.actual_delivery_date>sh.expected_delivery_date THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(sh.shipment_id),0),1) AS late_pct
FROM suppliers s
JOIN purchase_orders po ON s.supplier_id=po.supplier_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
JOIN shipments sh ON po.po_id=sh.po_id
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED' AND sh.status='DELIVERED'
  AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name
HAVING SUM(poi.line_total)>500000 AND
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN sh.actual_delivery_date>sh.expected_delivery_date THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(sh.shipment_id),0),1)>20
ORDER BY annual_spend DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Monthly demand seasonality index versus annual baseline
output:
SELECT FORMAT(so.order_date,'yyyy-MM') AS month,
       SUM(soi.ordered_quantity) AS monthly_qty,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(soi.ordered_quantity)/NULLIF(AVG(SUM(soi.ordered_quantity)) OVER (),0),1) AS seasonality_index
FROM sales_order_items soi
JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id=so.so_id
WHERE so.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE()) AND so.status!='CANCELLED'
GROUP BY FORMAT(so.order_date,'yyyy-MM')
ORDER BY month;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Non-standard vendor payment terms in active procurement
output:
SELECT s.payment_terms,
       COUNT(DISTINCT s.supplier_id) AS vendors,
       SUM(poi.line_total) AS annual_spend
FROM suppliers s
JOIN purchase_orders po ON s.supplier_id=po.supplier_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED' AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
  AND s.payment_terms NOT IN ('NET30','NET60','NET90')
GROUP BY s.payment_terms
ORDER BY annual_spend DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Procurement — open POs expected delivery within 7 days
output:
SELECT po.po_number, s.supplier_name,
       po.expected_delivery_date,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,GETDATE(),po.expected_delivery_date) AS days_to_delivery,
       po.total_amount
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE po.status IN ('APPROVED','SHIPPED')
  AND po.expected_delivery_date BETWEEN GETDATE() AND DATEADD(DAY,7,GETDATE())
ORDER BY po.expected_delivery_date;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Quality defect rate benchmarked against 2% industry standard
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(qc.defective_quantity)/NULLIF(SUM(gri.received_quantity),0),3) AS defect_rate_pct,
       IIF(ROUND(100.0*SUM(qc.defective_quantity)/NULLIF(SUM(gri.received_quantity),0),3)>2.0,
           'ABOVE BENCHMARK','WITHIN BENCHMARK') AS status
FROM quality_checks qc
JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON qc.grn_item_id=gri.grn_item_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON gri.po_item_id=poi.po_item_id
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE qc.inspection_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name
ORDER BY defect_rate_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Streamlined single-location inventory — SKUs held at one warehouse only
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT i.warehouse_id) AS locations,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS total_stock
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
WHERE i.quantity_on_hand>0
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT i.warehouse_id)=1
ORDER BY total_stock DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Vendor quote comparison across sourcing alternatives
output:
SELECT sp.sku, p.product_name, s.supplier_name,
       sp.unit_price,
       RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY sp.sku ORDER BY sp.unit_price ASC) AS price_rank,
       MIN(sp.unit_price) OVER (PARTITION BY sp.sku) AS lowest_quote,
       sp.unit_price-MIN(sp.unit_price) OVER (PARTITION BY sp.sku) AS premium
FROM sourcing_products sp
JOIN products p ON sp.sku=p.sku
JOIN suppliers s ON sp.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE sp.status='APPROVED'
ORDER BY sp.sku, price_rank;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Freight cost overrun versus budgeted route rate
output:
SELECT sh.tracking_number, sh.carrier,
       r.standard_freight_rate*sh.weight_kg AS budget,
       sh.freight_cost AS actual,
       ROUND(100.0*(sh.freight_cost-r.standard_freight_rate*sh.weight_kg)/NULLIF(r.standard_freight_rate*sh.weight_kg,0),1) AS overrun_pct
FROM shipments sh
JOIN routes r ON sh.route_id=r.route_id
WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED'
  AND sh.freight_cost>r.standard_freight_rate*sh.weight_kg*1.25
  AND sh.actual_delivery_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
ORDER BY overrun_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Contracts expiring next quarter
output:
SELECT c.contract_number, s.supplier_name,
       c.end_date, c.contract_value,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,GETDATE(),c.end_date) AS days_left
FROM contracts c
JOIN suppliers s ON c.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE c.status='ACTIVE'
  AND c.end_date BETWEEN GETDATE() AND DATEADD(QUARTER,1,GETDATE())
ORDER BY c.end_date;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Dead stock not moved in 90 days by category
output:
SELECT p.category,
       COUNT(DISTINCT p.sku) AS dead_skus,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost) AS dead_stock_value
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
WHERE i.quantity_on_hand>0
  AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM inventory_transactions it
      WHERE it.sku=p.sku AND it.transaction_type='OUTBOUND'
        AND it.transaction_date>=DATEADD(DAY,-90,GETDATE())
  )
GROUP BY p.category
ORDER BY dead_stock_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Active suppliers with no performance evaluation in 6 months
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.category,
       MAX(sp.evaluation_date) AS last_evaluated,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,MAX(sp.evaluation_date),GETDATE()) AS days_since_eval
FROM suppliers s
LEFT JOIN supplier_performance sp ON s.supplier_id=sp.supplier_id
WHERE s.status='ACTIVE'
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, s.category
HAVING MAX(sp.evaluation_date)<DATEADD(MONTH,-6,GETDATE()) OR MAX(sp.evaluation_date) IS NULL
ORDER BY days_since_eval DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Forecast undershoot — products 40% below forecast this year
output:
SELECT df.sku, p.product_name, df.forecast_period,
       df.forecasted_quantity, df.actual_quantity,
       ROUND(100.0*(df.forecasted_quantity-df.actual_quantity)/NULLIF(df.forecasted_quantity,0),1) AS undershoot_pct
FROM demand_forecasts df
JOIN products p ON df.sku=p.sku
WHERE df.actual_quantity IS NOT NULL
  AND df.actual_quantity<df.forecasted_quantity*0.60
ORDER BY undershoot_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Replenishment order frequency per SKU over last year
output:
SELECT poi.sku, p.product_name, s.supplier_name,
       COUNT(po.po_id) AS replenishment_cycles,
       ROUND(365.0/NULLIF(COUNT(po.po_id),0),1) AS avg_days_between_orders
FROM purchase_order_items poi
JOIN products p ON poi.sku=p.sku
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED' AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY poi.sku, p.product_name, s.supplier_name
HAVING COUNT(po.po_id)>=2
ORDER BY replenishment_cycles DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Inspection pass rate by product category this year
output:
SELECT p.category,
       COUNT(*) AS inspections,
       SUM(CASE WHEN qc.result='PASS' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS passed,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN qc.result='PASS' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0),1) AS pass_rate_pct
FROM quality_checks qc
JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON qc.grn_item_id=gri.grn_item_id
JOIN products p ON gri.sku=p.sku
WHERE YEAR(qc.inspection_date)=YEAR(GETDATE())
GROUP BY p.category
ORDER BY pass_rate_pct;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Warehouse inbound volume by day of week for staffing
output:
SELECT DATENAME(WEEKDAY,gr.receipt_date) AS weekday,
       DATEPART(WEEKDAY,gr.receipt_date) AS day_num,
       COUNT(gr.grn_id) AS grns,
       SUM(gri.received_quantity) AS units_in
FROM goods_receipts gr
JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON gr.grn_id=gri.grn_id
WHERE gr.receipt_date>=DATEADD(MONTH,-3,GETDATE())
GROUP BY DATENAME(WEEKDAY,gr.receipt_date), DATEPART(WEEKDAY,gr.receipt_date)
ORDER BY day_num;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Top 10 suppliers by purchase order count this year
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.category,
       COUNT(po.po_id) AS po_count,
       SUM(po.total_amount) AS total_value
FROM suppliers s
JOIN purchase_orders po ON s.supplier_id=po.supplier_id
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED' AND YEAR(po.order_date)=YEAR(GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, s.category
ORDER BY po_count DESC
OFFSET 0 ROWS FETCH NEXT 10 ROWS ONLY;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Monthly invoice outstanding amount and payment gap
output:
SELECT FORMAT(inv.due_date,'yyyy-MM') AS due_month,
       COUNT(inv.invoice_id) AS invoices_due,
       SUM(inv.total_amount-COALESCE(inv.paid_amount,0)) AS outstanding
FROM invoices inv
WHERE inv.due_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY FORMAT(inv.due_date,'yyyy-MM')
ORDER BY due_month;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Requisition to PO cycle time by department
output:
SELECT e.department,
       COUNT(*) AS converted,
       AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY,pr.created_date,po.order_date)) AS avg_days
FROM purchase_requisitions pr
JOIN purchase_orders po ON po.requisition_id=pr.requisition_id
JOIN employees e ON pr.created_by=e.employee_id
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED' AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY e.department
ORDER BY avg_days DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Single-source SKUs with no alternative approved vendor
output:
SELECT sp.sku, p.product_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT sp.supplier_id) AS vendor_count,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS current_stock
FROM sourcing_products sp
JOIN products p ON sp.sku=p.sku
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON sp.sku=i.sku
WHERE sp.status='APPROVED'
GROUP BY sp.sku, p.product_name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT sp.supplier_id)=1
ORDER BY current_stock ASC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Next month forecast versus last month actuals by category
output:
SELECT p.category,
       SUM(CASE WHEN df.forecast_period=FORMAT(GETDATE(),'yyyy-MM') THEN df.forecasted_quantity ELSE 0 END) AS current_forecast,
       SUM(CASE WHEN df.forecast_period=FORMAT(DATEADD(MONTH,-1,GETDATE()),'yyyy-MM') THEN df.actual_quantity ELSE 0 END) AS last_month_actual
FROM demand_forecasts df
JOIN products p ON df.sku=p.sku
GROUP BY p.category
ORDER BY current_forecast DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: New suppliers onboarded in last 90 days
output:
SELECT supplier_name, category, country, status, payment_terms, created_at
FROM suppliers
WHERE created_at>=DATEADD(DAY,-90,GETDATE())
ORDER BY created_at DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Approved sourcing products with no purchase activity last 6 months
output:
SELECT sp.sku, p.product_name, s.supplier_name, sp.unit_price
FROM sourcing_products sp
JOIN products p ON sp.sku=p.sku
JOIN suppliers s ON sp.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE sp.status='APPROVED'
  AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM purchase_order_items poi
      JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
      WHERE poi.sku=sp.sku AND po.supplier_id=sp.supplier_id
        AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(MONTH,-6,GETDATE())
  )
ORDER BY s.supplier_name, sp.sku;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Corrective actions open beyond SLA deadline
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, ca.sku,
       ca.raised_date,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,ca.raised_date,GETDATE()) AS days_open,
       ca.sla_days
FROM corrective_actions ca
JOIN suppliers s ON ca.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE ca.resolved_date IS NULL
  AND DATEDIFF(DAY,ca.raised_date,GETDATE())>ca.sla_days
ORDER BY days_open DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Accounts payable ageing buckets for overdue invoices
output:
SELECT
    SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY,inv.due_date,GETDATE()) BETWEEN 1 AND 30 THEN inv.total_amount-COALESCE(inv.paid_amount,0) ELSE 0 END) AS overdue_1_30,
    SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY,inv.due_date,GETDATE()) BETWEEN 31 AND 60 THEN inv.total_amount-COALESCE(inv.paid_amount,0) ELSE 0 END) AS overdue_31_60,
    SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY,inv.due_date,GETDATE()) BETWEEN 61 AND 90 THEN inv.total_amount-COALESCE(inv.paid_amount,0) ELSE 0 END) AS overdue_61_90,
    SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY,inv.due_date,GETDATE())>90 THEN inv.total_amount-COALESCE(inv.paid_amount,0) ELSE 0 END) AS overdue_90_plus
FROM invoices inv
WHERE inv.status IN ('UNPAID','PARTIALLY_PAID') AND inv.due_date<GETDATE();

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Shipment value currently at risk in transit
output:
SELECT sh.carrier,
       COUNT(*) AS shipments,
       SUM(sh.shipment_value) AS value_in_transit
FROM shipments sh
WHERE sh.status='IN_TRANSIT'
GROUP BY sh.carrier
ORDER BY value_in_transit DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Customer order complete versus partial fulfillment rate this quarter
output:
SELECT c.segment,
       COUNT(*) AS orders,
       SUM(CASE WHEN so.delivered_complete=1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS complete,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN so.delivered_complete=1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0),1) AS complete_pct
FROM sales_orders so
JOIN customers c ON so.customer_id=c.customer_id
WHERE so.status='DELIVERED'
  AND so.order_date>=DATEADD(QUARTER,DATEDIFF(QUARTER,0,GETDATE()),0)
GROUP BY c.segment
ORDER BY complete_pct;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Work orders completed on schedule versus late this year
output:
SELECT CASE WHEN GETDATE()<=wo.end_date THEN 'ON SCHEDULE' ELSE 'LATE' END AS status,
       COUNT(*) AS orders,
       SUM(wo.planned_qty) AS planned,
       SUM(COALESCE(wo.actual_qty,0)) AS produced
FROM work_orders wo
WHERE wo.status='COMPLETED' AND wo.end_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY CASE WHEN GETDATE()<=wo.end_date THEN 'ON SCHEDULE' ELSE 'LATE' END;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: POs pending approval by value band
output:
SELECT CASE WHEN po.total_amount>=1000000 THEN '>1M'
            WHEN po.total_amount>=500000  THEN '500K-1M'
            WHEN po.total_amount>=100000  THEN '100K-500K'
            ELSE '<100K' END AS value_band,
       COUNT(*) AS po_count,
       SUM(po.total_amount) AS total_value
FROM purchase_orders po
WHERE po.status='PENDING_APPROVAL'
GROUP BY CASE WHEN po.total_amount>=1000000 THEN '>1M'
              WHEN po.total_amount>=500000  THEN '500K-1M'
              WHEN po.total_amount>=100000  THEN '100K-500K'
              ELSE '<100K' END
ORDER BY total_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Freight claims unresolved beyond 60 days
output:
SELECT fc.claim_number, sh.carrier,
       fc.claim_amount, fc.claim_date,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,fc.claim_date,GETDATE()) AS days_open
FROM freight_claims fc
JOIN shipments sh ON fc.shipment_id=sh.shipment_id
WHERE fc.status IN ('OPEN','IN_REVIEW')
  AND DATEDIFF(DAY,fc.claim_date,GETDATE())>60
ORDER BY days_open DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Vendors inactive for over 12 months with no new POs
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.category, s.status
FROM suppliers s
WHERE s.status='ACTIVE'
  AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM purchase_orders po
      WHERE po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
        AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
        AND po.status!='CANCELLED'
  )
ORDER BY s.supplier_name;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Top 10 slow-moving SKUs by holding cost
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS qty,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost) AS holding_value,
       ROUND(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost)*0.20,2) AS annual_holding_cost
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, p.unit_cost
ORDER BY holding_value DESC
OFFSET 0 ROWS FETCH NEXT 10 ROWS ONLY;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Supplier price variance for same SKU across multiple purchase orders
output:
SELECT poi.sku, p.product_name, s.supplier_name,
       MIN(poi.unit_price) AS min_price,
       MAX(poi.unit_price) AS max_price,
       ROUND(STDEV(poi.unit_price),2) AS price_stdev
FROM purchase_order_items poi
JOIN products p ON poi.sku=p.sku
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED' AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY poi.sku, p.product_name, s.supplier_name
HAVING COUNT(*)>=3 AND STDEV(poi.unit_price)>0
ORDER BY price_stdev DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Stale sourcing prices not updated in 6 months
output:
SELECT sp.sku, p.product_name, s.supplier_name,
       sp.unit_price, sp.updated_at,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,sp.updated_at,GETDATE()) AS days_stale
FROM sourcing_products sp
JOIN products p ON sp.sku=p.sku
JOIN suppliers s ON sp.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE sp.status='APPROVED'
  AND DATEDIFF(DAY,sp.updated_at,GETDATE())>180
ORDER BY days_stale DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: SKUs with no demand forecast configured for next month
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name
FROM products p
WHERE p.product_type='FINISHED_GOODS'
  AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM demand_forecasts df
      WHERE df.sku=p.sku
        AND df.forecast_period=FORMAT(DATEADD(MONTH,1,GETDATE()),'yyyy-MM')
  )
ORDER BY p.product_name;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Warehouses with no physical count completed this month
output:
SELECT w.warehouse_name,
       MAX(pc.count_date) AS last_count,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,MAX(pc.count_date),GETDATE()) AS days_since_count
FROM warehouses w
LEFT JOIN physical_counts pc ON w.warehouse_id=pc.warehouse_id
GROUP BY w.warehouse_name
HAVING MAX(pc.count_date)<DATEADD(DAY,1-DAY(GETDATE()),CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE))
    OR MAX(pc.count_date) IS NULL
ORDER BY days_since_count DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: PO versus invoice spend reconciliation by vendor
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       SUM(poi.line_total) AS po_spend,
       COALESCE(SUM(ii.invoiced_quantity*ii.unit_price),0) AS invoiced,
       SUM(poi.line_total)-COALESCE(SUM(ii.invoiced_quantity*ii.unit_price),0) AS gap
FROM purchase_order_items poi
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
LEFT JOIN invoice_items ii ON poi.po_item_id=ii.po_item_id
WHERE po.status IN ('RECEIVED','CLOSED') AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name
ORDER BY ABS(gap) DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Orders shipped with pending customer return
output:
SELECT so.so_number, c.customer_name, so.total_value,
       ro.return_date, ro.reason_code
FROM sales_orders so
JOIN customers c ON so.customer_id=c.customer_id
JOIN return_orders ro ON so.so_id=ro.so_id
WHERE so.status='DELIVERED' AND ro.status='PENDING'
  AND so.ship_date>=DATEADD(MONTH,-3,GETDATE())
ORDER BY ro.return_date;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Kitting requirements — component shortfalls for next week production run
output:
SELECT b.parent_sku, b.child_sku, p.product_name AS component,
       b.quantity_required * wo.planned_qty AS required_qty,
       COALESCE(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand),0) AS available_qty,
       GREATEST(0, b.quantity_required*wo.planned_qty - COALESCE(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand),0)) AS shortfall
FROM work_orders wo
JOIN bom b ON wo.product_sku=b.parent_sku
JOIN products p ON b.child_sku=p.sku
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON b.child_sku=i.sku
WHERE wo.status IN ('PLANNED','IN_PROGRESS')
  AND wo.start_date <= DATEADD(DAY,7,GETDATE())
GROUP BY b.parent_sku, b.child_sku, p.product_name, b.quantity_required, wo.planned_qty
HAVING GREATEST(0, b.quantity_required*wo.planned_qty-COALESCE(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand),0))>0
ORDER BY shortfall DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Unitization efficiency — average carton fill rate per shipment
output:
SELECT sh.carrier, lp.service_type,
       COUNT(*) AS shipments,
       AVG(sh.weight_kg) AS avg_weight_kg,
       AVG(sh.carton_count) AS avg_cartons,
       ROUND(AVG(sh.weight_kg/NULLIF(sh.carton_count,0)),2) AS kg_per_carton
FROM shipments sh
JOIN logistics_partners lp ON sh.logistics_partner_id=lp.partner_id
WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED'
  AND sh.actual_delivery_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
  AND sh.carton_count IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY sh.carrier, lp.service_type
ORDER BY kg_per_carton DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Decoupling point analysis — where inventory buffers break the supply chain
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, p.category,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS buffer_stock,
       MAX(i.safety_stock) AS safety_stock,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(i.quantity_on_hand)/NULLIF(MAX(i.safety_stock),0),1) AS buffer_ratio_pct
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
WHERE i.quantity_on_hand > MAX(i.safety_stock) OVER (PARTITION BY i.sku)
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, p.category
ORDER BY buffer_ratio_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Demand volatility — standard deviation of weekly sales for each SKU
output:
SELECT soi.sku, p.product_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT DATEPART(WEEK,so.order_date)) AS weeks,
       ROUND(AVG(weekly.qty),1) AS avg_weekly_demand,
       ROUND(STDEV(weekly.qty),2) AS demand_volatility,
       ROUND(STDEV(weekly.qty)/NULLIF(AVG(weekly.qty),0),3) AS cv
FROM sales_order_items soi
JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id=so.so_id
JOIN products p ON soi.sku=p.sku
JOIN (
    SELECT soi2.sku,
           DATEPART(WEEK,so2.order_date) AS wk,
           SUM(soi2.ordered_quantity) AS qty
    FROM sales_order_items soi2
    JOIN sales_orders so2 ON soi2.so_id=so2.so_id
    WHERE so2.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY soi2.sku, DATEPART(WEEK,so2.order_date)
) weekly ON soi.sku=weekly.sku
WHERE so.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY soi.sku, p.product_name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT DATEPART(WEEK,so.order_date))>=4
ORDER BY demand_volatility DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Granularity drill-down — warehouse stock by bin location and SKU
output:
SELECT w.warehouse_name, p.sku, p.product_name,
       i.bin_location, i.quantity_on_hand
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
JOIN warehouses w ON i.warehouse_id=w.warehouse_id
WHERE i.quantity_on_hand>0
  AND i.bin_location IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY w.warehouse_name, i.bin_location;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Procurement cadence analysis — order frequency per supplier per month
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       FORMAT(po.order_date,'yyyy-MM') AS month,
       COUNT(po.po_id) AS po_count,
       SUM(po.total_amount) AS monthly_spend
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED'
  AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, FORMAT(po.order_date,'yyyy-MM')
ORDER BY s.supplier_name, month;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Proactive stockout prevention — SKUs approaching reorder point within 5 days cover
output:
WITH daily_out AS (
    SELECT sku, SUM(quantity)/30.0 AS daily_rate
    FROM inventory_transactions
    WHERE transaction_type='OUTBOUND'
      AND transaction_date>=DATEADD(DAY,-30,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY sku
)
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS stock,
       ROUND(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand)/NULLIF(d.daily_rate,0),1) AS days_cover
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON p.sku=i.sku
JOIN daily_out d ON p.sku=d.sku
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, d.daily_rate
HAVING ROUND(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand)/NULLIF(d.daily_rate,0),1) <= 5
ORDER BY days_cover ASC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Collaborative forecasting accuracy — vendor forecast versus actual GRN quantity
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, p.sku, p.product_name,
       SUM(vf.forecasted_qty) AS vendor_forecast,
       SUM(gri.received_quantity) AS actual_grn,
       ROUND(100.0*ABS(SUM(vf.forecasted_qty)-SUM(gri.received_quantity))/NULLIF(SUM(vf.forecasted_qty),0),2) AS forecast_error_pct
FROM vendor_forecasts vf
JOIN suppliers s ON vf.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
JOIN products p ON vf.sku=p.sku
JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON vf.sku=gri.sku
JOIN goods_receipts gr ON gri.grn_id=gr.grn_id
WHERE gr.receipt_date>=DATEADD(MONTH,-3,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, p.sku, p.product_name
HAVING SUM(vf.forecasted_qty)>0
ORDER BY forecast_error_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Synchronized replenishment — purchase orders aligned to production schedule start dates
output:
SELECT wo.wo_number, p_fg.product_name AS finished_good,
       b.child_sku AS component, p_comp.product_name,
       wo.start_date AS production_start,
       po.expected_delivery_date AS component_arrival,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,po.expected_delivery_date,wo.start_date) AS buffer_days
FROM work_orders wo
JOIN bom b ON wo.product_sku=b.parent_sku
JOIN products p_fg ON wo.product_sku=p_fg.sku
JOIN products p_comp ON b.child_sku=p_comp.sku
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON b.child_sku=poi.sku
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
WHERE wo.status='PLANNED'
  AND po.status IN ('APPROVED','SHIPPED')
  AND wo.start_date<=DATEADD(DAY,30,GETDATE())
ORDER BY buffer_days;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Stratification of vendors by reliability and spend tier
output:
WITH spend AS (
    SELECT po.supplier_id, SUM(poi.line_total) AS annual_spend
    FROM purchase_orders po JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
    WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED' AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY po.supplier_id
),
reliability AS (
    SELECT po.supplier_id,
           ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN sh.actual_delivery_date<=sh.expected_delivery_date THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0),1) AS otd
    FROM purchase_orders po JOIN shipments sh ON po.po_id=sh.po_id
    WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED' AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY po.supplier_id
)
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       sp.annual_spend,
       r.otd,
       CASE WHEN sp.annual_spend>=1000000 AND r.otd>=90 THEN 'STRATEGIC'
            WHEN sp.annual_spend>=500000 THEN 'PREFERRED'
            ELSE 'TRANSACTIONAL' END AS stratification_tier
FROM suppliers s
JOIN spend sp ON s.supplier_id=sp.supplier_id
JOIN reliability r ON s.supplier_id=r.supplier_id
ORDER BY sp.annual_spend DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Periodicity of stockout events — recurring versus one-time disruptions by SKU
output:
SELECT se.sku, p.product_name,
       COUNT(*) AS stockout_count,
       MIN(se.start_date) AS first_stockout,
       MAX(se.start_date) AS last_stockout,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,MIN(se.start_date),MAX(se.start_date)) AS span_days,
       CASE WHEN COUNT(*)>=3 THEN 'RECURRING' WHEN COUNT(*)=2 THEN 'REPEAT' ELSE 'ONE-TIME' END AS periodicity_class
FROM stockout_events se
JOIN products p ON se.sku=p.sku
WHERE se.start_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY se.sku, p.product_name
ORDER BY stockout_count DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Quantify total procurement value committed but not yet delivered
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.category,
       COUNT(po.po_id) AS open_pos,
       SUM(po.total_amount) AS committed_value,
       SUM(CASE WHEN sh.status='IN_TRANSIT' THEN sh.shipment_value ELSE 0 END) AS in_transit_value
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
LEFT JOIN shipments sh ON po.po_id=sh.po_id
WHERE po.status IN ('APPROVED','SHIPPED')
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, s.category
ORDER BY committed_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Rationalize sourcing — vendors supplying fewer than 3 SKUs for consolidation review
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.category,
       COUNT(DISTINCT sp.sku) AS approved_skus,
       SUM(poi.line_total) AS annual_spend
FROM sourcing_products sp
JOIN suppliers s ON sp.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
LEFT JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON sp.sku=poi.sku
LEFT JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
  AND po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
  AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
  AND po.status!='CANCELLED'
WHERE sp.status='APPROVED'
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, s.category
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT sp.sku)<3
ORDER BY annual_spend DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Anticipatory procurement — POs raised ahead of seasonal demand peaks
output:
SELECT FORMAT(po.order_date,'yyyy-MM') AS order_month,
       SUM(poi.ordered_quantity) AS qty_ordered,
       SUM(poi.line_total) AS spend,
       LAG(SUM(poi.line_total)) OVER (ORDER BY FORMAT(po.order_date,'yyyy-MM')) AS prev_month_spend,
       ROUND(100.0*(SUM(poi.line_total)-LAG(SUM(poi.line_total)) OVER (ORDER BY FORMAT(po.order_date,'yyyy-MM')))
             /NULLIF(LAG(SUM(poi.line_total)) OVER (ORDER BY FORMAT(po.order_date,'yyyy-MM')),0),1) AS mom_pct
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED' AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY FORMAT(po.order_date,'yyyy-MM')
ORDER BY order_month;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Bucketing supplier payments into weekly tranches for cash flow planning
output:
SELECT DATEPART(WEEK,inv.due_date) AS payment_week,
       MIN(inv.due_date) AS week_start,
       COUNT(inv.invoice_id) AS invoices,
       SUM(inv.total_amount-COALESCE(inv.paid_amount,0)) AS weekly_payment_bucket
FROM invoices inv
WHERE inv.status IN ('UNPAID','PARTIALLY_PAID')
  AND inv.due_date BETWEEN GETDATE() AND DATEADD(MONTH,3,GETDATE())
GROUP BY DATEPART(WEEK,inv.due_date)
ORDER BY DATEPART(WEEK,inv.due_date);

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Assess which vendors have let contracts lapse but we continue purchasing from them
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.category,
       MAX(c.end_date) AS contract_expired_on,
       COUNT(po.po_id) AS pos_after_expiry,
       SUM(poi.line_total) AS spend_after_expiry
FROM suppliers s
JOIN purchase_orders po ON s.supplier_id=po.supplier_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
LEFT JOIN contracts c ON s.supplier_id=c.supplier_id
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED'
  AND c.status='EXPIRED'
  AND po.order_date > c.end_date
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, s.category
ORDER BY spend_after_expiry DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Examine expired-contract vendors still receiving purchase orders
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       MAX(c.end_date) AS contract_expiry,
       SUM(po.total_amount) AS uncontracted_spend
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
LEFT JOIN contracts c ON s.supplier_id=c.supplier_id AND c.status='EXPIRED'
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED'
  AND po.order_date > COALESCE(c.end_date, DATEADD(YEAR,-10,GETDATE()))
  AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM contracts c2
      WHERE c2.supplier_id=s.supplier_id AND c2.status='ACTIVE'
  )
GROUP BY s.supplier_name
ORDER BY uncontracted_spend DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Determine the lead time variability for each supplier using standard deviation
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       COUNT(*) AS deliveries,
       ROUND(AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY,po.order_date,sh.actual_delivery_date)),1) AS avg_lead_days,
       ROUND(STDEV(DATEDIFF(DAY,po.order_date,sh.actual_delivery_date)),2) AS lead_stdev,
       ROUND(STDEV(DATEDIFF(DAY,po.order_date,sh.actual_delivery_date))/
             NULLIF(AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY,po.order_date,sh.actual_delivery_date)),0),3) AS variability_cv
FROM suppliers s
JOIN purchase_orders po ON s.supplier_id=po.supplier_id
JOIN shipments sh ON po.po_id=sh.po_id
WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED' AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name
HAVING COUNT(*)>=5
ORDER BY lead_stdev DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Compute lead time consistency score per vendor over the last year
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.category,
       ROUND(AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY,po.order_date,sh.actual_delivery_date)),1) AS avg_lead,
       ROUND(STDEV(DATEDIFF(DAY,po.order_date,sh.actual_delivery_date)),2) AS stdev_lead,
       100 - LEAST(100, ROUND(STDEV(DATEDIFF(DAY,po.order_date,sh.actual_delivery_date))/
               NULLIF(AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY,po.order_date,sh.actual_delivery_date)),0)*100,1)) AS consistency_score
FROM suppliers s
JOIN purchase_orders po ON s.supplier_id=po.supplier_id
JOIN shipments sh ON po.po_id=sh.po_id
WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED'
  AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, s.category
HAVING COUNT(*)>=5
ORDER BY consistency_score DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Evaluate which approved vendors supply every product category we purchase
output:
WITH all_cats AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT p.category FROM products p
    WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM purchase_order_items poi
                  JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
                  WHERE poi.sku=p.sku AND po.status!='CANCELLED')
),
vendor_cats AS (
    SELECT po.supplier_id, COUNT(DISTINCT p.category) AS cat_count
    FROM purchase_orders po
    JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
    JOIN products p ON poi.sku=p.sku
    WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED'
    GROUP BY po.supplier_id
)
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       vc.cat_count AS categories_supplied,
       (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM all_cats) AS total_categories
FROM vendor_cats vc
JOIN suppliers s ON vc.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE vc.cat_count=(SELECT COUNT(*) FROM all_cats)
ORDER BY s.supplier_name;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Verify that supplier performance evaluation scores are up to date for all active vendors
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.category,
       MAX(sp.evaluation_date) AS latest_eval,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,MAX(sp.evaluation_date),GETDATE()) AS days_since_eval,
       IIF(DATEDIFF(DAY,MAX(sp.evaluation_date),GETDATE())>90,'OVERDUE','CURRENT') AS eval_status
FROM suppliers s
LEFT JOIN supplier_performance sp ON s.supplier_id=sp.supplier_id
WHERE s.status='ACTIVE'
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, s.category
ORDER BY days_since_eval DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Check which vendors have experienced declining quality over the past 4 evaluations
output:
WITH scored AS (
    SELECT supplier_id, evaluation_date,
           ROUND(quality_score*0.4+delivery_score*0.4+cost_score*0.2,2) AS score,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY supplier_id ORDER BY evaluation_date DESC) AS rn
    FROM supplier_performance
)
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       MAX(CASE WHEN rn=1 THEN score END) AS latest,
       MAX(CASE WHEN rn=2 THEN score END) AS prev1,
       MAX(CASE WHEN rn=3 THEN score END) AS prev2,
       MAX(CASE WHEN rn=4 THEN score END) AS prev3
FROM scored sc
JOIN suppliers s ON sc.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
GROUP BY s.supplier_name
HAVING MAX(CASE WHEN rn=1 THEN score END) < MAX(CASE WHEN rn=4 THEN score END)
   AND COUNT(DISTINCT sc.rn)>=4
ORDER BY MAX(CASE WHEN rn=1 THEN score END)-MAX(CASE WHEN rn=4 THEN score END) ASC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Investigate consistently late vendors — delivery punctuality below 80% for two or more consecutive quarters
output:
WITH qotd AS (
    SELECT po.supplier_id,
           YEAR(sh.actual_delivery_date) AS yr,
           DATEPART(QUARTER,sh.actual_delivery_date) AS qtr,
           ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN sh.actual_delivery_date<=sh.expected_delivery_date THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0),1) AS otd
    FROM purchase_orders po
    JOIN shipments sh ON po.po_id=sh.po_id
    WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED' AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY po.supplier_id, YEAR(sh.actual_delivery_date), DATEPART(QUARTER,sh.actual_delivery_date)
)
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       COUNT(*) AS quarters_below_80,
       MIN(qotd.otd) AS worst_otd
FROM qotd
JOIN suppliers s ON qotd.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE qotd.otd<80
GROUP BY s.supplier_name
HAVING COUNT(*)>=2
ORDER BY quarters_below_80 DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Analyse on-time delivery trends for each supplier broken down by quarter
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       CONCAT(YEAR(sh.actual_delivery_date),' Q',DATEPART(QUARTER,sh.actual_delivery_date)) AS period,
       COUNT(*) AS shipments,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN sh.actual_delivery_date<=sh.expected_delivery_date THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0),1) AS otd_pct
FROM suppliers s
JOIN purchase_orders po ON s.supplier_id=po.supplier_id
JOIN shipments sh ON po.po_id=sh.po_id
WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED' AND sh.actual_delivery_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, YEAR(sh.actual_delivery_date), DATEPART(QUARTER,sh.actual_delivery_date)
ORDER BY s.supplier_name, YEAR(sh.actual_delivery_date), DATEPART(QUARTER,sh.actual_delivery_date);

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Review the average response time from PO creation to first shipment per vendor
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       COUNT(*) AS po_count,
       ROUND(AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY,po.order_date,sh.ship_date)),1) AS avg_days_po_to_ship
FROM suppliers s
JOIN purchase_orders po ON s.supplier_id=po.supplier_id
JOIN shipments sh ON po.po_id=sh.po_id
WHERE sh.status IN ('IN_TRANSIT','DELIVERED')
  AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name
HAVING COUNT(*)>=3
ORDER BY avg_days_po_to_ship;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Compute the total number of approved vendors per product category
output:
SELECT p.category,
       COUNT(DISTINCT sp.supplier_id) AS approved_vendor_count
FROM sourcing_products sp
JOIN products p ON sp.sku=p.sku
JOIN suppliers s ON sp.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE sp.status='APPROVED' AND s.status='ACTIVE'
GROUP BY p.category
ORDER BY approved_vendor_count DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Produce a ranked list of vendors by spend within each category
output:
SELECT s.category, s.supplier_name,
       SUM(poi.line_total) AS annual_spend,
       RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY s.category ORDER BY SUM(poi.line_total) DESC) AS spend_rank
FROM suppliers s
JOIN purchase_orders po ON s.supplier_id=po.supplier_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED' AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.category, s.supplier_name
ORDER BY s.category, spend_rank;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Summarise payment terms distribution across our entire active vendor base
output:
SELECT s.payment_terms,
       COUNT(*) AS vendor_count,
       ROUND(100.0*COUNT(*)/SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER (),1) AS pct_of_vendors,
       SUM(poi.line_total) AS annual_spend
FROM suppliers s
JOIN purchase_orders po ON s.supplier_id=po.supplier_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
WHERE s.status='ACTIVE' AND po.status!='CANCELLED'
  AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.payment_terms
ORDER BY vendor_count DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Assess which shipment routes have the highest average freight cost per kilogram
output:
SELECT r.origin, r.destination, r.carrier,
       COUNT(*) AS shipments,
       ROUND(AVG(sh.freight_cost/NULLIF(sh.weight_kg,0)),2) AS avg_cost_per_kg,
       SUM(sh.freight_cost) AS total_freight
FROM routes r
JOIN shipments sh ON r.route_id=sh.route_id
WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED'
  AND sh.actual_delivery_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY r.origin, r.destination, r.carrier
HAVING COUNT(*)>=5
ORDER BY avg_cost_per_kg DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Determine which freight routes are most prone to delivery delays
output:
SELECT r.origin, r.destination, r.carrier,
       COUNT(*) AS shipments,
       SUM(CASE WHEN sh.actual_delivery_date>sh.expected_delivery_date THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS late_count,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN sh.actual_delivery_date>sh.expected_delivery_date THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0),1) AS late_rate_pct,
       AVG(CASE WHEN sh.actual_delivery_date>sh.expected_delivery_date
               THEN DATEDIFF(DAY,sh.expected_delivery_date,sh.actual_delivery_date) ELSE 0 END) AS avg_delay_days
FROM routes r
JOIN shipments sh ON r.route_id=sh.route_id
WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED' AND sh.actual_delivery_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY r.origin, r.destination, r.carrier
HAVING COUNT(*)>=5
ORDER BY late_rate_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Extract all freight claims that are still unresolved past the 30-day resolution window
output:
SELECT fc.claim_number, sh.carrier, sh.tracking_number,
       fc.claim_date, fc.claim_amount,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,fc.claim_date,GETDATE()) AS days_open
FROM freight_claims fc
JOIN shipments sh ON fc.shipment_id=sh.shipment_id
WHERE fc.status IN ('OPEN','IN_REVIEW')
  AND DATEDIFF(DAY,fc.claim_date,GETDATE())>30
ORDER BY days_open DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Are there any logistics partners whose damage rate exceeds the 2% acceptable threshold?
output:
SELECT lp.partner_name, lp.service_type,
       COUNT(*) AS deliveries,
       SUM(sh.damage_reported) AS damaged,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(sh.damage_reported)/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0),2) AS damage_rate_pct
FROM logistics_partners lp
JOIN shipments sh ON lp.partner_id=sh.logistics_partner_id
WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED'
  AND sh.actual_delivery_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY lp.partner_name, lp.service_type
HAVING ROUND(100.0*SUM(sh.damage_reported)/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0),2)>2.0
ORDER BY damage_rate_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Investigate sea freight delays — which routes consistently arrive late by more than 3 days?
output:
SELECT r.origin, r.destination, sh.carrier,
       COUNT(*) AS sea_shipments,
       SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY,sh.expected_delivery_date,sh.actual_delivery_date)>3 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS severely_late,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY,sh.expected_delivery_date,sh.actual_delivery_date)>3 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0),1) AS severe_late_pct,
       AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY,sh.expected_delivery_date,sh.actual_delivery_date)) AS avg_delay
FROM shipments sh
JOIN routes r ON sh.route_id=r.route_id
JOIN logistics_partners lp ON sh.logistics_partner_id=lp.partner_id
WHERE lp.service_type='SEA' AND sh.status='DELIVERED'
  AND sh.actual_delivery_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY r.origin, r.destination, sh.carrier
HAVING COUNT(*)>=5
ORDER BY severe_late_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Gather all in-transit shipments along with their estimated arrival and supplier details
output:
SELECT sh.tracking_number, sh.carrier, lp.service_type,
       s.supplier_name, po.po_number,
       sh.expected_delivery_date,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,GETDATE(),sh.expected_delivery_date) AS days_to_arrival,
       sh.shipment_value
FROM shipments sh
JOIN logistics_partners lp ON sh.logistics_partner_id=lp.partner_id
JOIN purchase_orders po ON sh.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE sh.status='IN_TRANSIT'
ORDER BY sh.expected_delivery_date;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Derive the total freight spend as a percentage of total procurement spend by carrier
output:
SELECT sh.carrier,
       SUM(sh.freight_cost) AS freight_cost,
       SUM(sh.shipment_value) AS goods_value,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(sh.freight_cost)/NULLIF(SUM(sh.shipment_value),0),2) AS freight_as_pct_of_goods
FROM shipments sh
WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED'
  AND sh.actual_delivery_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY sh.carrier
ORDER BY freight_as_pct_of_goods DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Compute the cash-to-cash cycle days for our top 10 suppliers by spend
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       ROUND(AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY,po.order_date,sh.actual_delivery_date)),1) AS avg_procurement_days,
       ROUND(AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY,sh.actual_delivery_date,COALESCE(p.paid_date,DATEADD(DAY,30,inv.due_date)))),1) AS avg_payment_days,
       ROUND(AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY,po.order_date,sh.actual_delivery_date))+
             AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY,sh.actual_delivery_date,COALESCE(p.paid_date,DATEADD(DAY,30,inv.due_date)))),1) AS cash_to_cash_days
FROM suppliers s
JOIN purchase_orders po ON s.supplier_id=po.supplier_id
JOIN shipments sh ON po.po_id=sh.po_id
JOIN invoices inv ON inv.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
  AND inv.invoice_date BETWEEN po.order_date AND DATEADD(DAY,90,po.order_date)
LEFT JOIN payments p ON inv.invoice_id=p.invoice_id
WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED'
  AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name
ORDER BY cash_to_cash_days DESC
OFFSET 0 ROWS FETCH NEXT 10 ROWS ONLY;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Are there invoices where the billed amount is more than 10% above the agreed PO value?
output:
SELECT inv.invoice_number, s.supplier_name,
       po.total_amount AS po_value,
       inv.total_amount AS invoice_value,
       ROUND(100.0*(inv.total_amount-po.total_amount)/NULLIF(po.total_amount,0),1) AS overbill_pct
FROM invoices inv
JOIN suppliers s ON inv.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
JOIN purchase_orders po ON po.supplier_id=inv.supplier_id
  AND inv.invoice_date BETWEEN po.order_date AND DATEADD(DAY,90,po.order_date)
WHERE (inv.total_amount-po.total_amount)/NULLIF(po.total_amount,0)>0.10
  AND po.status NOT IN ('CANCELLED')
ORDER BY overbill_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Evaluate total payables outstanding grouped by supplier category and ageing bracket
output:
SELECT s.category,
       SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY,inv.due_date,GETDATE()) BETWEEN 1 AND 30
               THEN inv.total_amount-COALESCE(inv.paid_amount,0) ELSE 0 END) AS overdue_1_30,
       SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY,inv.due_date,GETDATE()) BETWEEN 31 AND 60
               THEN inv.total_amount-COALESCE(inv.paid_amount,0) ELSE 0 END) AS overdue_31_60,
       SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY,inv.due_date,GETDATE())>60
               THEN inv.total_amount-COALESCE(inv.paid_amount,0) ELSE 0 END) AS overdue_60_plus
FROM invoices inv
JOIN suppliers s ON inv.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE inv.status IN ('UNPAID','PARTIALLY_PAID') AND inv.due_date<GETDATE()
GROUP BY s.category
ORDER BY overdue_60_plus DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Generate a monthly spend trend report for the past 12 months grouped by supplier category
output:
SELECT FORMAT(po.order_date,'yyyy-MM') AS month,
       s.category,
       COUNT(po.po_id) AS po_count,
       SUM(poi.line_total) AS spend
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED'
  AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY FORMAT(po.order_date,'yyyy-MM'), s.category
ORDER BY month, s.category;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Review year-to-date spend against budget allocation by department
output:
SELECT e.department,
       SUM(pr.total_estimated_value) AS requisition_budget,
       SUM(poi.line_total) AS actual_po_spend,
       SUM(pr.total_estimated_value)-SUM(poi.line_total) AS budget_remaining
FROM purchase_requisitions pr
JOIN employees e ON pr.created_by=e.employee_id
JOIN purchase_orders po ON po.requisition_id=pr.requisition_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED'
  AND YEAR(po.order_date)=YEAR(GETDATE())
GROUP BY e.department
ORDER BY budget_remaining ASC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Derive the procurement cost savings versus standard cost for each buyer department
output:
SELECT e.department,
       SUM(poi.line_total) AS actual_spend,
       SUM(poi.ordered_quantity*p.unit_cost) AS standard_cost_spend,
       SUM(poi.ordered_quantity*p.unit_cost)-SUM(poi.line_total) AS savings
FROM purchase_order_items poi
JOIN products p ON poi.sku=p.sku
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN purchase_requisitions pr ON po.requisition_id=pr.requisition_id
JOIN employees e ON pr.created_by=e.employee_id
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED' AND YEAR(po.order_date)=YEAR(GETDATE())
GROUP BY e.department
ORDER BY savings DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Investigate open purchase orders where total spend commitment exceeds the remaining contract value
output:
SELECT c.contract_number, s.supplier_name,
       c.contract_value,
       SUM(po.total_amount) AS open_pos_value,
       SUM(poi_hist.line_total) AS historical_spend,
       c.contract_value-SUM(poi_hist.line_total)-SUM(po.total_amount) AS remaining_headroom
FROM contracts c
JOIN suppliers s ON c.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
JOIN purchase_orders po ON po.supplier_id=c.supplier_id
  AND po.status IN ('PENDING_APPROVAL','APPROVED')
JOIN purchase_orders po_hist ON po_hist.supplier_id=c.supplier_id
  AND po_hist.status IN ('RECEIVED','CLOSED')
  AND po_hist.order_date BETWEEN c.start_date AND c.end_date
JOIN purchase_order_items poi_hist ON po_hist.po_id=poi_hist.po_id
WHERE c.status='ACTIVE'
GROUP BY c.contract_number, s.supplier_name, c.contract_value
HAVING c.contract_value-SUM(poi_hist.line_total)-SUM(po.total_amount)<0
ORDER BY remaining_headroom ASC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Examine stock levels at each warehouse against the minimum safety stock threshold
output:
SELECT w.warehouse_name, p.sku, p.product_name,
       i.quantity_on_hand,
       i.safety_stock,
       i.quantity_on_hand-i.safety_stock AS buffer_above_safety
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
JOIN warehouses w ON i.warehouse_id=w.warehouse_id
WHERE i.quantity_on_hand<i.safety_stock*1.10
  AND i.safety_stock>0
ORDER BY buffer_above_safety ASC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Assess which SKUs have zero stock in all warehouses but have open customer orders
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name,
       COALESCE(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand),0) AS total_stock,
       COUNT(DISTINCT soi.so_id) AS open_orders,
       SUM(soi.ordered_quantity-COALESCE(soi.fulfilled_quantity,0)) AS unfulfilled_qty
FROM products p
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON p.sku=i.sku
JOIN sales_order_items soi ON p.sku=soi.sku
JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id=so.so_id
WHERE so.status IN ('OPEN','CONFIRMED')
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name
HAVING COALESCE(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand),0)=0
ORDER BY unfulfilled_qty DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Does any product category show a stockout rate above 5% of SKUs in that group?
output:
SELECT p.category,
       COUNT(DISTINCT p.sku) AS total_skus,
       COUNT(DISTINCT se.sku) AS skus_stocked_out,
       ROUND(100.0*COUNT(DISTINCT se.sku)/NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT p.sku),0),1) AS stockout_rate_pct
FROM products p
LEFT JOIN stockout_events se ON p.sku=se.sku
  AND se.start_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY p.category
HAVING ROUND(100.0*COUNT(DISTINCT se.sku)/NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT p.sku),0),1)>5
ORDER BY stockout_rate_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Extract the top 5 slowest-moving SKUs based on outbound volume in the last 90 days
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, p.category,
       COALESCE(SUM(it.quantity),0) AS outbound_90_days,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS current_stock
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON p.sku=i.sku
LEFT JOIN inventory_transactions it ON p.sku=it.sku
  AND it.transaction_type='OUTBOUND'
  AND it.transaction_date>=DATEADD(DAY,-90,GETDATE())
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, p.category
HAVING SUM(i.quantity_on_hand)>0
ORDER BY outbound_90_days ASC
OFFSET 0 ROWS FETCH NEXT 5 ROWS ONLY;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Produce an ABC inventory classification — which SKUs account for 70% of stock value?
output:
WITH sku_val AS (
    SELECT p.sku, p.product_name,
           SUM(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost) AS stock_value,
           SUM(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost)) OVER () AS total_value
    FROM inventory i
    JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
    GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name
)
SELECT sku, product_name, stock_value,
       ROUND(100.0*stock_value/total_value,2) AS pct_of_total,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(stock_value) OVER (ORDER BY stock_value DESC
             ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING)/total_value,1) AS cumulative_pct,
       CASE WHEN 100.0*SUM(stock_value) OVER (ORDER BY stock_value DESC ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING)/total_value<=70 THEN 'A'
            WHEN 100.0*SUM(stock_value) OVER (ORDER BY stock_value DESC ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING)/total_value<=90 THEN 'B'
            ELSE 'C' END AS abc_class
FROM sku_val
ORDER BY stock_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Investigate which SKUs consistently have negative cycle count variances indicating shrinkage
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name,
       COUNT(*) AS count_sessions,
       SUM(CASE WHEN i.quantity_on_hand<pc.physical_count_qty THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS times_system_over,
       ROUND(AVG(i.quantity_on_hand-pc.physical_count_qty),1) AS avg_variance
FROM physical_counts pc
JOIN inventory i ON pc.sku=i.sku AND pc.warehouse_id=i.warehouse_id
JOIN products p ON pc.sku=p.sku
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name
HAVING COUNT(*)>=2 AND AVG(i.quantity_on_hand-pc.physical_count_qty)<-5
ORDER BY avg_variance ASC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Examine the dock-to-stock conversion rate at each warehouse — how fast are receipts put away?
output:
SELECT w.warehouse_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT gr.grn_id) AS grns,
       AVG(DATEDIFF(HOUR,gr.receipt_date,it.transaction_date)) AS avg_hours_dock_to_stock,
       MAX(DATEDIFF(HOUR,gr.receipt_date,it.transaction_date)) AS worst_case_hours
FROM goods_receipts gr
JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON gr.grn_id=gri.grn_id
JOIN warehouses w ON gr.warehouse_id=w.warehouse_id
JOIN inventory_transactions it ON gri.sku=it.sku
  AND it.warehouse_id=gr.warehouse_id
  AND it.transaction_type='INBOUND'
  AND it.reference_id=gr.grn_number
WHERE gr.receipt_date>=DATEADD(MONTH,-3,GETDATE())
GROUP BY w.warehouse_name
ORDER BY avg_hours_dock_to_stock DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Analyse physical count accuracy — what percentage of SKUs had zero variance in the last cycle count?
output:
SELECT w.warehouse_name,
       COUNT(*) AS items_counted,
       SUM(CASE WHEN i.quantity_on_hand=pc.physical_count_qty THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS exact_match,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN i.quantity_on_hand=pc.physical_count_qty THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0),1) AS zero_variance_pct
FROM physical_counts pc
JOIN inventory i ON pc.sku=i.sku AND pc.warehouse_id=i.warehouse_id
JOIN warehouses w ON pc.warehouse_id=w.warehouse_id
WHERE pc.count_date=(SELECT MAX(count_date) FROM physical_counts)
GROUP BY w.warehouse_name
ORDER BY zero_variance_pct;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Review the on-time and in-full (OTIF) rate per customer for the past quarter
output:
SELECT c.customer_name, c.segment,
       COUNT(*) AS orders,
       SUM(CASE WHEN so.delivered_on_time=1 AND so.delivered_complete=1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS otif,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN so.delivered_on_time=1 AND so.delivered_complete=1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0),1) AS otif_pct
FROM customers c
JOIN sales_orders so ON c.customer_id=so.customer_id
WHERE so.status='DELIVERED'
  AND so.order_date>=DATEADD(QUARTER,-1,DATEADD(QUARTER,DATEDIFF(QUARTER,0,GETDATE()),0))
  AND so.order_date<DATEADD(QUARTER,DATEDIFF(QUARTER,0,GETDATE()),0)
GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.segment
ORDER BY otif_pct;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Determine which customers have had two or more orders cancelled in the past 6 months
output:
SELECT c.customer_name, c.segment,
       COUNT(*) AS cancelled_orders,
       SUM(so.total_value) AS cancelled_value
FROM customers c
JOIN sales_orders so ON c.customer_id=so.customer_id
WHERE so.status='CANCELLED'
  AND so.order_date>=DATEADD(MONTH,-6,GETDATE())
GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.segment
HAVING COUNT(*)>=2
ORDER BY cancelled_orders DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Produce a customer return rate report showing return frequency against order volume
output:
SELECT c.customer_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT so.so_id) AS total_orders,
       COUNT(DISTINCT ro.return_id) AS returns,
       ROUND(100.0*COUNT(DISTINCT ro.return_id)/NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT so.so_id),0),1) AS return_rate_pct,
       SUM(roi.quantity*roi.unit_price) AS returned_value
FROM customers c
JOIN sales_orders so ON c.customer_id=so.customer_id
LEFT JOIN return_orders ro ON so.so_id=ro.so_id
LEFT JOIN return_order_items roi ON ro.return_id=roi.return_id
WHERE so.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY c.customer_name
ORDER BY return_rate_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Extract average time from order placement to delivery for each customer segment this year
output:
SELECT c.segment,
       COUNT(*) AS delivered_orders,
       ROUND(AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY,so.order_date,so.actual_delivery_date)),1) AS avg_order_to_delivery_days,
       MIN(DATEDIFF(DAY,so.order_date,so.actual_delivery_date)) AS fastest_days,
       MAX(DATEDIFF(DAY,so.order_date,so.actual_delivery_date)) AS slowest_days
FROM customers c
JOIN sales_orders so ON c.customer_id=so.customer_id
WHERE so.status='DELIVERED'
  AND so.actual_delivery_date IS NOT NULL
  AND YEAR(so.order_date)=YEAR(GETDATE())
GROUP BY c.segment
ORDER BY avg_order_to_delivery_days;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Compute demand forecast MAPE and bias for each product over the last 12 months
output:
SELECT df.sku, p.product_name, p.category,
       COUNT(*) AS periods,
       ROUND(AVG(100.0*ABS(df.actual_quantity-df.forecasted_quantity)/NULLIF(df.actual_quantity,0)),2) AS mape_pct,
       ROUND(AVG(df.actual_quantity-df.forecasted_quantity),1) AS avg_bias,
       IIF(AVG(df.actual_quantity-df.forecasted_quantity)>0,'UNDER-FORECAST','OVER-FORECAST') AS bias_direction
FROM demand_forecasts df
JOIN products p ON df.sku=p.sku
WHERE df.actual_quantity IS NOT NULL AND df.actual_quantity>0
  AND df.forecast_period>=FORMAT(DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE()),'yyyy-MM')
GROUP BY df.sku, p.product_name, p.category
HAVING COUNT(*)>=3
ORDER BY mape_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Are there products where actual demand has been consistently above forecast for 3 or more months?
output:
SELECT df.sku, p.product_name,
       COUNT(*) AS consecutive_undershoot_months,
       AVG(df.actual_quantity-df.forecasted_quantity) AS avg_excess_demand
FROM demand_forecasts df
JOIN products p ON df.sku=p.sku
WHERE df.actual_quantity IS NOT NULL
  AND df.actual_quantity>df.forecasted_quantity
  AND df.forecast_period>=FORMAT(DATEADD(MONTH,-6,GETDATE()),'yyyy-MM')
GROUP BY df.sku, p.product_name
HAVING COUNT(*)>=3
ORDER BY avg_excess_demand DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Derive the optimal safety stock level using 95% service factor for all finished goods
output:
WITH stats AS (
    SELECT soi.sku,
           STDEV(mq.qty) AS demand_sd,
           AVG(ld.avg_lt) AS avg_lead_days,
           STDEV(ld.avg_lt) AS lt_sd
    FROM sales_order_items soi
    JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id=so.so_id
    JOIN (
        SELECT soi2.sku, FORMAT(so2.order_date,'yyyy-MM') AS ym, SUM(soi2.ordered_quantity) AS qty
        FROM sales_order_items soi2
        JOIN sales_orders so2 ON soi2.so_id=so2.so_id
        WHERE so2.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
        GROUP BY soi2.sku, FORMAT(so2.order_date,'yyyy-MM')
    ) mq ON soi.sku=mq.sku
    JOIN (
        SELECT poi.sku, AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY,po.order_date,sh.actual_delivery_date)) AS avg_lt
        FROM purchase_order_items poi
        JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
        JOIN shipments sh ON po.po_id=sh.po_id
        WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED'
        GROUP BY poi.sku
    ) ld ON soi.sku=ld.sku
    WHERE so.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY soi.sku
)
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name,
       ROUND(1.645*SQRT((st.avg_lead_days/30.0)*POWER(st.demand_sd,2)+POWER(0.5,2)*POWER(st.lt_sd,2)),0) AS safety_stock_95pct,
       MAX(i.safety_stock) AS current_safety_stock
FROM stats st
JOIN products p ON st.sku=p.sku
JOIN inventory i ON p.sku=i.sku
WHERE p.product_type='FINISHED_GOODS'
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, st.avg_lead_days, st.demand_sd, st.lt_sd
ORDER BY safety_stock_95pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Gather all batch numbers linked to failed quality inspections in the last 60 days for recall investigation
output:
SELECT b.batch_number, p.sku, p.product_name, s.supplier_name,
       gr.grn_number, gr.receipt_date,
       qc.defective_quantity, qc.inspection_date,
       qc.result AS inspection_outcome
FROM quality_checks qc
JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON qc.grn_item_id=gri.grn_item_id
JOIN goods_receipts gr ON gri.grn_id=gr.grn_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON gri.po_item_id=poi.po_item_id
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
JOIN products p ON gri.sku=p.sku
JOIN batches b ON gri.sku=b.sku
WHERE qc.result='FAIL'
  AND qc.inspection_date>=DATEADD(DAY,-60,GETDATE())
ORDER BY qc.inspection_date DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Evaluate corrective action closure rate per supplier — percentage resolved within 30 days
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       COUNT(*) AS total_cas,
