       SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY,ca.raised_date,ca.resolved_date)<=30 AND ca.resolved_date IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS resolved_on_time,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY,ca.raised_date,ca.resolved_date)<=30 AND ca.resolved_date IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0),1) AS closure_rate_pct
FROM corrective_actions ca
JOIN suppliers s ON ca.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE ca.raised_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name
HAVING COUNT(*)>=2
ORDER BY closure_rate_pct;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Is any single supplier responsible for more than 50% of quality failures in the past year?
output:
WITH total_fails AS (
    SELECT SUM(qc.defective_quantity) AS grand_total
    FROM quality_checks qc
    WHERE qc.inspection_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE()) AND qc.result='FAIL'
)
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       SUM(qc.defective_quantity) AS defective_qty,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(qc.defective_quantity)/NULLIF(tf.grand_total,0),1) AS pct_of_total_fails
FROM quality_checks qc
JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON qc.grn_item_id=gri.grn_item_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON gri.po_item_id=poi.po_item_id
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
CROSS JOIN total_fails tf
WHERE qc.result='FAIL' AND qc.inspection_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, tf.grand_total
HAVING ROUND(100.0*SUM(qc.defective_quantity)/NULLIF(tf.grand_total,0),1)>50
ORDER BY pct_of_total_fails DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Review all products with batch expiry within 7 days and the customers who received them last month
output:
SELECT b.batch_number, p.sku, p.product_name,
       b.expiry_date,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,GETDATE(),b.expiry_date) AS days_to_expiry,
       c.customer_name,
       so.so_number, so.ship_date
FROM batches b
JOIN products p ON b.sku=p.sku
JOIN sales_order_items soi ON p.sku=soi.sku
JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id=so.so_id
JOIN customers c ON so.customer_id=c.customer_id
WHERE b.expiry_date BETWEEN GETDATE() AND DATEADD(DAY,7,GETDATE())
  AND so.ship_date>=DATEADD(MONTH,-1,GETDATE())
  AND so.status IN ('SHIPPED','DELIVERED')
ORDER BY b.expiry_date;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Summarise the contract renewal pipeline — value and days remaining for contracts expiring this quarter
output:
SELECT c.contract_number, s.supplier_name, s.category,
       c.contract_value,
       c.end_date,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,GETDATE(),c.end_date) AS days_to_expiry,
       COALESCE(SUM(poi.line_total),0) AS ytd_utilisation
FROM contracts c
JOIN suppliers s ON c.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
LEFT JOIN purchase_orders po ON po.supplier_id=c.supplier_id
  AND po.order_date BETWEEN c.start_date AND GETDATE()
  AND po.status!='CANCELLED'
LEFT JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
WHERE c.status='ACTIVE'
  AND c.end_date BETWEEN GETDATE() AND DATEADD(QUARTER,1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY c.contract_number, s.supplier_name, s.category, c.contract_value, c.end_date
ORDER BY c.end_date;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Assess contract compliance — what percentage of procurement spend is covered by active contracts?
output:
WITH total_spend AS (
    SELECT SUM(poi.line_total) AS grand_total
    FROM purchase_order_items poi
    JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
    WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED' AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
),
contracted_spend AS (
    SELECT SUM(poi.line_total) AS contracted
    FROM purchase_order_items poi
    JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
    JOIN contracts c ON po.supplier_id=c.supplier_id
      AND po.order_date BETWEEN c.start_date AND c.end_date
      AND c.status='ACTIVE'
    WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED' AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
)
SELECT ts.grand_total AS total_spend,
       cs.contracted AS contracted_spend,
       ROUND(100.0*cs.contracted/NULLIF(ts.grand_total,0),1) AS contract_coverage_pct
FROM total_spend ts, contracted_spend cs;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Examine multi-contract suppliers to identify consolidation candidates
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.category,
       COUNT(c.contract_id) AS active_contracts,
       SUM(c.contract_value) AS total_contracted_value,
       STRING_AGG(c.contract_number,', ') WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY c.start_date) AS contract_numbers
FROM contracts c
JOIN suppliers s ON c.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE c.status='ACTIVE'
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, s.category
HAVING COUNT(c.contract_id)>1
ORDER BY active_contracts DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Extract all sourcing products where the approved price has not been reviewed in over 6 months
output:
SELECT sp.sku, p.product_name, p.category,
       s.supplier_name,
       sp.unit_price,
       sp.updated_at,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,sp.updated_at,GETDATE()) AS days_since_review
FROM sourcing_products sp
JOIN products p ON sp.sku=p.sku
JOIN suppliers s ON sp.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE sp.status='APPROVED'
  AND DATEDIFF(DAY,sp.updated_at,GETDATE())>180
ORDER BY days_since_review DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Investigate which production runs have the greatest gap between planned and actual output quantities
output:
SELECT wo.wo_number, p.product_name,
       wo.planned_qty,
       COALESCE(wo.actual_qty,0) AS actual_qty,
       wo.planned_qty-COALESCE(wo.actual_qty,0) AS output_gap,
       ROUND(100.0*COALESCE(wo.actual_qty,0)/NULLIF(wo.planned_qty,0),1) AS attainment_pct
FROM work_orders wo
JOIN products p ON wo.product_sku=p.sku
WHERE wo.status='COMPLETED'
  AND wo.end_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
  AND wo.planned_qty>COALESCE(wo.actual_qty,0)
ORDER BY output_gap DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Evaluate component availability against bill of materials requirements for all planned work orders
output:
SELECT wo.wo_number, p_fg.product_name AS finished_good,
       b.child_sku AS component_sku, p_comp.product_name AS component,
       b.quantity_required*wo.planned_qty AS required_qty,
       COALESCE(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand),0) AS available_qty,
       IIF(COALESCE(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand),0)>=b.quantity_required*wo.planned_qty,'SUFFICIENT','SHORT') AS availability
FROM work_orders wo
JOIN bom b ON wo.product_sku=b.parent_sku
JOIN products p_fg ON wo.product_sku=p_fg.sku
JOIN products p_comp ON b.child_sku=p_comp.sku
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON b.child_sku=i.sku
WHERE wo.status='PLANNED'
GROUP BY wo.wo_number, p_fg.product_name, b.child_sku, p_comp.product_name,
         b.quantity_required, wo.planned_qty
ORDER BY availability, wo.wo_number;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Derive the total cost of goods manufactured by product line this year
output:
SELECT p.category AS product_line,
       COUNT(wo.wo_id) AS work_orders,
       SUM(wo.actual_qty) AS units_produced,
       SUM(wo.actual_qty * p.unit_cost) AS total_cost_of_goods_manufactured
FROM work_orders wo
JOIN products p ON wo.product_sku=p.sku
WHERE wo.status='COMPLETED'
  AND YEAR(wo.end_date)=YEAR(GETDATE())
  AND wo.actual_qty IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY p.category
ORDER BY total_cost_of_goods_manufactured DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Compute the percentage of approved procurement spend placed with preferred vendors
output:
WITH total AS (
    SELECT SUM(poi.line_total) AS all_spend
    FROM purchase_order_items poi
    JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
    WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED' AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
)
SELECT 'PREFERRED' AS vendor_type,
       SUM(poi.line_total) AS spend,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(poi.line_total)/NULLIF(t.all_spend,0),1) AS spend_pct
FROM purchase_order_items poi
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN sourcing_products sp ON poi.sku=sp.sku AND po.supplier_id=sp.supplier_id
CROSS JOIN total t
WHERE sp.status='APPROVED'
  AND po.status!='CANCELLED'
  AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY t.all_spend;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Investigate purchase orders raised outside the approved sourcing list in the past quarter
output:
SELECT po.po_number, s.supplier_name, poi.sku, p.product_name,
       poi.ordered_quantity, poi.unit_price, poi.line_total
FROM purchase_order_items poi
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
JOIN products p ON poi.sku=p.sku
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED'
  AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(QUARTER,-1,DATEADD(QUARTER,DATEDIFF(QUARTER,0,GETDATE()),0))
  AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM sourcing_products sp
      WHERE sp.sku=poi.sku AND sp.supplier_id=po.supplier_id AND sp.status='APPROVED'
  )
ORDER BY poi.line_total DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Gather all EOQ parameters and compute the number of optimal orders per year for each SKU
output:
SELECT eoq.sku, p.product_name,
       eoq.annual_demand,
       eoq.economic_order_qty,
       ROUND(eoq.annual_demand/NULLIF(eoq.economic_order_qty,0),1) AS optimal_orders_per_year,
       ROUND(365.0/NULLIF(eoq.annual_demand/NULLIF(eoq.economic_order_qty,0),0),0) AS days_between_orders
FROM eoq_parameters eoq
JOIN products p ON eoq.sku=p.sku
ORDER BY optimal_orders_per_year DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Evaluate single-source risk — critical SKUs with only one approved supplier and low stock coverage
output:
WITH vendor_count AS (
    SELECT sku, COUNT(DISTINCT supplier_id) AS vendor_cnt
    FROM sourcing_products WHERE status='APPROVED'
    GROUP BY sku
),
days_cover AS (
    SELECT soi.sku,
           ROUND(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand)/NULLIF(SUM(soi.ordered_quantity)/90.0,0),1) AS days_stock
    FROM inventory i
    JOIN sales_order_items soi ON i.sku=soi.sku
    JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id=so.so_id
    WHERE so.order_date>=DATEADD(DAY,-90,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY soi.sku
)
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, p.category,
       vc.vendor_cnt,
       COALESCE(dc.days_stock,0) AS days_cover,
       IIF(vc.vendor_cnt=1 AND COALESCE(dc.days_stock,0)<14,'HIGH RISK','MODERATE') AS risk_level
FROM products p
JOIN vendor_count vc ON p.sku=vc.sku
LEFT JOIN days_cover dc ON p.sku=dc.sku
WHERE vc.vendor_cnt=1
ORDER BY risk_level DESC, days_cover ASC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Analyse stockout frequency and average duration by product category over the past year
output:
SELECT p.category,
       COUNT(*) AS stockout_events,
       COUNT(DISTINCT se.sku) AS skus_affected,
       ROUND(AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY,se.start_date,COALESCE(se.end_date,GETDATE()))),1) AS avg_duration_days,
       SUM(DATEDIFF(DAY,se.start_date,COALESCE(se.end_date,GETDATE()))) AS total_stockout_days
FROM stockout_events se
JOIN products p ON se.sku=p.sku
WHERE se.start_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY p.category
ORDER BY total_stockout_days DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Is the supply chain exposed to geographic risk — do we depend heavily on suppliers from a single country?
output:
SELECT s.country,
       COUNT(DISTINCT s.supplier_id) AS supplier_count,
       SUM(poi.line_total) AS annual_spend,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(poi.line_total)/SUM(SUM(poi.line_total)) OVER (),1) AS spend_pct
FROM suppliers s
JOIN purchase_orders po ON s.supplier_id=po.supplier_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED' AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.country
ORDER BY annual_spend DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Examine the expense transaction log for any anomalous purchase patterns above the approval limit
output:
SELECT et.transaction_id, e.employee_name, e.department,
       et.amount, et.expense_date, et.description,
       et.approval_status
FROM expense_transactions et
JOIN employees e ON et.employee_id=e.employee_id
WHERE et.amount>5000
  AND et.approval_status='PENDING'
  AND et.expense_date>=DATEADD(MONTH,-1,GETDATE())
ORDER BY et.amount DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Generate a full DML bulk insert of new EOQ parameters for SKUs missing from the table
output:
INSERT INTO eoq_parameters (sku, annual_demand, ordering_cost, holding_cost_rate, economic_order_qty)
SELECT p.sku,
       ROUND(SUM(soi.ordered_quantity),0) AS annual_demand,
       250.0 AS ordering_cost,
       0.20 AS holding_cost_rate,
       ROUND(SQRT(2.0*SUM(soi.ordered_quantity)*250.0/(NULLIF(p.unit_cost,0)*0.20)),0) AS eoq
FROM products p
JOIN sales_order_items soi ON p.sku=soi.sku
JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id=so.so_id
WHERE so.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
  AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM eoq_parameters ep WHERE ep.sku=p.sku)
GROUP BY p.sku, p.unit_cost;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Verify that all active purchase orders have a corresponding shipment record
output:
SELECT po.po_number, s.supplier_name, po.order_date,
       po.expected_delivery_date, po.total_amount, po.status
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE po.status IN ('APPROVED','SHIPPED')
  AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM shipments sh WHERE sh.po_id=po.po_id
  )
ORDER BY po.expected_delivery_date;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Produce an update to mark all received purchase orders as CLOSED where goods receipt is complete
output:
UPDATE purchase_orders
SET status='CLOSED', updated_at=GETDATE()
WHERE status='RECEIVED'
  AND po_id IN (
      SELECT DISTINCT poi.po_id
      FROM purchase_order_items poi
      JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON poi.po_item_id=gri.po_item_id
      GROUP BY poi.po_id
      HAVING SUM(poi.ordered_quantity)<=SUM(gri.received_quantity)
  );

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Perpetual inventory movement — net daily stock change per SKU this week
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name,
       CAST(it.transaction_date AS DATE) AS day,
       SUM(CASE WHEN it.transaction_type='INBOUND'  THEN  it.quantity ELSE 0 END) AS inbound,
       SUM(CASE WHEN it.transaction_type='OUTBOUND' THEN  it.quantity ELSE 0 END) AS outbound,
       SUM(CASE WHEN it.transaction_type='INBOUND'  THEN  it.quantity
                WHEN it.transaction_type='OUTBOUND' THEN -it.quantity ELSE 0 END) AS net_movement
FROM inventory_transactions it
JOIN products p ON it.sku=p.sku
WHERE it.transaction_date >= DATEADD(DAY,-7,GETDATE())
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, CAST(it.transaction_date AS DATE)
ORDER BY p.sku, day;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Daily net stock movement for each product over the past 7 days
output:
SELECT it.sku, p.product_name,
       CAST(it.transaction_date AS DATE) AS day,
       SUM(CASE WHEN it.transaction_type='INBOUND'  THEN  it.quantity
                WHEN it.transaction_type='OUTBOUND' THEN -it.quantity ELSE 0 END) AS net
FROM inventory_transactions it
JOIN products p ON it.sku=p.sku
WHERE it.transaction_date >= DATEADD(DAY,-7,GETDATE())
GROUP BY it.sku, p.product_name, CAST(it.transaction_date AS DATE)
ORDER BY it.sku, day;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Which SKUs had the largest net inbound movement last week?
output:
SELECT it.sku, p.product_name,
       SUM(CASE WHEN it.transaction_type='INBOUND'  THEN  it.quantity
                WHEN it.transaction_type='OUTBOUND' THEN -it.quantity ELSE 0 END) AS net_movement
FROM inventory_transactions it
JOIN products p ON it.sku=p.sku
WHERE it.transaction_date >= DATEADD(DAY,-7,GETDATE())
GROUP BY it.sku, p.product_name
ORDER BY net_movement DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Bespoke customer orders — sales orders with special handling instructions this month
output:
SELECT so.so_number, c.customer_name, so.order_date,
       so.total_value, so.special_instructions
FROM sales_orders so
JOIN customers c ON so.customer_id=c.customer_id
WHERE so.special_instructions IS NOT NULL
  AND so.order_date >= DATEADD(DAY,1-DAY(GETDATE()),CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE))
ORDER BY so.order_date DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Orders with special customer instructions placed this month
output:
SELECT so.so_number, c.customer_name,
       so.order_date, so.total_value, so.special_instructions
FROM sales_orders so
JOIN customers c ON so.customer_id=c.customer_id
WHERE so.special_instructions IS NOT NULL
  AND MONTH(so.order_date)=MONTH(GETDATE())
  AND YEAR(so.order_date)=YEAR(GETDATE())
ORDER BY so.order_date;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Are there any sales orders this month flagged with special handling requirements?
output:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS special_orders,
       SUM(so.total_value) AS total_value
FROM sales_orders so
WHERE so.special_instructions IS NOT NULL
  AND so.order_date >= DATEADD(DAY,1-DAY(GETDATE()),CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE));

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Requisitioned materials awaiting approval — purchase requisitions in pending status
output:
SELECT pr.requisition_number, e.employee_name, e.department,
       pr.priority, pr.created_date,
       pr.total_estimated_value,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,pr.created_date,GETDATE()) AS days_waiting
FROM purchase_requisitions pr
JOIN employees e ON pr.created_by=e.employee_id
WHERE pr.status='PENDING_APPROVAL'
ORDER BY pr.priority DESC, days_waiting DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: How many requisitions are waiting for approval and what is the total value at risk?
output:
SELECT pr.priority,
       COUNT(*) AS pending_reqs,
       SUM(pr.total_estimated_value) AS total_value,
       AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY,pr.created_date,GETDATE())) AS avg_wait_days
FROM purchase_requisitions pr
WHERE pr.status='PENDING_APPROVAL'
GROUP BY pr.priority
ORDER BY CASE pr.priority WHEN 'CRITICAL' THEN 1 WHEN 'HIGH' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Pending purchase requisitions broken down by department and priority level
output:
SELECT e.department, pr.priority,
       COUNT(*) AS reqs,
       SUM(pr.total_estimated_value) AS est_value
FROM purchase_requisitions pr
JOIN employees e ON pr.created_by=e.employee_id
WHERE pr.status='PENDING_APPROVAL'
GROUP BY e.department, pr.priority
ORDER BY e.department, CASE pr.priority WHEN 'CRITICAL' THEN 1 WHEN 'HIGH' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Disbursed payments this month — all payments made to suppliers
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       COUNT(p.payment_id) AS payment_count,
       SUM(p.amount) AS total_disbursed
FROM payments p
JOIN invoices inv ON p.invoice_id=inv.invoice_id
JOIN suppliers s ON inv.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE MONTH(p.payment_date)=MONTH(GETDATE())
  AND YEAR(p.payment_date)=YEAR(GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name
ORDER BY total_disbursed DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Total payments made to each supplier this month
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.category,
       SUM(p.amount) AS amount_paid,
       COUNT(p.payment_id) AS payment_count
FROM payments p
JOIN invoices inv ON p.invoice_id=inv.invoice_id
JOIN suppliers s ON inv.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE p.payment_date >= DATEADD(DAY,1-DAY(GETDATE()),CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE))
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, s.category
ORDER BY amount_paid DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Which vendors received the largest payment disbursements this month?
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, SUM(p.amount) AS disbursed
FROM payments p
JOIN invoices inv ON p.invoice_id=inv.invoice_id
JOIN suppliers s ON inv.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE p.payment_date >= DATEADD(DAY,1-DAY(GETDATE()),CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE))
GROUP BY s.supplier_name
ORDER BY disbursed DESC
OFFSET 0 ROWS FETCH NEXT 10 ROWS ONLY;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Remittance advice summary — all payments due within the next 14 days
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       COUNT(inv.invoice_id) AS invoices_due,
       SUM(inv.total_amount-COALESCE(inv.paid_amount,0)) AS amount_due,
       MIN(inv.due_date) AS earliest_due
FROM invoices inv
JOIN suppliers s ON inv.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE inv.status IN ('UNPAID','PARTIALLY_PAID')
  AND inv.due_date BETWEEN GETDATE() AND DATEADD(DAY,14,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name
ORDER BY earliest_due;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Which supplier payments are due in the next two weeks?
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, inv.invoice_number,
       inv.due_date,
       inv.total_amount-COALESCE(inv.paid_amount,0) AS amount_due
FROM invoices inv
JOIN suppliers s ON inv.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE inv.status IN ('UNPAID','PARTIALLY_PAID')
  AND inv.due_date BETWEEN GETDATE() AND DATEADD(DAY,14,GETDATE())
ORDER BY inv.due_date;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Compile the remittance schedule — outstanding invoices by due date for the next 14 days
output:
SELECT FORMAT(inv.due_date,'yyyy-MM-dd') AS due_date,
       COUNT(inv.invoice_id) AS invoices,
       SUM(inv.total_amount-COALESCE(inv.paid_amount,0)) AS daily_payment_due
FROM invoices inv
WHERE inv.status IN ('UNPAID','PARTIALLY_PAID')
  AND inv.due_date BETWEEN GETDATE() AND DATEADD(DAY,14,GETDATE())
GROUP BY FORMAT(inv.due_date,'yyyy-MM-dd')
ORDER BY due_date;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Deferred payment obligations — invoices with extended payment terms beyond 60 days
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.payment_terms,
       COUNT(inv.invoice_id) AS deferred_invoices,
       SUM(inv.total_amount) AS deferred_amount
FROM invoices inv
JOIN suppliers s ON inv.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE inv.status IN ('UNPAID','PARTIALLY_PAID')
  AND DATEDIFF(DAY,inv.invoice_date,inv.due_date)>60
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, s.payment_terms
ORDER BY deferred_amount DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Invoices with payment terms beyond 60 days that are still outstanding
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, inv.invoice_number,
       inv.invoice_date, inv.due_date,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,inv.invoice_date,inv.due_date) AS payment_term_days,
       inv.total_amount-COALESCE(inv.paid_amount,0) AS outstanding
FROM invoices inv
JOIN suppliers s ON inv.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE inv.status IN ('UNPAID','PARTIALLY_PAID')
  AND DATEDIFF(DAY,inv.invoice_date,inv.due_date)>60
ORDER BY outstanding DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Are there vendors with deferred payment terms over 90 days that have unpaid invoices?
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.payment_terms,
       COUNT(*) AS invoices,
       SUM(inv.total_amount-COALESCE(inv.paid_amount,0)) AS outstanding
FROM invoices inv
JOIN suppliers s ON inv.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE inv.status IN ('UNPAID','PARTIALLY_PAID')
  AND DATEDIFF(DAY,inv.invoice_date,inv.due_date)>90
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, s.payment_terms
HAVING COUNT(*)>0
ORDER BY outstanding DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Capitalised inventory — high-value items classified as capital expenditure in stock
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, p.product_type,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS qty,
       p.unit_cost,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost) AS capitalised_value
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
WHERE p.unit_cost >= 10000
  AND i.quantity_on_hand > 0
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, p.product_type, p.unit_cost
ORDER BY capitalised_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: High-value inventory items with unit cost above 10000 currently in stock
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS qty,
       p.unit_cost,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost) AS total_value
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
WHERE p.unit_cost>=10000 AND i.quantity_on_hand>0
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, p.unit_cost
ORDER BY total_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Amortised contract value — portion of contract spend consumed versus total contract value
output:
SELECT c.contract_number, s.supplier_name,
       c.contract_value,
       COALESCE(SUM(poi.line_total),0) AS consumed,
       c.contract_value-COALESCE(SUM(poi.line_total),0) AS remaining,
       ROUND(100.0*COALESCE(SUM(poi.line_total),0)/NULLIF(c.contract_value,0),1) AS consumed_pct
FROM contracts c
JOIN suppliers s ON c.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
LEFT JOIN purchase_orders po ON po.supplier_id=c.supplier_id
  AND po.order_date BETWEEN c.start_date AND c.end_date
  AND po.status!='CANCELLED'
LEFT JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
WHERE c.status='ACTIVE'
GROUP BY c.contract_number, s.supplier_name, c.contract_value
ORDER BY consumed_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Contract utilisation — how much of each active contract has been spent versus total value?
output:
SELECT c.contract_number, s.supplier_name,
       c.contract_value,
       COALESCE(SUM(poi.line_total),0) AS spent,
       ROUND(100.0*COALESCE(SUM(poi.line_total),0)/NULLIF(c.contract_value,0),1) AS utilisation_pct
FROM contracts c
JOIN suppliers s ON c.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
LEFT JOIN purchase_orders po ON po.supplier_id=c.supplier_id
  AND po.order_date BETWEEN c.start_date AND c.end_date AND po.status!='CANCELLED'
LEFT JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
WHERE c.status='ACTIVE'
GROUP BY c.contract_number, s.supplier_name, c.contract_value
ORDER BY utilisation_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Which active contracts are over 80% consumed and may need renewal soon?
output:
SELECT c.contract_number, s.supplier_name,
       c.contract_value, c.end_date,
       COALESCE(SUM(poi.line_total),0) AS spent,
       ROUND(100.0*COALESCE(SUM(poi.line_total),0)/NULLIF(c.contract_value,0),1) AS pct_consumed,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,GETDATE(),c.end_date) AS days_to_expiry
FROM contracts c
JOIN suppliers s ON c.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
LEFT JOIN purchase_orders po ON po.supplier_id=c.supplier_id
  AND po.order_date BETWEEN c.start_date AND c.end_date AND po.status!='CANCELLED'
LEFT JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
WHERE c.status='ACTIVE'
GROUP BY c.contract_number, s.supplier_name, c.contract_value, c.end_date
HAVING ROUND(100.0*COALESCE(SUM(poi.line_total),0)/NULLIF(c.contract_value,0),1)>80
ORDER BY pct_consumed DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Provisioned stock — safety buffer quantities set aside for demand uncertainty by SKU
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, p.category,
       MAX(i.safety_stock) AS provisioned_buffer,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS on_hand,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand)-MAX(i.safety_stock) AS freely_available
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
WHERE i.safety_stock>0
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, p.category
ORDER BY provisioned_buffer DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Safety buffer stock allocated per product category across all warehouses
output:
SELECT p.category,
       SUM(i.safety_stock) AS total_safety_stock_provisioned,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS total_on_hand,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand)-SUM(i.safety_stock) AS available_above_buffer
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
GROUP BY p.category
ORDER BY total_safety_stock_provisioned DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Is safety stock configured for all products that have open sales orders?
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name,
       COALESCE(MAX(i.safety_stock),0) AS safety_stock
FROM products p
JOIN sales_order_items soi ON p.sku=soi.sku
JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id=so.so_id
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON p.sku=i.sku
WHERE so.status IN ('OPEN','CONFIRMED')
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name
HAVING COALESCE(MAX(i.safety_stock),0)=0
ORDER BY p.product_name;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Expensed procurement items — purchase orders coded as operational expense this year
output:
SELECT p.product_type, p.category,
       COUNT(DISTINCT po.po_id) AS expense_pos,
       SUM(poi.line_total) AS total_expensed
FROM purchase_order_items poi
JOIN products p ON poi.sku=p.sku
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED'
  AND YEAR(po.order_date)=YEAR(GETDATE())
  AND p.product_type IN ('CONSUMABLE','MRO','SERVICE')
GROUP BY p.product_type, p.category
ORDER BY total_expensed DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: What is the total spend on consumable and MRO items this year?
output:
SELECT p.product_type,
       COUNT(DISTINCT poi.sku) AS sku_count,
       SUM(poi.line_total) AS total_spend
FROM purchase_order_items poi
JOIN products p ON poi.sku=p.sku
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED'
  AND YEAR(po.order_date)=YEAR(GETDATE())
  AND p.product_type IN ('CONSUMABLE','MRO')
GROUP BY p.product_type
ORDER BY total_spend DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Receipted goods this week — all goods receipt notes created in the last 7 days
output:
SELECT gr.grn_number, s.supplier_name, po.po_number,
       gr.receipt_date, w.warehouse_name,
       SUM(gri.received_quantity) AS units_received
FROM goods_receipts gr
JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON gr.grn_id=gri.grn_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON gri.po_item_id=poi.po_item_id
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
JOIN warehouses w ON gr.warehouse_id=w.warehouse_id
WHERE gr.receipt_date >= DATEADD(DAY,-7,GETDATE())
GROUP BY gr.grn_number, s.supplier_name, po.po_number, gr.receipt_date, w.warehouse_name
ORDER BY gr.receipt_date DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: All goods receipts processed in the last 7 days with supplier and warehouse details
output:
SELECT gr.grn_number, gr.receipt_date, s.supplier_name,
       w.warehouse_name, SUM(gri.received_quantity) AS units_in
FROM goods_receipts gr
JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON gr.grn_id=gri.grn_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON gri.po_item_id=poi.po_item_id
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
JOIN warehouses w ON gr.warehouse_id=w.warehouse_id
WHERE gr.receipt_date>=DATEADD(DAY,-7,GETDATE())
GROUP BY gr.grn_number, gr.receipt_date, s.supplier_name, w.warehouse_name
ORDER BY gr.receipt_date DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: How many GRNs were receipted per warehouse in the last 7 days?
output:
SELECT w.warehouse_name,
       COUNT(gr.grn_id) AS grn_count,
       SUM(gri.received_quantity) AS total_units
FROM goods_receipts gr
JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON gr.grn_id=gri.grn_id
JOIN warehouses w ON gr.warehouse_id=w.warehouse_id
WHERE gr.receipt_date>=DATEADD(DAY,-7,GETDATE())
GROUP BY w.warehouse_name
ORDER BY grn_count DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Despatched orders today — all outbound shipments processed in the last 24 hours
output:
SELECT sh.tracking_number, sh.carrier,
       s.supplier_name, po.po_number,
       sh.shipment_value, sh.weight_kg
FROM shipments sh
JOIN purchase_orders po ON sh.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE sh.ship_date >= DATEADD(HOUR,-24,GETDATE())
ORDER BY sh.ship_date DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Shipments despatched to customers in the past 24 hours
output:
SELECT osh.tracking_number, osh.carrier,
       c.customer_name, so.so_number,
       osh.shipment_value, osh.dispatch_date
FROM outbound_shipments osh
JOIN sales_orders so ON osh.so_id=so.so_id
JOIN customers c ON so.customer_id=c.customer_id
WHERE osh.dispatch_date >= DATEADD(HOUR,-24,GETDATE())
ORDER BY osh.dispatch_date DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Were any consigned shipments delivered late this quarter?
output:
SELECT sh.tracking_number, sh.carrier,
       sh.expected_delivery_date, sh.actual_delivery_date,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,sh.expected_delivery_date,sh.actual_delivery_date) AS days_late
FROM shipments sh
WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED'
  AND sh.actual_delivery_date>sh.expected_delivery_date
  AND sh.actual_delivery_date>=DATEADD(QUARTER,DATEDIFF(QUARTER,0,GETDATE()),0)
ORDER BY days_late DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Consigned inventory value by logistics partner awaiting final delivery
output:
SELECT lp.partner_name,
       COUNT(sh.shipment_id) AS consignments,
       SUM(sh.shipment_value) AS total_consignment_value,
       SUM(sh.weight_kg) AS total_kg
FROM shipments sh
JOIN logistics_partners lp ON sh.logistics_partner_id=lp.partner_id
WHERE sh.status='IN_TRANSIT'
GROUP BY lp.partner_name
ORDER BY total_consignment_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Total value of goods currently consigned to each logistics partner
output:
SELECT lp.partner_name, lp.service_type,
       COUNT(*) AS shipments,
       SUM(sh.shipment_value) AS value_in_transit
FROM shipments sh
JOIN logistics_partners lp ON sh.logistics_partner_id=lp.partner_id
WHERE sh.status='IN_TRANSIT'
GROUP BY lp.partner_name, lp.service_type
ORDER BY value_in_transit DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Transshipped cargo — shipments routed via intermediate hub before final delivery
output:
SELECT sh.tracking_number, sh.carrier,
       r.origin, r.destination,
       lp.partner_name,
       sh.shipment_value, sh.expected_delivery_date
FROM shipments sh
JOIN routes r ON sh.route_id=r.route_id
JOIN logistics_partners lp ON sh.logistics_partner_id=lp.partner_id
WHERE sh.status='IN_TRANSIT'
  AND r.via_hub IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY sh.expected_delivery_date;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: How many shipments are being routed via an intermediate hub right now?
output:
SELECT r.via_hub, COUNT(*) AS shipments,
       SUM(sh.shipment_value) AS total_value
FROM shipments sh
JOIN routes r ON sh.route_id=r.route_id
WHERE sh.status='IN_TRANSIT'
  AND r.via_hub IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY r.via_hub
ORDER BY shipments DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Palletized shipment cost analysis — freight rate per pallet by carrier
output:
SELECT sh.carrier,
       COUNT(*) AS shipments,
       SUM(sh.pallet_count) AS total_pallets,
       SUM(sh.freight_cost) AS total_freight,
       ROUND(SUM(sh.freight_cost)/NULLIF(SUM(sh.pallet_count),0),2) AS cost_per_pallet
FROM shipments sh
WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED'
  AND sh.pallet_count IS NOT NULL
  AND sh.actual_delivery_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY sh.carrier
ORDER BY cost_per_pallet;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: What is the average freight cost per pallet for each carrier this year?
output:
SELECT sh.carrier,
       SUM(sh.pallet_count) AS pallets,
       ROUND(AVG(sh.freight_cost/NULLIF(sh.pallet_count,0)),2) AS avg_cost_per_pallet
FROM shipments sh
WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED'
  AND sh.pallet_count>0
  AND YEAR(sh.actual_delivery_date)=YEAR(GETDATE())
GROUP BY sh.carrier
ORDER BY avg_cost_per_pallet;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Unitised load efficiency — average units per shipment per carrier mode
output:
SELECT lp.service_type, sh.carrier,
       COUNT(*) AS shipments,
       AVG(sh.weight_kg) AS avg_kg,
       ROUND(AVG(sh.shipment_value/NULLIF(sh.weight_kg,0)),2) AS value_density_per_kg
FROM shipments sh
JOIN logistics_partners lp ON sh.logistics_partner_id=lp.partner_id
WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED'
  AND sh.actual_delivery_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY lp.service_type, sh.carrier
ORDER BY value_density_per_kg DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Kitted assemblies — products requiring multi-component preparation before dispatch
output:
SELECT b.parent_sku, p.product_name AS finished_good,
       COUNT(b.child_sku) AS component_count,
       SUM(b.quantity_required*p_comp.unit_cost) AS kit_cost
FROM bom b
JOIN products p ON b.parent_sku=p.sku
JOIN products p_comp ON b.child_sku=p_comp.sku
WHERE p.product_type='FINISHED_GOODS'
GROUP BY b.parent_sku, p.product_name
ORDER BY component_count DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Which finished goods have the most components in their bill of materials?
output:
SELECT b.parent_sku, p.product_name,
       COUNT(b.child_sku) AS component_count,
       SUM(b.quantity_required) AS total_units_needed
FROM bom b
JOIN products p ON b.parent_sku=p.sku
GROUP BY b.parent_sku, p.product_name
ORDER BY component_count DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: How many components does each finished product require in the BOM?
output:
SELECT p.product_name, COUNT(b.child_sku) AS components
FROM bom b
JOIN products p ON b.parent_sku=p.sku
GROUP BY p.product_name
ORDER BY components DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Crossdocked items — shipments processed without entering regular storage
output:
SELECT sh.tracking_number, sh.carrier,
       s.supplier_name, po.po_number,
       sh.shipment_value, sh.arrived_at
FROM shipments sh
JOIN purchase_orders po ON sh.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE sh.shipment_type='CROSS_DOCK'
  AND sh.arrived_at>=DATEADD(MONTH,-1,GETDATE())
ORDER BY sh.arrived_at DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Total value of cross-dock shipments processed last month
output:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS crossdocked_shipments,
       SUM(sh.shipment_value) AS total_value,
       SUM(sh.weight_kg) AS total_kg
FROM shipments sh
WHERE sh.shipment_type='CROSS_DOCK'
  AND sh.arrived_at>=DATEADD(MONTH,-1,GETDATE())
  AND sh.arrived_at< DATEADD(DAY,1-DAY(GETDATE()),CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE));

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Decoupled inventory — stock held at distribution hubs separate from central warehouse
output:
SELECT w.warehouse_type, w.warehouse_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT i.sku) AS sku_count,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS total_units,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost) AS decoupled_value
FROM warehouses w
JOIN inventory i ON w.warehouse_id=i.warehouse_id
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
WHERE w.warehouse_type!='CENTRAL'
  AND i.quantity_on_hand>0
GROUP BY w.warehouse_type, w.warehouse_name
ORDER BY decoupled_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Stock held at regional distribution points away from the main warehouse
output:
SELECT w.warehouse_name, w.location,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS units,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost) AS value
FROM warehouses w
JOIN inventory i ON w.warehouse_id=i.warehouse_id
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
WHERE w.warehouse_type IN ('REGIONAL_HUB','SATELLITE')
  AND i.quantity_on_hand>0
GROUP BY w.warehouse_name, w.location
ORDER BY value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Buffered safety stock — excess inventory held above reorder point as risk buffer
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS on_hand,
       MAX(i.reorder_point) AS reorder_point,
       MAX(i.safety_stock) AS safety_buffer,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand)-MAX(i.reorder_point) AS excess_above_reorder
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
WHERE i.quantity_on_hand > i.reorder_point
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name
ORDER BY excess_above_reorder DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Identify SKUs where stock exceeds the reorder point by more than 50%
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS on_hand,
       MAX(i.reorder_point) AS reorder_point,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(i.quantity_on_hand)/NULLIF(MAX(i.reorder_point),0)-100,1) AS pct_above_reorder
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
WHERE i.reorder_point>0
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name
HAVING ROUND(100.0*SUM(i.quantity_on_hand)/NULLIF(MAX(i.reorder_point),0)-100,1)>50
ORDER BY pct_above_reorder DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Are there products where stock is more than double the reorder point?
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS on_hand,
       MAX(i.reorder_point) AS reorder_point
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
WHERE i.reorder_point>0
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name
HAVING SUM(i.quantity_on_hand)>MAX(i.reorder_point)*2
ORDER BY SUM(i.quantity_on_hand)/NULLIF(MAX(i.reorder_point),0) DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Expedited purchase orders — POs with delivery requested faster than standard lead time
output:
SELECT po.po_number, s.supplier_name,
       po.order_date, po.expected_delivery_date,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,po.order_date,po.expected_delivery_date) AS requested_days,
       p.lead_time_days AS standard_days
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
JOIN products p ON poi.sku=p.sku
WHERE DATEDIFF(DAY,po.order_date,po.expected_delivery_date) < p.lead_time_days*0.70
  AND po.status NOT IN ('CANCELLED','CLOSED')
GROUP BY po.po_number, s.supplier_name, po.order_date, po.expected_delivery_date, p.lead_time_days
ORDER BY requested_days;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Rush POs placed with lead time below 70% of the product standard
output:
SELECT po.po_number, s.supplier_name, po.total_amount,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,po.order_date,po.expected_delivery_date) AS requested_lead_days
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
JOIN products p ON poi.sku=p.sku
WHERE po.status NOT IN ('CANCELLED')
  AND DATEDIFF(DAY,po.order_date,po.expected_delivery_date)<p.lead_time_days*0.70
  AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY po.po_number, s.supplier_name, po.order_date, po.expected_delivery_date, po.total_amount, p.lead_time_days
ORDER BY requested_lead_days;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: How many purchase orders were expedited below standard lead time last year?
output:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT po.po_id) AS expedited_pos,
       SUM(po.total_amount) AS expedited_value
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
JOIN products p ON poi.sku=p.sku
WHERE po.status NOT IN ('CANCELLED')
  AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
  AND DATEDIFF(DAY,po.order_date,po.expected_delivery_date)<p.lead_time_days*0.70;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Deprioritised SKUs — products removed from active sourcing list in the past 6 months
output:
SELECT sp.sku, p.product_name, s.supplier_name,
       sp.status, sp.updated_at
FROM sourcing_products sp
JOIN products p ON sp.sku=p.sku
JOIN suppliers s ON sp.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE sp.status IN ('INACTIVE','SUSPENDED','DELISTED')
  AND sp.updated_at>=DATEADD(MONTH,-6,GETDATE())
ORDER BY sp.updated_at DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Sourcing products deactivated or suspended in the last 6 months
output:
SELECT sp.sku, p.product_name, s.supplier_name, sp.status, sp.updated_at
FROM sourcing_products sp
JOIN products p ON sp.sku=p.sku
JOIN suppliers s ON sp.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE sp.status NOT IN ('APPROVED','PENDING')
  AND sp.updated_at>=DATEADD(MONTH,-6,GETDATE())
ORDER BY sp.updated_at DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Were any sourcing products delisted from the approved vendor list this quarter?
output:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS delisted_count,
       COUNT(DISTINCT sp.sku) AS affected_skus
FROM sourcing_products sp
WHERE sp.status IN ('INACTIVE','DELISTED')
  AND sp.updated_at>=DATEADD(QUARTER,DATEDIFF(QUARTER,0,GETDATE()),0);

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Authorised suppliers — active vendors with at least one approved sourcing product
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.category,
       COUNT(DISTINCT sp.sku) AS approved_skus
FROM suppliers s
JOIN sourcing_products sp ON s.supplier_id=sp.supplier_id
WHERE s.status='ACTIVE' AND sp.status='APPROVED'
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, s.category
ORDER BY approved_skus DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Active vendors with at least one product on the approved sourcing list
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.category, s.country,
       COUNT(DISTINCT sp.sku) AS sourced_skus
FROM suppliers s
JOIN sourcing_products sp ON s.supplier_id=sp.supplier_id
WHERE s.status='ACTIVE' AND sp.status='APPROVED'
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, s.category, s.country
ORDER BY sourced_skus DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: How many approved vendors do we have per supplier category?
output:
SELECT s.category,
       COUNT(DISTINCT s.supplier_id) AS approved_vendors
FROM suppliers s
JOIN sourcing_products sp ON s.supplier_id=sp.supplier_id
WHERE s.status='ACTIVE' AND sp.status='APPROVED'
GROUP BY s.category
ORDER BY approved_vendors DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Sanctioned vendor check — suppliers flagged as blacklisted in the past year
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.category, s.country,
       s.status, s.updated_at AS status_change_date
FROM suppliers s
WHERE s.status='BLACKLISTED'
  AND s.updated_at>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
ORDER BY s.updated_at DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Which vendors have been blacklisted in the last 12 months?
output:
SELECT supplier_name, category, country, updated_at
FROM suppliers
WHERE status='BLACKLISTED'
  AND updated_at>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
ORDER BY updated_at DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Are there any purchases from vendors currently on the blacklist?
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, po.po_number, po.order_date, po.total_amount
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE s.status='BLACKLISTED'
  AND po.status NOT IN ('CANCELLED')
  AND po.order_date>=s.updated_at
ORDER BY po.order_date DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Quarantined inventory — items held for quality investigation not available for fulfilment
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, w.warehouse_name,
       i.quantity_on_hand AS quarantine_qty,
       i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost AS quarantine_value
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
JOIN warehouses w ON i.warehouse_id=w.warehouse_id
WHERE i.status='QUARANTINE'
  AND i.quantity_on_hand>0
ORDER BY quarantine_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Stock currently held under quarantine across all warehouse locations
output:
SELECT w.warehouse_name, p.category,
       COUNT(DISTINCT i.sku) AS skus_quarantined,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS qty_quarantined,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost) AS value_quarantined
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
JOIN warehouses w ON i.warehouse_id=w.warehouse_id
WHERE i.status='QUARANTINE'
GROUP BY w.warehouse_name, p.category
ORDER BY value_quarantined DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Is there any quarantined inventory that has been held for more than 30 days?
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, w.warehouse_name,
       i.quantity_on_hand,
       i.quarantine_date,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,i.quarantine_date,GETDATE()) AS days_quarantined
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
JOIN warehouses w ON i.warehouse_id=w.warehouse_id
WHERE i.status='QUARANTINE'
  AND DATEDIFF(DAY,i.quarantine_date,GETDATE())>30
ORDER BY days_quarantined DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Inspected batches with pass rate below the 95% threshold this year
output:
SELECT gr.grn_number, s.supplier_name, p.sku, p.product_name,
       gri.received_quantity, qc.defective_quantity,
       ROUND(100.0*(gri.received_quantity-qc.defective_quantity)/NULLIF(gri.received_quantity,0),1) AS pass_rate_pct
FROM quality_checks qc
JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON qc.grn_item_id=gri.grn_item_id
JOIN goods_receipts gr ON gri.grn_id=gr.grn_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON gri.po_item_id=poi.po_item_id
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
JOIN products p ON gri.sku=p.sku
WHERE YEAR(qc.inspection_date)=YEAR(GETDATE())
  AND 100.0*(gri.received_quantity-qc.defective_quantity)/NULLIF(gri.received_quantity,0)<95
ORDER BY pass_rate_pct;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Quality batches failing the 95% pass rate threshold — supplier and product breakdown
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, p.category,
       COUNT(*) AS batches_below_threshold,
       SUM(gri.received_quantity) AS total_received,
       SUM(qc.defective_quantity) AS total_defective
FROM quality_checks qc
JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON qc.grn_item_id=gri.grn_item_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON gri.po_item_id=poi.po_item_id
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
JOIN products p ON gri.sku=p.sku
WHERE 100.0*(gri.received_quantity-qc.defective_quantity)/NULLIF(gri.received_quantity,0)<95
  AND qc.inspection_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, p.category
ORDER BY batches_below_threshold DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Certified suppliers — vendors holding active quality certification on record
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.category,
       c.contract_number AS cert_ref,
       c.end_date AS cert_expiry
FROM suppliers s
JOIN contracts c ON s.supplier_id=c.supplier_id
WHERE c.status='ACTIVE'
  AND c.contract_type='CERTIFICATION'
ORDER BY c.end_date;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Accredited vendors — list all suppliers holding ISO or equivalent quality accreditation
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.category,
       c.contract_number AS accreditation_ref,
       c.end_date AS accreditation_expiry,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,GETDATE(),c.end_date) AS days_valid
FROM suppliers s
JOIN contracts c ON s.supplier_id=c.supplier_id
WHERE c.status='ACTIVE'
  AND c.contract_type IN ('CERTIFICATION','ACCREDITATION')
ORDER BY days_valid;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Which vendors have quality accreditation expiring in the next 90 days?
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name, s.category,
       c.contract_number,
       c.end_date AS accreditation_expiry,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,GETDATE(),c.end_date) AS days_remaining
FROM suppliers s
JOIN contracts c ON s.supplier_id=c.supplier_id
WHERE c.status='ACTIVE'
  AND c.contract_type IN ('CERTIFICATION','ACCREDITATION')
  AND c.end_date BETWEEN GETDATE() AND DATEADD(DAY,90,GETDATE())
ORDER BY days_remaining;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Validated demand forecasts — periods where forecast was within 15% of actual demand
output:
SELECT df.sku, p.product_name,
       COUNT(*) AS total_periods,
       SUM(CASE WHEN ABS(df.actual_quantity-df.forecasted_quantity)/NULLIF(df.actual_quantity,0)<=0.15 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS accurate_periods,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN ABS(df.actual_quantity-df.forecasted_quantity)/NULLIF(df.actual_quantity,0)<=0.15 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0),1) AS accuracy_pct
FROM demand_forecasts df
JOIN products p ON df.sku=p.sku
WHERE df.actual_quantity IS NOT NULL AND df.actual_quantity>0
GROUP BY df.sku, p.product_name
HAVING COUNT(*)>=3
ORDER BY accuracy_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Which SKUs have forecast accuracy above 85% for all periods in the last year?
output:
SELECT df.sku, p.product_name,
       COUNT(*) AS periods,
       ROUND(AVG(100.0*(1-ABS(df.actual_quantity-df.forecasted_quantity)/NULLIF(df.actual_quantity,0))),1) AS avg_accuracy_pct
FROM demand_forecasts df
JOIN products p ON df.sku=p.sku
WHERE df.actual_quantity IS NOT NULL AND df.actual_quantity>0
  AND df.forecast_period>=FORMAT(DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE()),'yyyy-MM')
GROUP BY df.sku, p.product_name
HAVING COUNT(*)>=3
  AND AVG(100.0*(1-ABS(df.actual_quantity-df.forecasted_quantity)/NULLIF(df.actual_quantity,0)))>=85
ORDER BY avg_accuracy_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Ratified purchase orders — POs approved by authorised signatories this month
output:
SELECT po.po_number, s.supplier_name, po.order_date,
       po.approved_by, po.total_amount, po.status
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE po.status IN ('APPROVED','RECEIVED','CLOSED')
  AND MONTH(po.order_date)=MONTH(GETDATE())
  AND YEAR(po.order_date)=YEAR(GETDATE())
ORDER BY po.order_date DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Purchase orders approved this month with the name of the authorising person
output:
SELECT po.po_number, s.supplier_name,
       po.total_amount, po.order_date, po.approved_by
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE po.status NOT IN ('PENDING_APPROVAL','CANCELLED')
  AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(DAY,1-DAY(GETDATE()),CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE))
ORDER BY po.order_date DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Harmonised category spend — total procurement spend across all subcategories for each main category
output:
SELECT s.category,
       COUNT(DISTINCT po.po_id) AS po_count,
       COUNT(DISTINCT po.supplier_id) AS vendors,
       SUM(poi.line_total) AS harmonised_spend
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED'
  AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.category
ORDER BY harmonised_spend DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Total procurement spend consolidated by supplier category for the past year
output:
SELECT s.category,
       SUM(poi.line_total) AS total_spend,
       COUNT(DISTINCT po.supplier_id) AS vendors
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED'
  AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.category
ORDER BY total_spend DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: How is our total procurement spend distributed across supplier categories?
output:
SELECT s.category,
       SUM(poi.line_total) AS spend,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(poi.line_total)/SUM(SUM(poi.line_total)) OVER (),1) AS pct_of_total
FROM purchase_order_items poi
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED'
  AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.category
ORDER BY spend DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Aggregated supplier spend — rolling 12-month total grouped by vendor and month
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       FORMAT(po.order_date,'yyyy-MM') AS month,
       SUM(poi.line_total) AS monthly_spend,
       SUM(SUM(poi.line_total)) OVER (PARTITION BY s.supplier_name
           ORDER BY FORMAT(po.order_date,'yyyy-MM')
           ROWS BETWEEN 11 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS rolling_12m_spend
FROM purchase_orders po
JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
JOIN suppliers s ON po.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED'
  AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-2,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name, FORMAT(po.order_date,'yyyy-MM')
ORDER BY s.supplier_name, month;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Rolling 12-month vendor spend trend using window function
output:
WITH monthly AS (
    SELECT po.supplier_id,
           FORMAT(po.order_date,'yyyy-MM') AS month,
           SUM(poi.line_total) AS spend
    FROM purchase_orders po
    JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
    WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED' AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-2,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY po.supplier_id, FORMAT(po.order_date,'yyyy-MM')
)
SELECT s.supplier_name, m.month, m.spend,
       SUM(m.spend) OVER (PARTITION BY m.supplier_id ORDER BY m.month
                         ROWS BETWEEN 11 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS rolling_12m
FROM monthly m
JOIN suppliers s ON m.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
ORDER BY s.supplier_name, m.month;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Distributed warehouse stock — how is total inventory split across locations?
output:
SELECT w.warehouse_name, w.warehouse_type,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS total_units,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost) AS stock_value,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost)/SUM(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost)) OVER (),1) AS pct_of_total
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
JOIN warehouses w ON i.warehouse_id=w.warehouse_id
GROUP BY w.warehouse_name, w.warehouse_type
ORDER BY stock_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: What percentage of total inventory value is held at each warehouse?
output:
SELECT w.warehouse_name,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost) AS value,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost)/SUM(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost)) OVER (),1) AS pct
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
JOIN warehouses w ON i.warehouse_id=w.warehouse_id
GROUP BY w.warehouse_name
ORDER BY pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Concentrated stock — SKUs where over 80% of total inventory sits in one warehouse
output:
WITH sku_total AS (
    SELECT sku, SUM(quantity_on_hand) AS total FROM inventory GROUP BY sku
)
SELECT i.sku, p.product_name, w.warehouse_name,
       i.quantity_on_hand AS at_this_warehouse,
       st.total AS total_stock,
       ROUND(100.0*i.quantity_on_hand/NULLIF(st.total,0),1) AS concentration_pct
FROM inventory i
JOIN sku_total st ON i.sku=st.sku
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
JOIN warehouses w ON i.warehouse_id=w.warehouse_id
WHERE 100.0*i.quantity_on_hand/NULLIF(st.total,0)>80
  AND st.total>0
ORDER BY concentration_pct DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Normalised supplier scorecard — performance scores rescaled 0 to 100 across all vendors
output:
WITH raw AS (
    SELECT supplier_id,
           AVG(quality_score) AS q,
           AVG(delivery_score) AS d,
           AVG(cost_score) AS c
    FROM supplier_performance
    WHERE evaluation_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY supplier_id
)
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       ROUND(r.q,1) AS quality,
       ROUND(r.d,1) AS delivery,
       ROUND(r.c,1) AS cost,
       ROUND(r.q*0.4+r.d*0.4+r.c*0.2,1) AS composite_score,
       RANK() OVER (ORDER BY r.q*0.4+r.d*0.4+r.c*0.2 DESC) AS overall_rank
FROM raw r
JOIN suppliers s ON r.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
ORDER BY composite_score DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Ranked vendor scorecard combining quality delivery and cost metrics
output:
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       ROUND(AVG(sp.quality_score),1) AS quality,
       ROUND(AVG(sp.delivery_score),1) AS delivery,
       ROUND(AVG(sp.cost_score),1) AS cost,
       ROUND(AVG(sp.quality_score)*0.4+AVG(sp.delivery_score)*0.4+AVG(sp.cost_score)*0.2,1) AS composite,
       RANK() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(sp.quality_score)*0.4+AVG(sp.delivery_score)*0.4+AVG(sp.cost_score)*0.2 DESC) AS rnk
FROM supplier_performance sp
JOIN suppliers s ON sp.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE sp.evaluation_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_id, s.supplier_name
ORDER BY composite DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Which vendor has the highest composite performance score in our latest evaluations?
output:
SELECT TOP 1 s.supplier_name,
       ROUND(AVG(sp.quality_score)*0.4+AVG(sp.delivery_score)*0.4+AVG(sp.cost_score)*0.2,1) AS composite
FROM supplier_performance sp
JOIN suppliers s ON sp.supplier_id=s.supplier_id
WHERE sp.evaluation_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY s.supplier_name
ORDER BY composite DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Categorised invoice status — count and value by payment status across all vendors
output:
SELECT inv.status,
       COUNT(*) AS invoice_count,
       SUM(inv.total_amount) AS total_invoiced,
       SUM(COALESCE(inv.paid_amount,0)) AS total_paid,
       SUM(inv.total_amount-COALESCE(inv.paid_amount,0)) AS outstanding
FROM invoices inv
GROUP BY inv.status
ORDER BY outstanding DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Invoice payment status breakdown — how many are paid unpaid or partially paid?
output:
SELECT status,
       COUNT(*) AS count,
       SUM(total_amount) AS amount
FROM invoices
GROUP BY status
ORDER BY amount DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: What is the total value of invoices by payment status this financial year?
output:
SELECT status,
       COUNT(*) AS invoices,
       SUM(total_amount) AS total_value,
       SUM(COALESCE(paid_amount,0)) AS paid,
       SUM(total_amount-COALESCE(paid_amount,0)) AS outstanding
FROM invoices
WHERE YEAR(invoice_date)=YEAR(GETDATE())
GROUP BY status;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Prioritised reorder list — SKUs to order ranked by days of cover remaining
output:
WITH daily AS (
    SELECT soi.sku, SUM(soi.ordered_quantity)/90.0 AS rate
    FROM sales_order_items soi
    JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id=so.so_id
    WHERE so.order_date>=DATEADD(DAY,-90,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY soi.sku
)
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, p.lead_time_days,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS stock,
       ROUND(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand)/NULLIF(d.rate,0),1) AS days_cover,
       CASE WHEN ROUND(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand)/NULLIF(d.rate,0),1)<p.lead_time_days THEN 'ORDER NOW'
            WHEN ROUND(SUM(i.quantity_on_hand)/NULLIF(d.rate,0),1)<p.lead_time_days*1.5 THEN 'PLAN ORDER'
            ELSE 'MONITOR' END AS action
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON p.sku=i.sku
JOIN daily d ON p.sku=d.sku
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, p.lead_time_days, d.rate
ORDER BY days_cover;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Which products need to be ordered urgently based on days of cover versus lead time?
output:
WITH cov AS (
    SELECT i.sku,
           SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS stock,
           SUM(i.quantity_on_hand)/NULLIF(AVG(soi.ordered_quantity)/90.0,0) AS days_cover
    FROM inventory i
    LEFT JOIN sales_order_items soi ON i.sku=soi.sku
    LEFT JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id=so.so_id AND so.order_date>=DATEADD(DAY,-90,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY i.sku
)
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, c.stock,
       ROUND(c.days_cover,1) AS days_cover,
       p.lead_time_days
FROM cov c
JOIN products p ON c.sku=p.sku
WHERE ROUND(c.days_cover,1)<p.lead_time_days
ORDER BY days_cover;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Ranked procurement savings opportunities — SKUs with biggest gap between current price and lowest historical price
output:
SELECT poi.sku, p.product_name,
       MIN(poi.unit_price) AS lowest_ever,
       MAX(poi.unit_price) AS highest_ever,
       AVG(poi.unit_price) AS avg_price,
       AVG(poi.unit_price)-MIN(poi.unit_price) AS potential_saving_per_unit,
       SUM(poi.ordered_quantity) AS annual_volume,
       (AVG(poi.unit_price)-MIN(poi.unit_price))*SUM(poi.ordered_quantity) AS total_saving_opportunity
FROM purchase_order_items poi
JOIN products p ON poi.sku=p.sku
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED' AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY poi.sku, p.product_name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT poi.unit_price)>1
ORDER BY total_saving_opportunity DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Where is the biggest price variance opportunity in our procurement spend?
output:
SELECT poi.sku, p.product_name,
       MIN(poi.unit_price) AS min_paid,
       MAX(poi.unit_price) AS max_paid,
       MAX(poi.unit_price)-MIN(poi.unit_price) AS price_range
FROM purchase_order_items poi
JOIN products p ON poi.sku=p.sku
JOIN purchase_orders po ON poi.po_id=po.po_id
WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED' AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY poi.sku, p.product_name
HAVING MAX(poi.unit_price)>MIN(poi.unit_price)
ORDER BY price_range DESC
OFFSET 0 ROWS FETCH NEXT 10 ROWS ONLY;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Triangulated risk score — combining spend defect rate and stockout frequency per vendor
output:
WITH spend_r AS (
    SELECT po.supplier_id, SUM(poi.line_total) AS spend
    FROM purchase_orders po JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
    WHERE po.status!='CANCELLED' AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY po.supplier_id
),
defect_r AS (
    SELECT po.supplier_id,
           ROUND(100.0*SUM(qc.defective_quantity)/NULLIF(SUM(gri.received_quantity),0),2) AS defect_pct
    FROM purchase_orders po
    JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
    JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON poi.po_item_id=gri.po_item_id
    JOIN quality_checks qc ON gri.grn_item_id=qc.grn_item_id
    WHERE po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY po.supplier_id
),
stockout_r AS (
    SELECT sp.supplier_id, COUNT(se.stockout_id) AS stockouts
    FROM sourcing_products sp
    LEFT JOIN stockout_events se ON sp.sku=se.sku AND se.start_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY sp.supplier_id
)
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       sp.spend,
       COALESCE(dr.defect_pct,0) AS defect_pct,
       COALESCE(sr.stockouts,0) AS stockout_count,
       ROUND(COALESCE(dr.defect_pct,0)*0.4+COALESCE(sr.stockouts,0)*10+sp.spend/1000000*0.2,2) AS risk_score
FROM suppliers s
JOIN spend_r sp ON s.supplier_id=sp.supplier_id
LEFT JOIN defect_r dr ON s.supplier_id=dr.supplier_id
LEFT JOIN stockout_r sr ON s.supplier_id=sr.supplier_id
ORDER BY risk_score DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Weighted risk exposure combining vendor defect rate delivery failures and spend concentration
output:
WITH d AS (
    SELECT po.supplier_id,
           ROUND(100.0*SUM(qc.defective_quantity)/NULLIF(SUM(gri.received_quantity),0),2) AS defect
    FROM purchase_orders po
    JOIN purchase_order_items poi ON po.po_id=poi.po_id
    JOIN goods_receipt_items gri ON poi.po_item_id=gri.po_item_id
    JOIN quality_checks qc ON gri.grn_item_id=qc.grn_item_id
    WHERE po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY po.supplier_id
),
late AS (
    SELECT po.supplier_id,
           ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN sh.actual_delivery_date>sh.expected_delivery_date THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0),1) AS late_pct
    FROM purchase_orders po JOIN shipments sh ON po.po_id=sh.po_id
    WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED' AND po.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
    GROUP BY po.supplier_id
)
SELECT s.supplier_name,
       COALESCE(d.defect,0) AS defect_pct,
       COALESCE(l.late_pct,0) AS late_pct,
       ROUND(COALESCE(d.defect,0)*0.5+COALESCE(l.late_pct,0)*0.5,2) AS combined_risk
FROM suppliers s
LEFT JOIN d ON s.supplier_id=d.supplier_id
LEFT JOIN late l ON s.supplier_id=l.supplier_id
WHERE s.status='ACTIVE'
ORDER BY combined_risk DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Haulage cost per kilogram delivered — which carrier offers the best rate?
output:
SELECT sh.carrier,
       COUNT(*) AS deliveries,
       SUM(sh.weight_kg) AS total_kg,
       SUM(sh.freight_cost) AS total_freight,
       ROUND(SUM(sh.freight_cost)/NULLIF(SUM(sh.weight_kg),0),2) AS cost_per_kg
FROM shipments sh
WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED'
  AND sh.actual_delivery_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY sh.carrier
ORDER BY cost_per_kg;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Which freight carrier delivers at the lowest cost per kg?
output:
SELECT sh.carrier,
       ROUND(SUM(sh.freight_cost)/NULLIF(SUM(sh.weight_kg),0),2) AS cost_per_kg,
       COUNT(*) AS shipments
FROM shipments sh
WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED'
  AND sh.actual_delivery_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY sh.carrier
ORDER BY cost_per_kg
OFFSET 0 ROWS FETCH NEXT 1 ROWS ONLY;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Carrier freight rate comparison — cost efficiency ranked by price per kilogram
output:
SELECT sh.carrier,
       SUM(sh.freight_cost) AS total_spend,
       SUM(sh.weight_kg) AS kg_delivered,
       ROUND(SUM(sh.freight_cost)/NULLIF(SUM(sh.weight_kg),0),2) AS rate_per_kg,
       RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(sh.freight_cost)/NULLIF(SUM(sh.weight_kg),0) ASC) AS efficiency_rank
FROM shipments sh
WHERE sh.status='DELIVERED' AND sh.actual_delivery_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
GROUP BY sh.carrier
ORDER BY efficiency_rank;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Bonded warehouse stock — inventory held under customs control awaiting clearance
output:
SELECT w.warehouse_name, p.sku, p.product_name,
       i.quantity_on_hand, i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost AS held_value
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
JOIN warehouses w ON i.warehouse_id=w.warehouse_id
WHERE w.warehouse_type='BONDED'
  AND i.quantity_on_hand>0
ORDER BY held_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: What stock is currently held in bonded storage pending customs release?
output:
SELECT w.warehouse_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT i.sku) AS sku_count,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS units,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost) AS bonded_value
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
JOIN warehouses w ON i.warehouse_id=w.warehouse_id
WHERE w.warehouse_type='BONDED'
GROUP BY w.warehouse_name
ORDER BY bonded_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Is there any inventory sitting in bonded warehouses that has been held for more than 30 days?
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, w.warehouse_name,
       i.quantity_on_hand,
       i.bonded_since,
       DATEDIFF(DAY,i.bonded_since,GETDATE()) AS days_held
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
JOIN warehouses w ON i.warehouse_id=w.warehouse_id
WHERE w.warehouse_type='BONDED'
  AND DATEDIFF(DAY,i.bonded_since,GETDATE())>30
  AND i.quantity_on_hand>0
ORDER BY days_held DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Write-off candidates — products with no outbound movement for over 12 months
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, p.category,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS qty,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost) AS writeoff_exposure
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
WHERE i.quantity_on_hand>0
  AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM inventory_transactions it
      WHERE it.sku=p.sku AND it.transaction_type='OUTBOUND'
        AND it.transaction_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
  )
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, p.category
ORDER BY writeoff_exposure DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Which products have had zero sales in the past year and are now at risk of write-off?
output:
SELECT p.sku, p.product_name, p.category,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand) AS dead_stock_qty,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost) AS book_value
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON p.sku=i.sku
WHERE i.quantity_on_hand>0
  AND p.sku NOT IN (
      SELECT DISTINCT soi.sku FROM sales_order_items soi
      JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.so_id=so.so_id
      WHERE so.order_date>=DATEADD(YEAR,-1,GETDATE())
  )
GROUP BY p.sku, p.product_name, p.category
ORDER BY book_value DESC;

instruction: Convert the following natural language question to a SQL query for a Supply Chain Management database.
input: Dead stock exposure — items unsold for 12 months requiring write-off provision
output:
SELECT p.category,
       COUNT(DISTINCT p.sku) AS skus_at_risk,
       SUM(i.quantity_on_hand*p.unit_cost) AS provision_required
FROM inventory i
JOIN products p ON i.sku=p.sku
